
# Changelog

## DX-WWW TypeScript Source Contract

- Tightened the agent-facing source policy so framework logic, Node scripts,
  and tests use `.ts`, UI source uses `.tsx`, and new hand-authored `.js`,
  `.mjs`, or `.cjs` files are reserved for documented generated/runtime
  artifacts or compatibility shims.

## DX-WWW n8n Studio Foundation

- Added governed host-executed editor-session request batches. Trusted DX/Zed
  host code can now collect pending dynamic option, resource locator, mapper
  schema, and credential metadata request plans, execute a bounded batch
  through host-owned adapters, and apply returned records through the existing
  redacted no-secret response pipeline.
- Updated automation scope so dynamic options/resource locators report
  host-executed request batch support while remaining partial until
  provider-specific executors are registered.
- Added provider credential validation execution to the credential vault
  bridge. The vault can now resolve a credential by id, load its secret only
  inside the adapter boundary, call an injected provider validator, and return
  a redacted `dx.n8n-studio.credential-validation.receipt`; automation scope
  now reports the credential bridge as partial instead of metadata-only.
- Added semantic generated-only node summary rendering across the local
  generated n8n connector catalog. Generic generated node schemas now include
  a non-expression `generatedNodeSummary` notice with connector description,
  source file, runtime mode, auth kind, provenance, and governed adapter
  boundaries, and the inspector renders notice fields plus parameter
  descriptions as read-only context.
- Added host-injected scheduler route execution for runtime execution-proof
  retries. The public retry route now delegates to a reusable factory that
  preserves the default redacted credential-handoff-required response, while
  trusted DX/Zed host code can register a runtime-context resolver and receive
  real bounded read-only retry results without passing credential values
  through the public request payload.
- Added an injectable runtime execution-proof scheduler handoff. Host-owned
  scheduler code can now pass a validated retry request, credential vault, n8n
  API transport, workflow document, prior trigger receipt, and receipt root
  into the existing bounded retry executor, while the public route remains
  free of credential values, secret refs, and transport ownership.
- Added id-only scheduler POST validation for runtime execution-proof retry
  follow-through. The retry discovery route now accepts the advertised POST
  envelope, stores no request body, rejects secret-bearing scheduler payloads
  without echoing field names or values, and returns an explicit
  credential-handoff-required response until DX/Zed supplies the injected n8n
  API transport.
- Added durable runtime execution-proof receipt persistence and delayed
  history retry planning. The new server helper writes the known redacted
  runtime proof receipt under an injected receipt root, returns a Zed/DX
  discovery export, produces a deterministic read-only retry plan for delayed
  n8n execution-history availability, and can run bounded injected retry
  attempts until imported proof is available without storing webhook URLs,
  request bodies, API keys, secret refs, provider bodies, or node output
  payloads.
- Updated the n8n Studio automation scope and Zed automation packet so DX/Zed
  consumers can discover the pending runtime execution-proof receipt path and
  the retry/import follow-through needed before live automation is enabled.
- Added a governed runtime execution-proof retry scheduler action descriptor
  for Zed/DX discovery. The Zed automation packet now includes a disabled
  credential-handoff-required action, and
  `/api/n8n-studio/runtime-execution-proof-retry` serves the same redacted
  contract without serializing credential values, webhook URLs, provider
  payloads, or secret-like field names.
- Added webhook runtime execution proof follow-through. Credential-vault
  runtime trigger submission can now load both the secret webhook trigger URL
  and n8n API key inside adapter boundaries, submit the live trigger request,
  import execution history through the governed n8n API client, and return a
  redacted execution-proof receipt that stores no trigger URL, request secret,
  API key, or provider response body.
- Added governed webhook runtime trigger request contracts. The new runtime
  trigger bridge reads local n8n Webhook source semantics, blocks
  manual/internal trigger execution behind provider controls, lets the
  credential vault load webhook trigger URLs inside the adapter boundary, and
  returns only redacted origin/status receipt metadata while requiring
  imported execution receipts for proof.
- Added credential-vault runtime handoff submission. Callers can now submit
  governed n8n runtime handoff by credential id while the vault owns secret
  loading and returns only redacted receipt metadata, keeping API keys and
  secret refs out of Studio-visible state.
- Added a governed n8n runtime handoff receipt on the injected n8n API
  client. The handoff composes sanitized workflow publish/update, optional
  activation, and optional execution-history import, tracks the provider
  workflow id before history lookup, and explicitly reports that Studio did
  not call a fake live execution endpoint.
- Updated n8n Studio automation scope and Zed automation packet language so
  downstream DX/Zed surfaces can discover runtime handoff availability while
  keeping live execution disabled until provider trigger controls and
  execution receipts are configured.
- Added governed editor-session parameter overlays. Configured dynamic option
  responses now update visible selected-node parameter options through a small
  overlay helper, and resource-locator draft state can retain resolved
  options, query metadata, and pagination cursors without enabling live
  provider execution.
- Updated parameter controls to render configured `options` and
  `multiOptions` as select controls and to surface resolved resource-locator
  options from governed responses.
- Wired current workflow export into the real n8n Studio UI state. The
  Import / export panel now has an export action that POSTs the active
  `WorkflowDocument` to `/api/n8n-studio/export`, tracks idle/exporting/
  exported/failed status, stores redacted receipt metadata, and avoids keeping
  exported workflow JSON payloads in client state.
- Added a Zed-readable receipt exporter for the n8n Studio automation bridge.
  The writer materializes the `dx.agents.zed.automation_list.v1` packet and
  `dx.n8n-studio.readiness` snapshot under a configurable receipt root so Zed
  and DX automation surfaces can consume the same file contract they already
  read, without exposing secrets or claiming live execution.
- Updated n8n Studio automation scope from planned to partial runtime support:
  governed workflow publish handoff exists, while execution receipt import
  remains the blocker before live runs can be enabled.
- Added governed n8n workflow publish handoff support to the injected n8n API
  client. The bridge can submit sanitized current workflow JSON through
  `POST /workflows` for creates, `PUT /workflows/{id}` for explicit updates,
  and optional `POST /workflows/{id}/activate`, returning a redacted
  publish receipt with `liveProviderExecution=false` and no secret-bearing
  response state.
- Added source-owned current workflow JSON export. The export route now
  accepts POSTed DX `WorkflowDocument` payloads, sanitizes secret-like
  parameter and credential fields, and returns n8n-shaped workflow JSON plus
  export receipt metadata for the edited workflow instead of only the seeded
  boot workflow.
- Added a source-only Zed automation-list bridge packet for n8n Studio.
  `/api/n8n-studio/zed-automation` now projects the editor-session readiness
  into a `dx.agents.zed.automation_list.v1` row with runtime unavailable,
  redaction metadata, redacted receipt references, and no secret-bearing
  actions.
- Added an explicit automation scope contract to readiness so DX Agents, Zed
  plugin surfaces, and the DX automation screen can target n8n runtime-backed
  automation instead of n8n Vue editor parity. The scope reports capability
  states for metadata catalog, workflow JSON authoring, node connection
  authoring, runtime execution, credential bridge, and dynamic
  options/resource locators.
- Added a governed editor-session request-batch endpoint for DX Agents and
  Zed plugin panel consumers. `GET /api/n8n-studio/editor-session` now returns
  pending blocked request plans with kind counts, selected-node metadata,
  provider-boundary flags, and redaction metadata while keeping secret values
  out of the response.
- Added governed editor-session credential validation response handling.
  Selected credentials now produce separate `credential-test` plans, accepted
  validation responses store valid/invalid/unknown status metadata without
  live provider execution, and unsafe or secret-bearing validation payloads are
  rejected by the runtime response decoder before reaching Studio state.
- Added governed editor-session response batch plumbing across Studio state and
  API surfaces. The n8n Studio store now exposes an editor-session action, and
  `/api/n8n-studio/editor-session` accepts response batches through a runtime
  decoder that rejects unsafe/live/secret-bearing records before returning the
  updated editor-session readiness projection.
- Added governed editor-session credential-list response application. Matching
  credential request plans can now store sanitized id/name/type/source/redaction
  picker options, update selected credential id/name metadata, and drop
  unexpected secret fields before entering Studio state.
- Added governed editor-session resource mapper schema response application.
  Matching mapper request plans can now store sanitized schema fields, field
  words, mode metadata, and resolved field counts from redacted
  no-live-provider responses, dropping unexpected provider fields before they
  enter Studio state.
- Added governed editor-session resource-locator search response application.
  Matching locator plans can now store sanitized result options, resolved
  query text, and pagination cursor metadata from redacted no-live-provider
  transport responses while keeping unresolved request plans blocked.
- Added governed editor-session dynamic option response application. The
  editor-session adapter can now apply redacted, no-live-provider transport
  responses to matching dynamic option request plans, stores only sanitized
  option `name`/`value`/description/action fields, tracks fulfilled request
  count, and exposes that count through the readiness response while remaining
  requests stay blocked.
- Added source-only trigger configuration boundaries for generated trigger
  nodes. Trigger records with sparse generated metadata now render a
  non-expression `triggerConfiguration` notice alongside credentials, making
  the inspector honest about source-backed trigger/event adapter work still
  being required before live webhook execution can run.
- Improved generic generated node metadata rendering by preferring generated
  executable `actions` for operation selectors and catalog operation summaries
  when present. This keeps nodes such as Chargebee from exposing operator-like
  filter values as top-level workflow actions while retaining real executable
  actions in generated Studio surfaces.
- Added source-backed Discord v2 send-and-wait custom-form field extraction
  from local n8n Form node helper sources. Generated Studio boot now preserves
  `defineForm`, JSON form definitions, and field-based `formFields`
  fixed-collection children for custom-form responses, including dropdown
  option entries and child display rules, without enabling live Discord
  execution.
- Added source-backed Discord v2 send-and-wait parameter extraction from local
  n8n helper sources, including `sendAndWait` operation normalization,
  send-to resource locators, message text, response type options, approval
  option labels, wait-time limit controls, attribution toggles, and
  free-text/custom-form response options in generated Studio boot without
  enabling live Discord execution.
- Expanded source-backed Discord v2 message operation extraction beyond send
  into delete, get, get many, and react schemas from local
  `nodes/Discord/v2/actions/message` source. Generated Studio boot now
  preserves message channel resource locators, message IDs, simplify options,
  return-all/limit controls, emoji inputs, and channel-search dynamic
  boundaries without enabling live Discord execution.
- Expanded source-backed Discord v2 parameter extraction beyond message send
  into channel and member operations from local `nodes/Discord/v2/actions`
  source. Generated Studio boot now preserves channel create/delete/get/get
  many/update controls, member get-many/role add/role remove controls,
  parent-category/user/server resource locators, role dynamic options, and
  shared `returnAllOrLimit` utility fields without enabling live Discord
  execution.
- Added source-backed Discord v2 message-send parameter extraction from local
  `nodes/Discord/v2/actions` source, including connection type credentials,
  message operation selection, server/channel/user resource locators, content,
  options, embed/file fixed collections, and list-search dynamic boundaries in
  generated Studio boot without claiming live Discord execution.
- Added source-backed expansion for Notion v2 database-page search filters
  from local `GenericFunctions.ts` and `Filters.ts`, preserving `filterType`,
  `matchType`, `filters.conditions`, typed condition options, manual filter
  value fields, and `getFilterProperties` dynamic option boundaries in both
  isolated source extraction and generated Studio boot.
- Added source-backed expansion for Notion v2 `blocks(resource, operation)`
  fragments from local `Blocks.ts`, preserving `blockUi` fixed-collection
  fields, rich-text/text child controls, and `getBlockTypes` dynamic option
  boundaries in both isolated source extraction and generated Studio boot.
- Added source-backed Notion v2 parameter extraction and generated boot
  overlay coverage for local `VersionDescription.ts` plus shared
  block/database/database-page/page/user description arrays, including Notion
  credentials, resource locators, property collections, and dynamic option
  boundaries.
- Added recursive local/shared object-spread expansion to the shared n8n
  source-description reader and refactored Google Sheets to use it for
  `readFilter`, `combineFiltersOptions`, and nested shared option fragments.
- Refactored Gmail operation-field extraction to use the shared
  source-description reader for array entry parsing, completing Gmail's move
  off adapter-local base parsing helpers.
- Added same-source array spread expansion to the shared n8n
  source-description reader and refactored OpenAI source-backed parameter
  extraction to use it for spread-backed chat/image/text field arrays.
- Refactored Gmail source-backed base metadata to share the common
  `versionDescription` parameter and credential reader while preserving its
  adapter-local operation-field array expansion.
- Extended the shared n8n source-description reader so adapters can include
  leading const object parameters before `versionDescription.properties`, then
  refactored Google Sheets to share version-property and credential extraction
  while keeping its specialized nested spread expansion local.
- Added a shared n8n source-description reader for source-backed parameter
  adapters. Airtable and Postgres now share object-entry parsing, shared
  source reference expansion, shared option-array expansion, n8n
  `updateDisplayOptions` merge semantics, version property extraction, and
  credential extraction.
- Added source-backed Postgres v2 database operation parameter extraction from
  local n8n Postgres action descriptions. The Studio registry now preserves
  schema/table resource locators, insert/update/upsert `resourceMapper`
  metadata, SQL query options, select/delete fixed collections, shared option
  children, and generated-boot overlay coverage for
  `n8n-nodes-base.postgres`.
- Added source-backed Airtable v2 base/record operation parameter extraction
  from local n8n Airtable action descriptions. The Studio registry now
  preserves Airtable base/table/view resource locators, create/update/upsert
  column `resourceMapper` metadata, search/sort fields, insert/update option
  children, `/columns.mappingMode` child display rules, and generated-boot
  overlay coverage for `n8n-nodes-base.airtable`.
- Preserved nested editor-session dynamic parameter paths. Dynamic readiness
  plans now build provider-boundary ids from the full parameter value path, so
  nested fields such as `filtersUI.values.0.lookupColumn` no longer collapse
  to the leaf field name in DX Agents bridge metadata.
- Applied source-backed child parameter display rules during schema
  construction. Nested option children now evaluate n8n display rules against
  root values such as `/columns.mappingMode`, top-level source property reads
  ignore leading comments without inheriting nested child display options, and
  OpenAI routing model filters continue to resolve from nested load-options
  source.
- Preserved nested Google Sheets shared option spread variants. The
  source-backed append schema now carries imported option child controls and
  the duplicate `handlingExtraData` child with `/columns.mappingMode` display
  rules, matching another real n8n parameter-description edge case.
- Added generated-boot coverage for source-backed Google Sheets read
  parameters. The boot benchmark now verifies the app-level merged registry
  preserves read filters and shared `commonDescription` child controls for the
  UI/API boot path.
- Imported Google Sheets shared read-option fragments from local
  `commonDescription.ts`. Source-backed read schemas now preserve
  `options.dataLocationOnSheet` and `options.outputFormatting` child controls,
  including range-definition and output-formatting fields, instead of
  dropping identifier-based option entries.
- Added same-file object-spread resolution for Google Sheets source
  parameters. The read operation now carries n8n's spread-backed `filtersUI`
  fixedCollection, nested `lookupColumn` dynamic option boundary, and
  `combineFilters` options into the Studio schema instead of dropping them.
- Expanded source-backed Google Sheets v2 operation parameter extraction
  beyond append. The adapter now reads local n8n sheet append-or-update,
  clear, create, delete, read, update, and spreadsheet create/delete
  `description` arrays, while generated Studio boot overlays Google Sheets
  with the same richer source-backed registry.
- Added editable n8n `resourceMapper` controls to the parameter inspector.
  Google Sheets column mapping now has a mapper mode selector and mapped-value
  editor connected through the existing selected-node parameter mutation path,
  while keeping the source-only mapper schema boundary visible.
- Exposed n8n `resourceMapper` schema requests through editor-session
  readiness. Google Sheets column mapping now emits a blocked
  `resource-mapper-schema` request plan, bridge-visible mapper counts, and
  readiness response metadata while keeping secrets redacted and live provider
  execution disabled.
- Rendered n8n `resourceMapper` fields as explicit source-only parameter
  boundaries. Google Sheets column mapping now surfaces the mapper method,
  dependency fields, and disabled live-provider status in the parameter control
  instead of appearing only as generic structured text.
- Added source-backed Google Sheets v2 append parameter extraction for n8n
  Studio from local Google Sheets version/resource/operation descriptions. The
  parameter model now carries n8n `resourceMapper` fields as source-only
  dynamic schema boundaries, the display-rule parser skips complex source
  conditions it cannot evaluate safely, and generated Studio boot overlays
  Google Sheets with source-backed metadata instead of the generated shell.
- Made the DX n8n Studio store generated-catalog aware. The store now has an
  injectable factory for generated boot state and a semantic registry, routes
  node-creator actions through that registry, and keeps parameter/import-export
  mutations aligned with generated node descriptions for future Forge/DX store
  consumers.
- Wired the n8n Studio export API to generated catalog boot context. The route
  now uses the local generated connector source, includes generated metadata
  and catalog node counts for bridge consumers, and keeps the exported workflow
  response source-owned, redacted, and non-executable.
- Wired the n8n Studio readiness API to the local generated connector boot
  state. The route now reports generated metadata and full catalog node counts
  from the same source-owned catalog used by the Studio shell while preserving
  `liveProviderExecution=false` for the DX Agents/Zed bridge.
- Bootstrapped the default n8n Studio page from local generated connector
  metadata. The shared server boot helper reads the generated connector catalog,
  creates the full Studio catalog, merges generated node types with first-party
  source-backed registry overrides, and passes both the generated state and
  registry into the client app so generated-only nodes are addable by default.
- Made import preview application registry-aware. `applyImportExportActionToStudioState`
  now carries an injected semantic registry through preview loading, apply/save
  transitions, selected parameter creation, credential readiness, and
  editor-session readiness so generated Gmail previews apply with real
  inspector surfaces.
- Made workflow import sanitation registry-aware for generated connector
  metadata. `sanitizeImportedWorkflow`, node sanitation, and import preview
  creation can now use an injected semantic registry so generated-registry
  workflows keep supported nodes such as Gmail, preserve valid connections and
  pinned data, and continue stripping secret-like parameters.
- Made n8n Studio node creation registry-aware for generated connector
  metadata. Catalog filtering, addability checks, workflow insertion, selected
  parameter schema creation, and editor-session readiness can now use an
  injected semantic registry, so generated entries such as Gmail can be added
  with real resource/operation/credential surfaces when the generated registry
  is provided.
- Replaced the n8n Studio catalog panel placeholder search with a real
  source-owned node creator path. The panel now owns query state, filters
  catalog records, projects semantic-registry addability, and dispatches
  add-node actions for supported first-party nodes; unsupported source-visible
  entries stay disabled with a registry-boundary reason instead of creating
  fake workflow nodes.
- Added node-creator workflow insertion actions that create unique node ids and
  names, seed visible default parameters from semantic node descriptions, place
  new nodes to the right of the current graph, and reuse canvas selection to
  refresh parameters, credentials, expression state, and editor-session
  readiness.
- Added source-backed Gmail v2 parameter extraction for n8n Studio from local
  `nodes/Google/Gmail/v2/GmailV2.node.ts`, `MessageDescription.ts`,
  `LabelDescription.ts`, `DraftDescription.ts`, and `ThreadDescription.ts`.
  The adapter reads base Gmail resource/authentication fields, source credential
  metadata, message/label/draft/thread operations and fields, `multiOptions`
  label fields, and function-backed dynamic option boundaries for Gmail labels,
  aliases, and thread messages.
- Expanded source-backed parameter schemas to collect dynamic load boundaries
  from nested child fields and collection items, preserving paths such as
  `options.fromAlias` for editor-session readiness instead of only scanning
  top-level parameters.
- Added `examples/n8n-studio` as a DX-native n8n-compatible Studio foundation
  with `app/`, `components/`, `lib/`, `lib/stores`, `server`, `styles`,
  `public`, root extensionless `dx`, and Forge-first/no-node_modules policy.
- Added typed source-owned domains for local n8n catalog metadata, workflow
  document/canvas projection, parameter and expression surfaces, credential
  readiness, resource locator state, pinned data, execution readiness, AI tool
  lifecycle, import/export, and receipt summaries.
- Added readiness, catalog, and export route handlers that expose the provider
  boundary and keep `liveProviderExecution=false` until real n8n adapter and
  credential receipts exist.
- Added `benchmarks/n8n-studio-contract.test.ts` to guard DX-WWW conventions,
  local n8n manifest counts, required editor surfaces, secret redaction, and
  the no-upstream-UI-runtime/no-template-local-dependencies rule.
- Added `benchmarks/n8n-studio-semantic-metadata.test.ts` and the first
  semantic metadata slice: per-node source-owned metadata for HTTP Request,
  Slack, and OpenAI; parameter schema construction with display rules,
  credential selects, resource locator modes, dynamic load boundaries, and
  n8n workflow import sanitation for unknown nodes, secrets, invalid
  connections, pin data, and webhook IDs.
- Added `benchmarks/n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts` and generated local
  connector ingestion for `examples/n8n-studio`: a pure adapter over
  `integrations/n8n-nodes-base/generated/dx-automations-connectors.json`, a
  deterministic generated node-type registry, generated resource/operation and
  credential-select parameter shells, generated catalog projection, and catalog
  route wiring with source-missing fallback metadata.
- Added `benchmarks/n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts` and source-backed
  Slack message parameter extraction for `examples/n8n-studio`: the adapter
  reads local n8n `MessageDescription.ts` parameter descriptions, resolves
  spread resource locators, emits message post fields such as select,
  channel/user locator, message type, text, blocks, attachments, and options,
  and marks complex source fields for UI rendering follow-up.
- Expanded the source-parameter adapter to extract nested child parameters for
  Slack collection and fixed-collection fields, including attachment fields and
  nested title/value/short groups, while keeping complex source fields visible
  in the parameter inspector as explicit rendering boundaries.
- Added nested collection editor state for source-backed Slack parameters:
  repeated attachment values now become `collectionItems`, fixedCollection item
  groups retain labels and values, and the inspector renders recursive
  parameter controls for child fields instead of summary-only text.
- Added source-owned nested parameter mutation actions for n8n Studio, including
  immutable add/remove/update helpers for collection paths, selected-node schema
  refresh after mutations, store action wiring, and callback-gated collection
  control hooks with no visible dummy buttons.
- Wired nested parameter mutations into the live n8n Studio client shell so the
  rendered inspector applies selected-node add/remove/update operations through
  source-owned state instead of static fixture props.
- Added type-aware n8n Studio parameter input coercion for boolean checkbox
  updates, finite numeric values, JSON fields, and structured resource-locator
  payloads before selected-node mutations are applied.
- Added field-level resource-locator draft state for n8n Studio, including
  active mode, cached label/query, selected value, source-preserved URL modes,
  and explicit source-only editor-session adapter boundaries for list/search.
- Added selected-node credential picker readiness for n8n Studio with redacted
  id/name-only credential options, required credential status, and visible
  provider-boundary markers that keep secret values out of Studio state.
- Added import/export session readiness for n8n Studio with source-only import
  source options, sanitized preview summaries, issue actions, non-executable
  import boundaries, and export receipt metadata shared by the panel and export
  route.
- Added source-owned import apply/save flows for n8n Studio with a typed reducer
  that loads sanitized previews without mutating the current workflow, blocks
  previews with blocker sanitation issues, applies clean imports into the real
  editor document/canvas/inspector state, refreshes dependent readiness and
  receipts, records source-only draft save metadata, and adds first-party Manual
  Trigger metadata for common n8n imports.
- Added source-backed OpenAI parameter extraction for n8n Studio from local
  `nodes/OpenAi/ChatDescription.ts`, `TextDescription.ts`, and
  `ImageDescription.ts`, including chat prompt fixed-collection messages,
  text-edit fields, image-generation fields, dynamic model picker boundaries,
  source-only editor-session request plans, and default Studio OpenAI metadata
  aligned to the real parameter shape.
- Added source-owned expression editor state for n8n Studio, including selected
  parameter expression parsing, `$json`/`$node`/`$parameter`/`$workflow`/`$env`
  and `$credentials` reference discovery, secret-reference diagnostics,
  recursive parameter field annotations, and a real inspector panel wired from
  Studio state instead of fixed demo expression text.
- Added execution debug readiness for n8n Studio with a blocked manual-run
  attempt, per-node debug log rows, receipt import boundaries, no-secret
  markers, execution action metadata, and readiness API counters for the DX
  Agents bridge.
- Added execution receipt import for n8n Studio so replayable receipt data can
  populate success/error node logs, timings, item counts, and provider error
  summaries while dropping unknown-node logs and stripping secret-like fields.
- Added source-backed HTTP Request V3 parameter extraction for n8n Studio from
  local n8n TypeScript descriptions, including curl import, credential-select
  type arrays, boolean and hide display rules, fixedCollection query/header/body
  fields, and tool-only optimize-response parameters without importing the n8n
  runtime.
- Added n8n public API execution-history import planning for n8n Studio from
  local `nodes/N8n/ExecutionDescription.ts` and `n8n-api-coverage.json`, plus a
  sanitized `GET /executions` history adapter that produces governed execution
  receipts without storing raw provider output, secret-like fields, or error
  stacks.
- Added an authenticated n8n public API client boundary for n8n Studio with an
  injected transport for `GET /workflows/{id}` and `GET /executions`, redacted
  readiness metadata for the DX Agents bridge, sanitized workflow imports, and
  execution-history receipt import without persisting API keys in returned
  Studio state.
- Added a credential-vault bridge for n8n Studio that reports redacted vault
  readiness, resolves `n8nApi` credentials by id through an injected secret
  loader, and keeps secret values, secret refs, and secret-like field names out
  of returned Studio state.
- Added editor-session adapter readiness for n8n Studio with source-owned
  request plans for dynamic node parameters, resource-locator list/search
  calls, and selected-node credential picker data, projected into Studio state
  and the readiness API while keeping live provider execution disabled.
- Added source-owned canvas interaction runtime for n8n Studio with typed
  selection, drag, pan, zoom, fit, tidy, guarded delete, select-all, and
  keyboard nudge actions, plus UI pointer/keyboard wiring and selected-node
  inspector surface refresh from canvas state.
- Added source-owned canvas edge handling for n8n Studio with validated
  source/target handle connection creation, duplicate and self-loop rejection,
  dynamic edge rendering, draggable reconnect handles, and endpoint-specific
  source/target reconnect validation.

## Dev Server Mode Selection

- Added a typed `dx dev` server-mode contract with `auto`, `axum`, and
  `may-minihttp`. Auto mode keeps Axum for hot reload, devtools, route handlers,
  and server source directories, while static projects can use the tiny
  may-minihttp-style TCP responder.
- Reused the existing bounded DX wire helpers for the tiny path in normal
  dev-server builds instead of adding a second HTTP framework dependency.
- Reduced Axum GET/HEAD overhead by skipping body collection for bodyless
  requests while preserving body parsing when `Content-Length` or
  `Transfer-Encoding` signals a body.
- Added source-owned `examples/template-axum` and
  `examples/template-may-minihttp` examples with `.ts`/`.tsx` app sources and no
  `.js`, `.cjs`, or `.mjs` template scripts.

## Architecture Scope Reset

- Removed external framework debugging tool clone targets and Turbopack runtime/build
  adoption from the active DX-WWW completion contract. DX-WWW is a DX-owned WWW
  framework with Next-familiar authoring. External framework debugging tool clone
  targets are out of scope. Turbopack runtime/build adoption is out of scope.
  DX dev feedback, hot reload, the basic overlay at `/_dx/feedback`,
  diagnostics/code frames, route graph/status, App Router support,
  Forge-first/no-node_modules behavior, and Turbopack reference/provenance stay
  in scope. Runtime/build adoption is not a DX-WWW goal, and Turbopack does not
  power `dx build` or `dx dev`.

- Reframed the default template architecture contract and launch readiness Zed
  handoff summary around DX-owned behavior. The active contract no longer
  publishes Turbopack adoption/power fields or `full_next_parity`; it now
  reports `dx_source_build=true`, external bundler runtime execution/requirement
  false, and `next_familiar_authoring=true`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`, the active
  scope removal guard, and touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains
  pending behind active shared Rust jobs.

- Reframed App Router request-props, navigation control-flow, metadata, and
  render-plan contracts around DX-owned source behavior. The active contract
  data and focused guards no longer require external framework parity or
  parity-blocker fields; they now expose source-owned contract markers, explicit
  external-runtime-required/executed false markers, and `production_blockers`
  for remaining render-plan work. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-router-source-owned-vocabulary.test.cjs`
  plus focused App Router request, navigation, metadata, and render-plan
  guards; focused Cargo proof remains pending behind active shared Rust jobs.

## 2026-05-23 Launch Work

- Extended Next-familiar `dx build` fixture coverage to `src/app` and
  `page.{tsx,jsx,ts,js}`. The fixture writer now walks all configured App
  Router roots, filters source files without `node_modules`, and computes
  metadata route scopes from the detected root instead of assuming root
  `app/`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-next-familiar-fixtures-contract.test.cjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt; no runtime parity or external framework clone
  claim is made.

- Added source-owned execution for simple numeric response status aliases in
  App Router route handlers. Safe `Response.json(...)` and `new Response(...)`
  paths now read local literal aliases such as `const accepted = 202` when
  parsing `{ status: accepted }`, without evaluating arbitrary expressions or
  importing Next/Node/Turbopack runtime paths. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending behind active
  shared Rust jobs.

- Kept package self-reference resolution behind explicit package exports. When
  `package.json` has `exports`, DX now builds source-owned aliases only for
  safe exported targets; unexported package self-reference subpaths and exports
  pointing through `node_modules` are reported as
  `package-export-adapter-boundary` dependencies with
  `node_modules_required=false`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-package-export-boundary.test.cjs`,
  the existing source resolver guard, and touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo
  proof was skipped because shared Rust jobs were active.

- Accepted safe `RedirectType.push` / `RedirectType.replace` arguments in the
  DX-owned App Router navigation control-flow scanner. The source-safe scanner
  now handles string and enum redirect type forms while continuing to ignore
  comments/string literals, avoid `node_modules`, and leave dynamic redirect
  destinations as adapter-boundary work. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\tsx-app-router-navigation-control-flow.test.ts`,
  `node --check .\benchmarks\tsx-app-router-navigation-control-flow.test.ts`,
  and targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo behavior proof timed out during a fresh
  alternate-target compile.

- Added source-owned execution for route-handler header reads. Safe App Router
  route handlers now resolve `request.headers.get(...)`, `const headers =
  request.headers` aliases, and literal `??` defaults from
  `DxReactRouteHandlerRequest` headers, covering common auth/preview/webhook
  checks without importing Next runtime, running Node lifecycle scripts, or
  claiming external bundler runtime adoption. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`;
  focused Cargo proof remains pending behind active shared Rust jobs.

- Added source-owned execution for typed route-handler body aliases. Safe
  `Response.json(...)` routes now recognize typed TypeScript request-body
  aliases such as `const body: Payload = await request.json()` and
  `(await request.json()) as Payload`, so `body.field` reads bind to the
  parsed DX request body instead of falling into unsupported literal handling.
  Guarded with `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-typed-body-alias.test.cjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt; the focused Cargo behavior test is present but
  pending behind active shared Cargo/rustc jobs.

- Added source-owned execution for `await request.text()` body aliases in safe
  App Router route handlers. Raw text bodies now bind through the same DX
  request-body alias path as `request.json()` for `Response.json(...)`
  responses, covering common webhook signature payloads without Node streams,
  provider execution, external framework clone parity, or external bundler runtime claims.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`;
  focused Cargo behavior proof timed out behind active shared Cargo/rustc jobs.

- Added source-owned execution for simple `await request.formData()` aliases in
  safe App Router route handlers. `form.get("field")` now resolves from an
  object-shaped DX request body with literal `??` fallbacks, covering common
  form/contact handlers while leaving multipart parsing, file uploads, and
  browser stream semantics outside this source-safe slice. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`;
  focused Cargo proof remains pending behind the shared Rust queue.

- Added source-owned execution for safe NextRequest URL property reads in App
  Router route handlers. `request.nextUrl.pathname`, `request.nextUrl.search`,
  `request.nextUrl.href`, simple `request.nextUrl` aliases, destructured URL
  property aliases, and `new URL(request.url)` aliases now resolve from the DX
  request path without Next runtime, Node lifecycle scripts, or external
  bundler runtime adoption.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`;
  focused Cargo proof remains pending behind active shared Rust jobs.

- Preserved App Router required versus optional catch-all semantics in the
  DX-owned router. `app/docs/[...slug]` now maps to a required internal
  catch-all pattern that does not match `/docs`, while
  `app/docs/[[...slug]]` keeps optional empty-param matching. `RoutePattern`
  and the exported trie matcher both carry this boundary without Next runtime,
  React/RSC, Turbopack, or template-local `node_modules`. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-app-router-catch-all-semantics.test.cjs`,
  prior router/API source guards, and touched-file rustfmt.

- Added a DX-owned generated-to-source source-map adapter inspired by
  Turbopack core `SourceMap`. The adapter normalizes source segments and
  resolves generated output positions back to source positions for future
  build/dev diagnostics while keeping Turbopack, Node/NAPI, and
  template-local `node_modules` outside the DX runtime path. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\next-rust-source-map-adapter.test.cjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof timed out behind active shared
  Cargo/rustc work, and no `dx build` integration or external bundler runtime adoption is
  claimed.

- Aligned build diagnostics with `src/app` App Router roots. Segment files and
  route handlers under `src/app` now use the same source-owned parse/code-frame
  validation as root `app/`, so broken `src/app/layout.tsx` or
  `src/app/api/**/route.ts` files are no longer skipped by diagnostics after
  route discovery finds them. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\diagnostics-code-frame-contract.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof timed out behind active shared
  Cargo/rustc jobs.

- Added a source-safe DX dev feedback code-frame endpoint and diagnostics enrichment.
  `/_dx/feedback/errors` now enriches safe diagnostics with DX-rendered source
  frames from project files, while `/_dx/feedback/source-frame` exposes the
  same renderer as a side-effect-free endpoint. Unsafe or missing source
  locations remain marked as adapter-boundary, and this does not claim external
  overlay runtime, React overlay, external bundler HMR, editor process launch, or
  template-local `node_modules` execution. Guarded with red/green focused
  Cargo and `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-dev-feedback-contract.test.cjs`.

- Added source-owned execution for NextRequest-style route-handler query
  reads. The safe App Router route-handler interpreter now resolves
  `request.nextUrl.searchParams.get(...)`,
  `new URL(request.url).searchParams.get(...)`, and simple local aliases with
  literal `??` defaults from the captured DX request search params. Guarded
  with `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`;
  no Next runtime, Node lifecycle execution, browser/server proof, full
  external framework clone parity, or external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Added source-owned App Router navigation support for `RedirectType.push` and
  `RedirectType.replace` in safe literal `redirect()` /
  `permanentRedirect()` calls. The DX control-flow metadata now accepts those
  Next-familiar redirect type arguments while preserving adapter-boundary
  handling for dynamic destinations and arbitrary expressions. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\tsx-app-router-navigation-control-flow.test.ts`
  and touched-file rustfmt; no React/RSC runtime, browser/server proof,
  external framework clone parity, or external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Registered explicit `HEAD` and `OPTIONS` exports in the DX-owned API route
  scanner. The router already modeled these methods, but source detection only
  populated GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH; route metadata now reflects
  HEAD/OPTIONS-only handlers across Rust, TS/JS, Python, and Go source shapes
  without using Next runtime, Turbopack, or template-local `node_modules`.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-api-router-http-method-detection.test.cjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof is still pending behind active
  shared Cargo work.

- Added installed-smoke validation for top-level server-data route manifests.
  The smoke harness now reads `.dx/build/manifest.json` `server_data_routes`
  and `server_data_routes_compiled`, checks the root route against the emitted
  `.dx/build/source-routes/root/server-data.json`, and rejects stale manifest
  output paths that drift from the source-build route-output pointer. Guarded
  with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`;
  no installed binary refresh, browser/server run, external bundler runtime adoption, or full
  external framework clone parity is claimed.

- Hardened DX-owned Rust route matching for browser request paths. The
  file-system route pattern matcher and API router now strip query/hash
  suffixes before matching and percent-decode dynamic/catch-all path params, so
  `/blog/launch%20notes?preview=true` resolves to the intended route data
  without relying on Next runtime, Turbopack, or `node_modules`. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-router-request-normalization.test.cjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt; no live dev-server/browser or Cargo proof is
  claimed.

- Added dependency invalidation propagation to the DX-owned Rust
  Next/Turbopack task graph adapter. The adapter now reports direct changes,
  dependency-stale nodes, and the combined affected rebuild frontier from
  source graph facts while keeping Turbopack, Node/NAPI, Turbo Persistence, and
  template-local `node_modules` out of the DX core path. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\next-rust-task-input-adapter.test.cjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; no `dx build` cache integration, Cargo proof, or
  external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Added DX dev feedback receipt and dx-check panels. The DX-owned Rust/Axum dev feedback
  surface now serves `/_dx/feedback/receipts` and
  `/_dx/feedback/dx-check`, inventories real `.dx/receipts` /
  `.dx/forge/receipts` JSON files, and surfaces check/diagnostics presence in
  the vanilla overlay without a Next, React, Turbopack, or template-local
  `node_modules` dependency. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-dev-feedback-contract.test.cjs` and a
  focused `dx-www` dev-feedback cargo test.

- Routed exported-const `createDxAIChatRoute(...)` handlers through the
  source-owned AI provider-boundary interpreter instead of the generic
  route-handler factory 501. With local `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` presence,
  `/api/ai/chat` now returns a 202 readiness-only receipt while preserving
  provider/runtime/tool execution boundaries. Guarded with red/green focused
  Cargo proof plus the route-handler readiness and App API extension guards;
  no live AI provider, browser, `dx build`, or Turbopack proof is claimed.

- Fixed explicit `HEAD` route-handler dispatch. The source-owned App Router
  route-handler interpreter now chooses an app-authored `HEAD` export before
  using the safe `GET` fallback, so HEAD-specific receipts no longer get
  shadowed by GET when both are present. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-head-method-parity.test.mjs`, the
  existing App API route-handler source guard, and touched-file rustfmt; no
  external framework clone parity, server/browser proof, or external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Published route-level server-data summaries in the top-level `dx build`
  manifest. `.dx/build/manifest.json` now includes `server_data_routes` and
  `server_data_routes_compiled` from the emitted
  `.dx/build/app/**/server-data.json` files, carrying route/output/source,
  status, entry count, request sample, execution model, no-Node/no-lifecycle
  flags, source-owned contract markers, and
  external-runtime-required/executed markers. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-router-server-data-build-contract.test.ts`;
  focused Rust behavior proof is currently blocked before execution by an
  unrelated `dx-www-compiler` lifetime error in
  `core/src/delivery/server_contract.rs`.

- Added installed-smoke validation for the compact graph consumer snapshot.
  The smoke harness now reads `.dx/receipts/graph/consumer-snapshot.json` and
  rejects output where source-module node counts, source-module concept
  coverage, compiled-from-source edge coverage, or Zed source-module metadata
  drift from the full graph receipt. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; no installed
  binary refresh, browser/server run, external bundler runtime adoption, or external framework
  parity is claimed.

- Added DX-owned dev feedback endpoints to the Rust/Axum dev server. `/_dx/feedback`
  now serves a small vanilla overlay, and `/_dx/feedback/events`,
  `/_dx/feedback/errors`, `/_dx/feedback/routes`, `/_dx/feedback/hmr`, and
  `/_dx/feedback/open-in-editor` expose source-backed route graph, HMR status,
  diagnostics receipt visibility, and explicit editor adapter-boundary
  metadata. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-dev-feedback-contract.test.cjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; no browser/server run, copied external overlay runtime,
  external bundler HMR, or template-local `node_modules` path is claimed.

- Expanded the default launch template Database ORM surface. The
  `db/drizzle-sqlite` lane now shows a real source-owned SQLite capability
  matrix for migrations, views, relational queries, joins, set operations,
  CTEs, transactions, prepared queries, conflict writes, mutations, and
  analytics, all backed by `dxDrizzlePackage` metadata and refreshed receipts.
  This remains source-owned/adaptor-boundary proof only; live SQLite files,
  migration rollout, driver install, browser proof, and production data policy
  remain app-owned.

- Tightened the installed `dx build` smoke output contract for source-module
  graph proof. The smoke report now summarizes `source-module` nodes for the
  launch fixture server support modules and fails when either the node or its
  `compiled-from-source` chunk edge is missing. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; no governed
  installed-binary refresh, browser/server run, external bundler runtime adoption, or full
  external framework clone parity is claimed.

- Routed exported-const `createDxAgentRoute(...)` handlers through the
  source-owned AI provider-boundary interpreter instead of the generic
  route-handler factory 501. With `DX_ENABLE_EXTENDED_AI_ROUTES=true` and
  local `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` presence, `/api/ai/agent` now returns a 202
  readiness-only receipt while preserving provider/runtime/tool execution
  boundaries. Guarded with a red/green focused Cargo test plus the
  route-handler readiness and App API extension guards; no live model/tool,
  browser, `dx build`, or Turbopack proof is claimed.

- Added bounded source-owned execution for `Response.json(body, options)` and
  `NextResponse.json(body, options)` when `body` is a safe alias for
  `await request.json()` or `request.body`. The route-handler interpreter keeps
  safe status/header handling and object-literal JSON responses while avoiding
  any Next runtime, Node, lifecycle script, full serializer, or Turbopack
  execution claim. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending behind active shared
  Cargo/rustc jobs.

- Tightened the final launch coordinator dirty-worktree owner hints for the
  current Forge/source guard set. Provider-boundary route-handler guards,
  package slice/materialization guards, source-build graph module proof,
  hot-reload/dev-feedback guards, and Forge status-panel token checks now route to
  an owning lane instead of becoming unclassified dirty entries. The dirty
  worktree still blocks final signoff; this only makes the handoff actionable.

- Extended provider-boundary truth across Payments and Instant route
  handlers. Stripe readiness, checkout, and webhook receipts plus Instant
  readiness/POST receipts now distinguish `providerConfigured` from
  `providerBoundary`, leaving `providerBoundary: true` and
  `liveProviderExecution: false` when local config makes a dry-run or
  readiness receipt eligible. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\provider-route-handler-boundary-truth.test.mjs`,
  existing route-handler/AI/n8n provider-boundary guards, benchmark syntax
  check, and touched-file rustfmt; no live Stripe Checkout, webhook
  verification, fulfillment, hosted InstantDB transport, browser proof,
  `dx build`, or external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Tightened template-local launch score wording. The default template README
  and changelog now describe `89/100` as the Forge/template source-check
  ceiling rather than production launch readiness, and keep browser route
  proof, build proof, and live provider proof explicitly gated. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-docs-honesty.test.cjs`; no
  browser, build, deploy, provider, or server proof is claimed.

- Added bounded source-owned execution for `new Response(...)` in App Router
  route handlers. The safe interpreter now handles literal/text response
  bodies, `JSON.stringify({ ... })` object bodies, safe status/header options,
  and existing request/body/context aliases without requiring the Next runtime,
  Node, or lifecycle scripts. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending behind the active
  shared Cargo/rustc queue. This does not claim full Web Response streaming,
  external framework clone parity, or external bundler runtime adoption.

- Emitted Rust source-build graph `source-module` nodes for support files under
  `lib/`, `server/`, and `src`, next to the executable `source-module-chunk`
  outputs. Chunks now link to their source node with `compiled-from-source`,
  and invalidation/concept-map receipts understand the source node kind without
  requiring project-local `node_modules`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-build-graph-source-modules.test.cjs`,
  `node --check`, and touched-file rustfmt; focused Cargo proof is still
  blocked by unrelated dx-style duplicate method definitions before `dx-www`
  tests run.

- Preferred package `source` conditions in the DX source-owned resolver for
  `package.json` `imports` and self-reference `exports`. Source-first package
  layouts now resolve `source` targets before generated `import` / `default`
  targets without requiring `node_modules` or claiming Turbopack/package-manager
  execution. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-source-condition-guard.test.cjs`;
  focused Cargo behavior proof remains pending behind active shared jobs.

- Aligned the Type-Safe API request-policy launch slice with the current
  source-owned generator and receipt contract. The materialized
  `api/trpc` request-policy surface now uses the canonical
  `x-dx-launch-template` header, and the dashboard workflow receipt lists DX
  runner guard commands while keeping live tRPC execution, dependency
  installation, browser proof, build proof, production router/auth/persistence,
  and observability explicitly unclaimed.

- Verified the Forge package lane proof sweep after the receipt mirror refresh.
  Package-status/read-model guards, package-lane panel materialization, and the
  launch truth/materializer route cluster now pass in this checkout when test
  temp output is directed away from the low-space `G:\Temp\UserTemp` root. This
  records source/materialization evidence only; browser runtime proof, broad
  builds, live provider execution, local servers, deploys, and real n8n
  execution remain unclaimed.

- Kept AI provider readiness honest after credential presence. The
  source-owned provider-boundary interpreter now distinguishes
  `providerConfigured` from `providerBoundary`, leaving `providerBoundary:
  true` and `liveProviderExecution: false` when local AI credentials make a
  202 readiness handoff eligible. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\ai-route-handler-provider-boundary.test.mjs`,
  `node --check .\benchmarks\ai-route-handler-provider-boundary.test.mjs`,
  and touched-file rustfmt; no model streaming, AI SDK runtime transport,
  provider billing, tool execution, browser proof, `dx build` proof, or
  external bundler runtime adoption is claimed.

- Wired the official Tailwind v4.3 fixture matrix to the normal focused live
  comparison guard. `liveComparison.normalTest` now points at
  `benchmarks/dx-style-live-tailwind-v43-comparison.test.cjs`,
  `normalTestRunsLiveTailwind` is true, and the guard executes real
  `tailwindcss@4.3.0` plus `@tailwindcss/cli@4.3.0` through the temporary
  `--no-save` live runner instead of relying only on checked-in equal-output
  canaries. This remains test-only evidence and does not add Tailwind as a
  dx-style runtime dependency.

- Made the `auth/better-auth` materialized session route honest and
  source-visible as a provider-gated boundary. The launch template route,
  preview route, and shared live-runtime materializer now expose Better Auth
  adapter identity, `credentialsConfigured: false`, missing Google OAuth env
  markers, and the app-owned cookie/database/deployment boundary while still
  avoiding any fake live session claim. Refreshed all 20 package receipt
  helpers after the shared materializer changed, and verified package-status,
  package-lane panel, receipt-refresh, launch honesty, maturity, and docs
  guards without claiming browser, build, provider, deploy, or server proof.

- Added bounded source-owned `notFound()` support to the App Router route
  handler interpreter. Route handlers that call `notFound()` now return a 404
  JSON receipt and `x-dx-route-handler-not-found:
  source-owned-safe-interpreter`, giving DX-WWW a real Next-familiar control
  flow path without requiring the Next runtime or claiming full not-found
  parity. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending behind the active
  shared Cargo/rustc queue.

- Refreshed launch-template Forge receipt mirrors after active worker changes
  moved `examples/template/launch-shell.tsx` and
  `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts`. The package-owned helpers
  synchronized the receipts, package-status row mirrors, and typed read-model
  hashes across all 20 lanes without hand-editing package truth or claiming
  browser, build, provider, deploy, server, or live n8n execution proof.

- Tightened installed `dx build` smoke proof for source-build server-data
  consistency. The smoke report now reads `route_outputs[].server_data_output`,
  requires the manifest-declared route-local
  `.dx/build/source-routes/root/server-data.json` artifact, and fails when the
  pointer is missing even if legacy `.dx/build/app/server-data.json` exists.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; no
  installed-binary refresh, browser/server run, external bundler runtime adoption, or full
  external framework runtime clone parity is claimed.

- Shared normalized route-handler URL matching across the remaining
  source-owned helper families. Payments, AI, Automation Connectors, Supabase,
  and Instant readiness now use `DxReactRouteHandlerRequest::path_for_match()`
  instead of raw `request.path` matching, aligning package helper interpreters
  with the outer App Router matcher for absolute request URLs while preserving
  handler-visible request data. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`;
  no live provider execution, browser proof, `dx build` proof, or Turbopack
  execution is claimed.

- Added JSONC tolerance to the source-owned resolver config parser.
  `jsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.json` can now contain line comments, block
  comments, and trailing commas while preserving string contents such as URLs.
  This keeps real TypeScript config files usable for DX aliases without
  requiring `node_modules` or claiming TypeScript compiler parity. Guarded with
  red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-config-extends-source-guard.test.cjs`;
  focused Cargo behavior proof remains pending behind the active shared
  Cargo/rustc queue.

- Strengthened dx-style's Tailwind v4.3 utility/directive proof matrix without
  claiming full parity. The official fixture matrix now includes
  `utilityDocsCoverage` for every public utility docs area, representative
  comparison class entries for the second-pair utility grammar gap, and
  `cssDirectiveCanaries` for supported, partial, missing, and
  unsupported-by-design CSS directive surfaces. Guarded with a red/green
  official fixture matrix test. Full Tailwind utility value/modifier grammar
  and full CSS directive parity remain unclaimed.

- Kept the Automation Connectors dry-run route honest after credential
  readiness. The n8n route-handler interpreter now distinguishes
  `providerConfigured` from `providerBoundary`, leaving `providerBoundary:
  true` and `liveProviderExecution: false` for GET readiness and 202 Zed
  handoff receipts. GET readiness also exposes `providerConfiguredCount` and
  `providerMissingConfigCount`, so local credential presence cannot read as
  live n8n execution. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\automation-route-handler-provider-boundary.test.mjs`,
  `node --check .\benchmarks\automation-route-handler-provider-boundary.test.mjs`,
  and `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check .\core\src\delivery\route_handler_automations.rs`;
  focused Cargo proof remains pending behind the active shared Cargo/rustc
  queue.

- Aligned the Documentation System Forge package lane with the current
  app-owned MDX adapter boundary. `content/fumadocs-next` now keeps the
  Fumadocs dashboard workflow guard, package docs, dashboard workflow receipt,
  package-status row, and typed read model on `dxWwwNextBoundary` instead of
  the removed template-local Next adapter claim. Guarded with focused
  Fumadocs/package-slice/Next-boundary checks; no live Fumadocs rendering,
  hosted search, OpenAPI proxy execution, browser proof, build, server, or
  deploy proof was claimed.

- Added source-owned resolver support for local `jsconfig.json` /
  `tsconfig.json` `extends` arrays. DX now follows relative or absolute
  source-owned extended configs, ignores bare package extends such as
  `next/tsconfig` without requiring `node_modules`, and preserves root/later
  local alias precedence for duplicate path patterns. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-config-extends-source-guard.test.cjs`;
  focused Cargo behavior proof remains pending behind the active shared
  Cargo/rustc queue.

- Added DX build graph support for source support modules under `lib/`,
  `server/`, and `src`. These files are now `source-module` nodes in the
  `dx.www.moduleGraph` contract, so App Router routes can retain graph edges
  through `@/lib/...`, `@/server/...`, and `@/src/...` imports and rebuild
  invalidation can walk from a changed support module back to the route without
  package installs or `node_modules` resolution. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts` and
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-core-map.test.cjs`; no browser
  proof, installed-binary refresh, Cargo proof, or external bundler runtime adoption was
  claimed.

- Refreshed Automation Connectors and Validation & Schemas package truth after
  `examples/template/package-catalog.ts` changed its Documentation
  System boundary marker. The package-owned helpers updated the n8n and Zod
  receipt, package-status, and typed read-model hash mirrors without claiming
  live n8n workflow execution, browser validation proof, provider credentials,
  build, server, deploy, or browser route proof.

- Added source-build server-data manifest consistency for App Router routes.
  The source-owned build engine now writes route-local `server-data.json`
  artifacts under `.dx/build/source-routes/*/`, exposes them through
  `source-build-manifest.json` as `server_data_routes`, attaches
  `server_data_output` to route outputs, and records server-data route/entry
  counts in the source-build receipt summary. Route-local server-data now
  reports source-owned contract markers and external-runtime-required/executed
  markers instead of publishing a full external framework runtime parity field.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-router-server-data-build-contract.test.ts`
  and focused
  `cargo test -q -p dx-www --test source_build_server_data source_build_manifest_records_route_server_data_outputs --no-default-features --features cli --jobs 1 -- --nocapture`.
  This is source-build behavior only; no external framework runtime clone parity, Turbopack
  build execution, browser/dev-server proof, installed-binary refresh, or
  deploy proof was claimed.

- Hardened DX diagnostic code-frame rendering for tab-indented source. Code
  frames now expand tabbed lines before rendering and translate source-span
  columns into terminal-visible display columns before caret placement, so bad
  TSX/App Router diagnostics stay aligned without claiming broader
  Next/Turbopack diagnostic parity. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\diagnostics-code-frame-contract.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "DX diagnostic code-frame contract is public and adapter-boundary only"`,
  `node --check .\benchmarks\diagnostics-code-frame-contract.test.mjs`, and
  `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check .\dx-www\src\diagnostics\code_frame.rs`;
  focused Cargo output showed the new unit passing once, but concurrent
  Cargo/rustc locks prevented a clean final Cargo exit.

- Added DX build graph support for Next-familiar `@/` source-root imports.
  App Router routes and components now keep graph edges for imports such as
  `@/components/LaunchPanel` and `@/styles/app.generated.css`, preserving
  route invalidation without package installs or `node_modules` resolution.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`; no browser
  proof, installed-binary refresh, Rust resolver Cargo proof, or Turbopack
  execution was claimed.

- Refreshed Automation Connectors package truth after the launch-template
  automation status surface moved to DX theme tokens. The n8n receipt,
  package-status row, and typed read model now agree on the current
  `automations-status.tsx` hash without claiming live n8n execution, webhook
  registration, credentials, browser proof, build, server, or deploy proof.

- Refreshed Documentation System package truth after the source-owned
  Fumadocs default Next config boundary changed. The Documentation System
  receipt, package-status row, and typed read model now agree on
  `core/src/ecosystem/forge_fumadocs.rs` without claiming live Fumadocs
  rendering, hosted search, dependency installation, browser proof, build,
  server, or deploy proof.

- Normalized core safe route-handler endpoint matching for absolute request
  URLs. Absolute inputs such as
  `https://example.test/api/status?preview=1#details` now match
  `app/api/status/route.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` by stripping only the URL
  scheme/authority before existing search/fragment matching, while preserving
  the original request value for handler-visible data. The source-owned tRPC
  compat interpreter now uses the same normalized path, so absolute
  `/api/trpc/health` requests do not fall through to the generic 501 helper
  boundary after endpoint matching. The `route_handler_compat.rs` helper
  family now shares `DxReactRouteHandlerRequest::path_for_match()` for
  database-api, query-cache, Instant route-factory, and Better Auth
  readiness/session/catch-all path gates. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`;
  focused Cargo proof was attempted and timed out after 120s while other
  Cargo/rustc jobs became active.

- Completed non-catalog package metadata cleanup for the launch-template package truth
  surfaces. Preview manifests, Studio edit-contract metadata, and browser
  preview pages no longer expose `www/template`, `dx-icons`, or aggregate
  `shadcn/ui` as Forge package ids; root surfaces now use catalog ids such as
  `3d/launch-scene` and `dx/icon/search`, while `/ui` lists concrete shadcn
  slices. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts` and the
  focused Forge/template launch cluster. No browser proof, live provider proof,
  broad build, local server, or deploy was claimed.

- Hardened DX build graph CSS asset URL parsing. The graph scanner now accepts
  case-insensitive and whitespace-formatted CSS `url()` functions, keeping
  public asset edges and downstream invalidation intact for production CSS such
  as `URL ( "/icons/mark.svg?v=1#sprite" )`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`; no server,
  browser proof, installed-binary refresh, or external bundler runtime adoption was claimed.

- Expanded dx-style's DX-owned Tailwind v4.3 CSS-first slice for functional
  `@utility` directives. Flat custom utilities now resolve common
  theme-key/bare/arbitrary/literal `--value(...)` forms, slash
  `--modifier(...)`, v4.3 `--default(...)`, and `@utility`-local `--alpha()`
  / `--spacing()` helpers without Tailwind runtime or CLI dependencies. The
  parser now keeps layered/nested `@utility` unsupported through precise
  diagnostics, and the directive ledger marks `--alpha()` / `--spacing()` as
  partial rather than globally missing. Guarded with
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test tailwind_v4_css_first -- --nocapture`.

- Normalized core safe route-handler endpoint matching for request search and
  fragment suffixes. The matcher now ignores `?query` and `#fragment` when
  deciding whether `app/api/**/route.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` owns a request, while
  leaving the original `request.path` visible to the handler body. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`
  and targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending while other
  workers' Cargo/rustc jobs are active.

- Added a Forge package-row maturity guard for the launch template. The new
  focused benchmark verifies that all 20 visible package lanes remain
  lock-backed, receipt-present, non-catalog-only, source-owned or
  adapter-boundary classified, and free of live-provider/runtime overclaims.
  Refreshed the launch-template receipt/status/read-model hashes against the
  current runtime materializer. This is package truth integrity only; browser,
  build, server, deploy, and live provider proof remain unclaimed.

- Tightened WebAssembly Bridge receipt hash honesty. The wasm-bindgen receipt
  helper now detects and refreshes the nested
  `source_guard_runbook_surface.file_hashes` mirror inside the dashboard
  workflow receipt, so the source-guard runbook hash cannot be current at the
  top level while stale inside the receipt surface. Guarded with focused
  WebAssembly Bridge receipt/hash-refresh tests; no generated `.wasm`, browser
  runtime, build, server, deploy, or provider proof was claimed.

- Aligned core App Router route-handler execution with route-group and
  parallel-slot path semantics. The safe contract/executor now filters
  non-public App Router path segments such as `(internal)` and `@admin` while
  deriving endpoints for `app/api/**/route.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, matching the CLI
  route matcher's public URL behavior without claiming external framework runtime
  parity. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof remains pending while the shared
  Cargo/rustc/link queue is active.

- Added a source-owned Tailwind v4.3 CSS directive ledger for dx-style. The
  new `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/directive_ledger.rs` ledger tracks
  `@theme`, Tailwind import migration input, `@source`, `@source inline(...)`,
  `@source not ...`, `@utility`, `@custom-variant`, CSS `@variant`, `@apply`,
  `@reference`, `--alpha()`, `--spacing()`, `@plugin`, `@config`, and legacy
  `@tailwind` with supported, partial, missing, or unsupported-by-design
  statuses. The Tailwind compatibility docs now include a `Full CSS directive
  parity | Partial` row, while the utility ledger remains an honest docs-table
  proof ledger instead of a full value/modifier parity claim. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-v43-gap-matrix.test.ts` and focused
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test tailwind_v43_gap_matrix -- --test-threads=1`
  using `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=F:\dx-www-codex-target-directive-ledger`.

- Restored the launch-template Authentication route-handler contract after
  drift. `/api/auth/readiness` now preserves its derived route status after
  spreading helper readiness metadata, and `/api/auth/session` plus the
  materializer source return the source-owned 200 no-store anonymous session
  receipt backed by `createTemplateBetterAuthSessionReceipt()`. This does not
  claim live Better Auth cookies, OAuth, database adapter execution, browser
  proof, or deploy proof.

- Added package self-reference `exports` support to the source resolver. Safe
  local targets from `package.json` self-reference exports now take precedence
  over broad fallback aliases, while unsafe `node_modules` export targets are
  ignored and stay no-install unresolved source aliases. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-config-source-guard.test.cjs`;
  focused Cargo behavior proof remains blocked by unrelated dirty `dx-style`
  compile errors in the shared tree.

- Aligned core App Router route-handler execution with Next-familiar route
  extensions. The safe contract/executor now derives endpoints for `route.tsx`,
  `route.js`, and `route.jsx` using the same filename family as the build
  collector, instead of accepting only `/route.ts`. This prevents source-build
  route metadata from recognizing a handler that the safe runtime contract
  would reject. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  targeted rustfmt; focused Cargo proof was attempted twice but timed out in
  the active shared Cargo/rustc/link queue, so broad/compiled/runtime proof
  remains unclaimed.

- Added the Forge browser-proof approval checklist. The launch notes now keep
  package-status/read-model integrity separate from governed browser/runtime
  evidence, naming `/`, `/dashboard`, `/login`, and `/launch` as the approval
  routes and preserving provider packages as boundary/readiness lanes until
  real credential-backed execution exists.

- Split dev HTTP helpers out of the giant CLI module. `dev_http.rs` now owns
  request parsing, Axum request/response conversion, cache/header policy,
  hot-reload source tokening, response header formatting, and raw dev wire
  serialization, while `Cli` keeps thin wrappers for existing tests and call
  sites. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\cli-dev-http-split-safety.test.ts`; this is
  architecture-risk reduction only, not a broad Cargo/build/server proof.

- Added a configured local receipt path for the Stripe webhook route-handler
  interpreter. `/api/stripe/webhook` now returns a 202
  `configured-source-owned-webhook-receipt` when Stripe server/webhook env
  presence, a `stripe-signature` header, and a JSON payload are present. The
  receipt classifies the event type but still keeps signature verification,
  event routing, fulfillment, live Stripe execution, secret values, browser
  proof, and deploy proof unclaimed.

- Aligned the Automation Connectors source-guard runbook with the current
  package receipt hash contract. The runbook now reports the same 14 tracked
  files as the helper, receipt, package-status row, and typed read model, and
  the focused package-status test compares the runbook count to the receipt
  manifest so the mismatch cannot return silently. This is source/receipt
  integrity only; no live n8n, browser, build, server, deploy, provider
  credential, or webhook proof was claimed.

- Corrected Automation Connectors package-status honesty. The package docs now
  match the pruned 14-file receipt helper/read-model contract and explicitly
  keep `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` out of Automation Connectors hash proof. The
  package-owned helper refreshed the Automation Connectors receipt,
  package-status row, and typed read model without claiming live n8n execution,
  provider credentials, browser proof, build proof, server proof, or deploy
  proof.

- Tightened launch runtime proof wording for Forms + Validation. The runtime
  contract now uses `runtimeProof: false` and moves the static browser-preview
  evidence to `sourceProof: "source-owned-static-preview"`, with generated
  launch-runtime copies and Forge receipt hashes refreshed. This is source and
  receipt honesty only; no browser, live form runtime, provider, build, server,
  or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added governed dx-style live Tailwind comparison and a utility docs-table
  ledger. `tools/style/tailwind-live-comparison.cjs` reads the checked-in
  equal-output canary fixture and can run real Tailwind only when explicitly
  opted in with `DX_STYLE_RUN_LIVE_TAILWIND=1` or `--live`, using
  `@tailwindcss/cli@4.3.0` whose dependency pins `tailwindcss@4.3.0`; normal
  tests verify the harness in skip mode and dx-style manifests still do not
  declare Tailwind. `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/utility_ledger.rs`
  now records Tailwind v4.3 public utility docs areas with representative
  supported canaries and still-unproven value/modifier canaries, explicitly
  keeping full utility grammar parity unclaimed. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-v43-gap-matrix.test.ts` and
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test tailwind_v43_gap_matrix -- --test-threads=1`
  via `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=F:\dx-www-codex-target`.

- Hardened launch form-runtime proof honesty. The Forms plus Validation &
  Schemas runtime contract now reports `runtimeProof: false` with
  `sourceProof: "source-owned-static-preview"` in both the authored runtime
  source and materialized public runtime asset, and the source-honesty guard
  checks both files so browser-proof wording cannot drift back into generated
  launch output.

- Added honest dx-style plugin/theme-extension gap proof. The Tailwind v4.3
  matrix now keeps official Tailwind plugins explicitly `Missing` with
  typography/forms/aspect-ratio and plugin API canaries, keeps advanced
  theme/config extension behavior `Partial` with animation, transition,
  container-query, grid edge grammar, and plugin `theme()` canaries, and adds
  a source-owned utility docs ledger without adding Tailwind runtime or broad
  utility internals. The style README now says the PostCSS starter contract is
  scoped instead of "100/100".

- Added the governed dx-style live Tailwind comparison guard. The
  `tools/style/tailwind-live-comparison.cjs` harness reads the checked-in
  equal-output canary fixture, keeps normal tests at `liveTailwindExecuted:
  false`, and only runs `@tailwindcss/cli@4.3.0` behind
  `DX_STYLE_RUN_LIVE_TAILWIND=1`. The gap-matrix guard now also points the
  `@source not inline(...)` proof at the CSS-first Rust test that owns it,
  without claiming full Tailwind parity or adding a Tailwind runtime dependency.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 3D transform utility proof. The generator now
  emits normal CSS for named perspective presets like `perspective-dramatic`,
