  transform custom-property and safe arbitrary values like
  `transform-(--dx-transform)` and `transform-[matrix3d(...)]`,
  `scale-z-(--dx-scale-z)`, and negative `-scale-z-125` without Tailwind
  runtime or CLI dependency. Invalid forms such as `perspective-impossible`
  and `transform-[rotate(45deg);color:red]` remain unsupported through
  dx-check diagnostics.

- Added source-build discovery for destructured App Router route-handler helper
  exports. `.dx/build` method metadata now sees patterns like
  `export const { GET, POST } = createDxInstantRouteHandlers();` while avoiding
  false `GET` claims for alias destructuring. Guarded with focused red/green
  `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli exported_methods_detects_destructured_helper_exports --lib --message-format short`; this is build-metadata parity, not live provider or external framework route-handler runtime proof.

- Lowered DX Build readiness product scoring to an honest preview ceiling.
  `dx.build.readiness` and the source-projection path now report product
  `82/100` with `product_score_ceiling` and `product_score_basis` while
  installed-binary smoke, governed runtime proof, and live browser proof are
  still pending. The installed-smoke and readiness-gate checks now consume the
  same ceiling instead of preserving the old pending-proof `99/100` score.

- Hardened dx-style `@source` inline exclusion semantics. The CSS-first parser
  now understands Tailwind-style numeric brace ranges such as
  `bg-red-{50,{100..300..100},950}` and owns
  `@source not inline(...)` as an explicit class-family exclusion that
  subtracts generated candidates from scanned/safelisted output. This keeps
  migration CSS DX-owned without Tailwind installed and still does not claim
  full Tailwind source graph/glob parity.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 gradient interpolation modifier proof. The
  generator emits normal CSS for linear direction/angle interpolation modifiers,
  radial and conic interpolation modifiers, hue-method aliases, and safe
  arbitrary interpolation method values without Tailwind runtime or CLI
  dependency. Guarded examples include `bg-linear-to-r/oklch`,
  `bg-linear-to-r/longer`, `bg-linear-45/srgb`,
  `bg-conic/decreasing`, `bg-conic-180/shorter`, and
  `bg-conic/[in_hsl_longer_hue]`; invalid forms such as
  `bg-linear-to-r/banana` and `bg-conic/[bad;value]` remain unsupported through
  dx-check diagnostics.

- Added executable canaries for the strongest remaining dx-style Tailwind v4.3
  partial gaps. The matrix now names a Tailwind docs-table ledger for full
  utility grammar, OKLCH/default-palette and display-p3 fixtures for palette
  parity, stacked variant ordering plus escaped arbitrary selector fixtures for
  selector grammar, and an Autoprefixer equal-output fixture for browser
  fallback parity. This adds truth gates only; no broad utility generation,
  palette table expansion, selector rewrite, Autoprefixer clone, Tailwind
  dependency, or live Tailwind output comparison was added.

- Synced the launch README score wording with the guarded source truth. The
  public launch paragraph now states that the bounded Forge/template score is
  89/100 and that the Forge/template score stays below 90 until governed
  browser/provider proof, while keeping the separate dx-style 90-92
  source-guarded product path caveated. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-docs-honesty.test.cjs`; no browser, live
  provider, broad build, server, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Exposed route-handler receipt proof through the source-build manifest.
  `.dx/build/source-build-manifest.json` now carries
  `route_handler_receipts` with the receipt artifact path, executed GET/HEAD
  count, skipped unsafe-method count, and no-node_modules/lifecycle flags, so
  DX CLI/Zed/Friday-style consumers can find the proof without relying on
  in-process Rust report fields. Guarded with red/green
  `cargo test -q -p dx-www --test source_build_engine source_build_engine_records_app_api_route_handlers_in_manifest_and_graph --no-default-features --features cli --message-format short`;
  no installed-binary refresh, live `dx build`, server, browser, broad build,
  or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added a dx-style Tailwind dependency-leakage truth guard. The Tailwind v4.3
  feature matrix now carries `tailwind package dependency leakage` as an
  unsupported-by-design canary for the JS config/plugin ecosystem, and the
  focused Node guard checks dx-style-owned manifests for Tailwind package
  dependency declarations. This tightens the no-Tailwind-dependency boundary
  without adding utility grammar or claiming live Tailwind output parity.

- Added dx-style Tailwind replacement product-path proof for the starter,
  template, build graph, and dev-shell path. The focused guards now prove no
  Tailwind CDN/runtime path, no template-local Tailwind/PostCSS package/config
  assumption, no fake fallback route HTML, and no scoped TSX runtime hex/rgb
  colors where dx tokens should be used. Static browser-preview `/`,
  `/launch`, `/dashboard`, and `/login` pages load dx-style generated
  `styles/globals.css`, and the build graph receipt guard keeps generated CSS
  visible as `styles/app.generated.css`/`dx-style-css` evidence. Guarded with
  the default template contract, no-Tailwind-runtime guard, build graph receipt
  test, focused unsupported-class dx-check diagnostic proof, scoped leak scans,
  route-artifact checks, diff hygiene, and conflict-marker scan. This is about
  92/100 source-guarded product-path proof; live browser route proof,
  dev-server run, broad build proof, deploy, and live-provider proof remain
  unclaimed.

- Added n8n route-handler credential-presence handoff. The safe runtime env
  projection now includes redacted Slack and Notion credential presence for
  n8n connector routes, and the template n8n readiness/receipt helpers use
  that presence to distinguish missing config from a local Zed handoff. A
  credential-backed connector can now return a 202 `zed-handoff-created`
  receipt without exposing secrets or executing n8n. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`
  plus
  `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler n8n_configured_connector_run_returns_zed_handoff_without_provider_runtime --lib`;
  live n8n execution, provider API calls, webhook registration, browser proof,
  broad builds, servers, and deploys remain unclaimed.

- Hardened launch-template materializer temp cleanup. The Forge reality,
  app-route, and native-scroll materializer guards now remove generated `dx-*`
  temp directories with `try/finally`, so repeated source audit runs do not
  fill `G:\Temp\UserTemp` and fail with `ENOSPC` before package truth is
  evaluated. Guarded with the focused launch audit cluster and app-route/native
  scroll materializer tests; no browser runtime, live provider, broad build,
  server, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added explicit static browser-preview route-surface proof for the dx-style
  starter lane. The default template contract now names `/`, `/launch`,
  `/dashboard`, and `/login`, requires each checked-in preview page to load the
  dx-style generated `styles/globals.css`, and rejects Tailwind runtime scripts
  plus fake fallback wording on those surfaces. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`; this is
  static source proof, not live browser/server proof.

- Repaired Markdown & MDX Content package-status guard wiring. The
  `materialized_source.execution_guard` now points at the real checked-in `.ts`
  source guard instead of a stale `.cjs` path, and the audit cleanup guard now
  rejects stale or missing benchmark references from package-status as well as
  receipts. Guarded with red/green focused launch audit cleanup plus the
  Markdown & MDX Content slice guard; no live renderer, browser, server, broad
  build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added App Router route-handler alias export discovery. The source-build
  route-handler collector now recognizes named export clauses such as
  `export { handler as GET }` and `export { HEAD }`, preventing common
  Next-familiar route modules from being omitted from method metadata in build
  manifests, graphs, and receipts. Guarded with focused
  `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli exported_methods_detects_alias_reexports --lib`;
  external framework route-handler runtime parity, provider execution, broad builds,
  servers, browser proof, and deploys remain unclaimed.

- Added dx-style text-shadow color utility generation. Text-shadow size
  helpers now read `--tw-text-shadow-color` with CSS fallbacks, palette and
  opacity forms such as `text-shadow-cyan-500/50` emit
  `--tw-text-shadow-color`, typed arbitrary color values such as
  `text-shadow-[color:oklch(...)]/40` use existing OKLCH/color-mix behavior,
  and typed custom-property shorthand such as
  `text-shadow-(color:--dx-text-shadow-color)` survives variant parsing
  without a Tailwind runtime. The generated-output parity receipt records the
  color and stacked `hover:not-focus:*` canaries, and dx-check diagnostics keep
  unknown text-shadow-like classes unsupported. Guarded with red/green
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test text_shadow_color_css`; the focused dx-check
  unsupported-class test passed during implementation, while the parity receipt
  rerun was blocked by `rustc-LLVM ERROR: IO failure on output stream: no space
  on device` after `G:\` reached 0 free bytes. `git diff --check` and a
  touched-file conflict-marker scan were run; no local server, Tailwind
  CLI/runtime, package install, full Cargo workspace build, or broad launch
  build was run.

- Hardened Forge package receipt freshness. Package docs now publish receipt
  refresh commands through the module-safe
  `tools/launch/run-template-receipt-helper.js` wrapper, and the receipt runner
  guard checks that contract. Automation Connectors also prunes the legacy
  `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` hash from its package proof, so the n8n provider
  boundary no longer goes stale when unrelated CLI work changes the giant
  module. Guarded with `node --test --test-concurrency=1` across the 20
  package `*-receipt-hash-refresh.test.ts` files; no browser proof, live
  provider execution, broad build, server run, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Closed the TSX launch runtime hardcoded-color gap in the dx-style starter
  proof. The runtime shell now derives launch color variables from dx tokens,
  uses token-backed values for canvas proof colors, and the default template
  contract rejects Tailwind runtime/CDN leaks plus hex/rgb color literals in
  the TSX runtime. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`; browser
  route proof, broad build, live dev server, and deploy proof remain unclaimed.

- Improved CSS-first directive compatibility for dx-style. Tailwind v4-style
  CSS input now stays DX-owned: `@import "tailwindcss"` is migration input only,
  `@theme` continues to emit DX tokens, static `@source` / `@source not` /
  `@source inline(...)` directives shape scanner input with brace/range
  safelisting, `@source not inline(...)` subtracts explicit inline exclusions,
  flat `@utility` declarations and one trailing `*` functional segment register
  utilities, safe `@custom-variant` wrappers register variants, and authored CSS
  handles common `@variant` pseudo/dark wrappers. Unsupported Tailwind
  JS/config/plugin directives now produce receipt and core dx-check diagnostics
  through `unsupported_css_directive_findings` rather than being silently
  ignored. This does not claim Tailwind JS plugin ecosystem parity.

- Added a source-owned auth session receipt for the launch template. The
  `/api/auth/session` route now returns a 200 anonymous session receipt through
  `createTemplateBetterAuthSessionReceipt`, and the Rust route-handler
  interpreter recognizes the same helper with an
  `x-dx-auth-session` no-store response. The receipt keeps
  `liveSessionExecution=false` and does not execute Better Auth, read cookies,
  claim a database adapter, or prove live sessions. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`;
  browser/server proof, broad builds, live auth provider proof, and deploys
  remain unclaimed.

- Added source-owned App Router request-value decoding. Page and API route
  matchers now share a small `route_request_values` helper so dynamic params,
  catch-all params, and search params decode `%xx` sequences, query `+` becomes
  a space, and invalid percent pairs remain visible instead of disappearing.
  Guarded with red/green focused
  `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli decodes_request_params_and_search_params --lib`,
  the focused app API route-handler source guard, and leaf-file rustfmt; broad
  builds, servers, deploys, complete Next runtime parity, the unrelated Better Auth
  route-handler readiness guard drift, and global formatting of the giant CLI
  module remain unclaimed.

- Repaired stale Motion & Animation receipt guard paths. The source template
  receipt and browser-preview mirror now reference the real checked-in `.ts`
  benchmark guards instead of non-existent `.cjs` files. Guarded with the
  focused Forge launch truth cluster; no browser animation runtime, server,
  broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Repaired stale package workflow receipt guards for Drizzle, Documentation
  System, Internationalization, Backend Platform Client, and Validation &
  Schemas. The receipts now reference existing `.ts` benchmark guards, and
  the focused package tests assert readiness-workflow vocabulary instead of
  stale demo keys. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-launch-proof.test.ts .\benchmarks\fumadocs-dashboard-workflow.test.ts .\benchmarks\next-intl-dashboard-workflow.test.ts .\benchmarks\supabase-dashboard-workflow.test.ts .\benchmarks\zod-dashboard-settings-workflow.test.ts`;
  no browser, provider, server, broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Repaired public launch score-ceiling truth. README and launch notes now match
  the checked-in Forge reality manifest's bounded score, separate unbounded
  source score of 93/100, and live-proof ceiling of 89/100.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-docs-honesty.test.cjs`; no browser,
  provider, server, broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Hardened extended AI route-handler boundaries. The safe Rust interpreter now
  projects `DX_ENABLE_EXTENDED_AI_ROUTES` and mirrors the template
  `extended-route-disabled` response before extended AI routes can return local
  stream receipts or provider-runtime boundaries. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`;
  no live provider call, stream transport, server, browser proof, broad build,
  deploy, or Cargo proof was run.

- Hardened the installed `dx build` smoke deploy-adapter route-handler proof.
  The harness now requires `.dx/build/deploy-adapter.json` to include the
  `/api/checkout` POST route handler as `skipped-build-execution` with
  `node_modules_required=false`, `runtime_boundary.source_owned=true`, and
  external runtime flags set to false,
  so deploy output cannot look complete while dropping a non-GET App Router
  handler. The route-handler receipt count check now also comes from the
  fixture contract instead of a hardcoded literal. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; installed-binary
  refresh, live `dx build`, browser/server proof, broad builds, and deploys
  remain unclaimed.

- Added a configured local dry-run path for the InstantDB first-party route.
  `/api/instant` POST now returns a 202 DX route receipt only when
  `NEXT_PUBLIC_INSTANT_APP_ID` is present, while keeping `runtimeProof: false`,
  `networkCalls: false`, and hosted realtime/auth/storage/stream execution
  unclaimed. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs --test-name-pattern "DX route-handler runtime owns launch readiness helper interpreters"`
  plus focused `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler react_route_handler_runtime_marks_instant_post_configured_without_provider_runtime --lib -- --nocapture`.

- Closed the dx-style starter/dev-shell Tailwind leak. The default starter
  config now uses `tooling.dx_style.mode="generated-css"`, the default layout
  and dev/error HTML shells link `styles/theme.dx.css` plus
  `styles/app.generated.css`, and the old Tailwind CDN-backed fake marketing
  shell is guarded out. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`; no dev
  server, browser route proof, broad build, or deploy proof was run.

- Hardened the opt-in AI stream routes. `/api/ai/text-stream` and
  `/api/ai/ui-stream` now return the shared missing-config provider boundary
  when `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` is absent, even if
  `DX_ENABLE_EXTENDED_AI_ROUTES=true`, so local stream bridges cannot be
  mistaken for provider readiness. The AI Forge source manifest, package lock,
  cache manifest, package status, safety archive, package doc, and receipt
  hashes were refreshed from the updated route source. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts --test-name-pattern "extended AI provider routes"`
  plus green focused Lane 5/Forge package lock/read-model checks; no live
  provider call, model streaming, browser proof, server, broad build, or deploy
  was run.

- Cleaned stale local-interaction vocabulary across package workflow receipts.
  Top-level Forge package receipts and their browser-preview mirrors now use
  `local_readiness_interactions` instead of `local_demo_interactions`, and the
  audit cleanup guard rejects stale demo keys plus `data-dx-*demo` receipt
  markers. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts --test-name-pattern "package workflow receipts"`;
  no browser, provider, server, broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added the dx-style Tailwind v4.3 gap matrix. The latest Tailwind package
  target was verified with `npm view tailwindcss version` returning `4.3.0`,
  and dx-style now exposes a source-owned matrix for supported, partial,
  unsupported-by-design, and missing Tailwind areas. The highest-value canaries
  mark `@source inline/not`, flat `@utility`, and simple `@custom-variant` as
  partial scoped support, while CSS `@variant`, Tailwind JS config/plugin
  boundaries, and governed live Tailwind output comparison remain hard gaps.
  Public docs now state that dx-style is not a complete Tailwind v4.3
  replacement. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-v43-gap-matrix.test.ts` and green
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test tailwind_v43_gap_matrix -- --test-threads=1`
  with existing dead-code warnings; live Tailwind execution, official fixture
  ingestion, full selector ordering parity, full palette parity, and full
  utility grammar remain unclaimed.

- Cleaned stale AI SDK receipt vocabulary. The AI SDK launch assistant receipt
  and browser-preview mirror now use readiness wording for local
  prompt/provider interactions instead of `local_demo_interactions`, and the
  visible-proof guard rejects both that key and `data-dx-ai-demo` in the
  receipt. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts`; no model
  streaming, live provider credential, browser, server, broad build, or deploy
  proof was claimed.

- Cleaned the conversion-proof landing page's public vocabulary. Media cards,
  the secondary action, and route strategy copy now use preview/workflow
  wording instead of generic legacy showcase language, and the audit cleanup
  guard catches stale media, action, and route-label regressions.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts --test-name-pattern "conversion proof landing"`;
  no browser, provider, server, broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Hardened the installed `dx build` smoke route-handler contract. The tiny
  App Router smoke fixture now includes both `/api/health` GET and
  `/api/checkout` POST handlers, and the harness requires the POST handler in
  the source-build manifest, route-handler receipts, graph receipt, and
  server-contracts. The receipt collection now requires explicit skipped
  evidence for requestless-unsafe POST execution instead of silently ignoring
  that route. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; governed
  installed-binary refresh, live `dx build` product proof, servers, browser
  proof, broad builds, and deploys remain unclaimed.

- Cleaned stale InstantDB receipt vocabulary. The Realtime App Database package
  receipt and checked-in browser-preview mirror now use readiness/workflow
  markers instead of preserving `data-dx-instant-demo` and
  `local_demo_interactions`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\instantdb-dashboard-workflow.test.ts`; no hosted
  InstantDB runtime, browser, provider credential, server, broad build, or
  deploy proof was claimed.

- Hardened the AI provider route-handler interpreter for configured default
  and opt-in extended route readiness. `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` and
  `AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY` are projected as redacted presence-only runtime inputs,
  and `/api/ai/chat` plus explicitly enabled extended provider routes with
  credential presence can now return `202`
  `provider-configured-readiness-only` without executing model streaming,
  provider SDK calls, tools, billing, or secret reads. Disabled or
  missing-config extended AI routes remain 501 boundaries in the safe
  interpreter. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt; the focused Rust image-route handoff test was
  added but Cargo timed out in the shared compile queue before a result, so
  compiled proof is not claimed.

- Fixed launch receipt-helper command metadata under the template
  `type: module` boundary. Every `examples/template/*-receipt-hashes.ts`
  helper now publishes the repo-root
  `tools/launch/run-template-receipt-helper.js` dispatcher for `--check`,
  `--write`, and `--check --json` instead of direct
  `node examples/template/...` commands. Validation & Schemas
  receipt/status/read-model freshness was refreshed through the runner, and the
  source-honesty guard now rejects direct command template regressions across
  all receipt helpers. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`; no
  browser, provider, server, broad build, or deploy proof was claimed.

- Added route-handler policy readiness for Fumadocs OpenAPI proxy and AI
  provider boundaries. The Fumadocs proxy interpreter now evaluates
  `DX_FUMADOCS_OPENAPI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, returning 400/403/202 policy receipts
  instead of an unconditional 501, while still making no network call and
  claiming no Fumadocs runtime execution. Safe route-handler env projection now
  includes that allowlist plus redacted AI provider credential presence, and AI
  provider-boundary responses can report provider-configured readiness-only
  without calling a model provider. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\route-handler-readiness-interpreters.test.mjs`;
  live proxying/provider calls, servers, browser proof, broad builds, and
  deploys remain unclaimed.

- Cleaned stale conversion-proof public package claims. The conversion proof
  `/backend` route and preview manifests no longer advertise the non-catalog
  `backend/convex-compatible` or `backend/convex` package ids, the Vercel
  landing manifest no longer publishes `icons/lucide-react`, and the `/ui`
  manifest uses the current lock-backed component package ids. The stale
  auth, validation, automation, Supabase, scene, WebAssembly, and Drizzle
  launch markers in the conversion proof now use readiness/workflow wording
  instead of live-demo/demo markers, and the old DX landing selector now maps
  to cataloged `migration/static-site` instead of non-catalog
  `dx-www/launch-landing`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts --test-name-pattern "conversion proof"`;
  no browser, provider, server, deploy, or broad build proof was claimed.

- Hardened the AI SDK receipt helper under module-shaped templates. The
  `ai/vercel-ai` receipt helper now advertises the shared
  `tools/launch/run-template-receipt-helper.js` entrypoint in package-status,
  the typed read model, source-guard docs, and its own JSON output, and the
  package-owned receipt hashes were refreshed to current bytes. Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts --test-name-pattern "receipt hash helpers"`
  plus `node tools\launch\run-template-receipt-helper.js examples/template/ai-sdk-receipt-hashes.ts --check --json`;
  no AI provider call, model streaming, server, browser, or deploy proof was
  claimed.

- Scoped Lane 7 dashboard readiness scores. The UI/Motion/3D/WASM dashboard
  summary now marks its aggregate score as `lane7-package-summary` and each
  package row as `lane7-package-readiness-row`, with matching generated
  template/browser-preview HTML. Guarded with a red/green Lane 7 template test
  and the focused Forge launch truth cluster; browser/WebGL/WASM runtime proof,
  live provider credentials, broad builds, servers, and deploys remain
  unclaimed.

- Closed the launch package catalog honesty-label gap. Every
  `launchPackageCatalog` row now has an explicit `honestyLabel`, and
  `launch-template-source-honesty` guards both missing labels and labels outside
  the typed union. This is source/catalog truth, not browser runtime proof or
  provider execution. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`; receipt
  checks also surfaced separate stale materializer/helper-runner drift that
  remains launch-hardening work.

- Hardened the installed `dx build` smoke around App Router execution
  contracts. The smoke harness now parses `.dx/build/app/app-router-execution.json`
  and fails on wrong root route metadata, missing no-node-modules proof,
  reported local node_modules presence, or external framework runtime clone parity claims.
  Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; installed-default
  binary refresh, live route execution, broad builds, servers, deploys, and full
  external framework clone parity remain unclaimed.

- Hardened the AI SDK launch route against provider overclaims. The default
  `app/api/ai/chat` route, generated-starter materializer, and browser-preview
  mirror now use `provider-configured-readiness-only`, dynamic env-presence
  reporting, and explicit `runtimeProof: false` / `liveProviderProof: false`
  when an app supplies `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY`. The route still performs no model
  streaming or provider runtime call. The package source-honesty guard now
  scopes catalog parsing to `launchPackageCatalog` before enforcing explicit
  honesty labels. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts
  .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`; browser proof, live AI
  credentials, broad builds, servers, and deploys remain unclaimed.

- Closed the AI SDK extended-route inventory gap. Every non-chat
  `app/api/ai/*` route is now guarded as an opt-in provider boundary outside
  the default launch AI proof surface, and the package reality/dashboard row
  lists text stream, UI stream, and file upload along with the existing
  agent/image/object/speech/transcribe/video endpoints. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-launch-visible-proof.test.ts --test-name-pattern
  "extended AI provider routes"` after rematerializing the launch template and
  browser preview; no provider credentials, model streaming, browser proof,
  server, broad build, or deploy was run.

- Added App Router page-loader discovery to the public `DataLoader` registry.
  Loader discovery now includes `app/**/page.{tsx,jsx,ts,js,html}` beside
  legacy `pages/**/*.html`, derives route paths without `page` filenames, skips
  route groups/parallel slots, and ignores non-page files. Also removed the
  redundant `PAGE_EXTENSION` / `"html"` project-scan branch that became a
  compile warning after launch extension cleanup. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; the final targeted Cargo rerun timed out on the busy
  workspace while another Cargo job was active, so broad/compiled proof remains
  unclaimed.

- Extended App API route-handler matching to Next-familiar extensions. The
  dev/runtime matcher now resolves `route.tsx`, `route.js`, and `route.jsx`
  beside `route.ts` for exact, dynamic, and catch-all `app/api/**` routes,
  and ignores App Router route-group folders when mapping public API paths,
  aligning runtime lookup with source-build discovery without requiring
  `node_modules` or claiming external framework route-handler clone parity. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs`, green focused
  `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli
  route_handler_match_accepts_next_route_handler_extensions --lib`, green
  focused `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli
  route_handler_match_ignores_app_router_route_groups --lib`, green
  route-handler interpreter source guard, and touched-file rustfmt. The focused
  Cargo run still printed unrelated dirty-tree warnings in `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs`
  and `dx-www/src/project.rs`; broad builds, local servers, installed-binary
  refresh, deploys, and live HTTP proof remain skipped.

- Scoped launch dashboard package scores so they cannot be mistaken for the
  global Forge score gate. Package-row cells now carry
  `data-dx-score-scope="package-readiness-row"` plus the package score value in
  the source component and generated dashboard/preview HTML, while the global
  launch score remains capped at 89 until governed browser or live-provider
  proof exists. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-score-gate.test.ts --test-name-pattern "launch route copy"`;
  no server, browser proof, provider credentials, broad build, or deploy was
  run.

- Hardened the installed `dx build` smoke around server-data route contracts.
  `.dx/build/app/server-data.json` must now describe the root `/` route from
  `app/page.tsx`, and the `loadLaunchMetrics` entry must materialize the
  expected route-handler count instead of merely existing. Guarded with
  red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`;
  installed-default binary refresh, live route execution, broad builds,
  servers, deploys, and complete Next runtime parity remain skipped.

- Added a dx-style HWB color fallback canary. The Rust-native PostCSS
  compatibility layer now emits a legacy `rgba(51, 115, 179, 0.75)` fallback
  before `hwb(210 20% 30% / 75%)`, and the code-backed matrix plus JSON
  fixture now include `hwb-color-fallbacks`. Guarded with a red/green targeted
  Rust canary and green focused
  `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with
  15/15 tests passing; arbitrary PostCSS plugins, full modern color-space
  conversion, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, broad builds, servers, and
  deploys remain unclaimed.

- Promoted the launch database readiness route in route-probe evidence. The
  launch stabilizer now treats `/api/database-api/readiness` as a normal
  source-owned 200 readiness route through `--include-database-readiness`
  instead of counting it as an expected 501 adapter-boundary gap. The old
  boundary-named flag remains a compatibility alias to the same 200 probe.
  The coordinator dirty-file owner map now also classifies current
  parser-extension, route-handler, Forge/package, Flow Forge, launch-status,
  and dx-build proof handoff paths instead of leaving them unassigned.
  Guarded with red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\launch-stabilize-route-probe.test.cjs
  .\benchmarks\launch-stabilize-coordinator.test.cjs`; live port-3000 route
  proof, broad builds, deploys, and installed-binary refresh remain skipped.

- Moved generated launch report contracts out of the template-readiness receipt
  namespace. `launch-adoption-report` and `launch-manifest-drift` now advertise
  `.dx/forge/reports/*.json` output paths, while
  `.dx/forge/template-readiness` remains reserved for checked-in source
  readiness receipts. The template-readiness receipt guard now scans CLI and
  materializer sources as well, preventing hidden missing receipt references.
  Guarded with
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-template-readiness-receipts.test.ts`;
  browser runtime proof, provider credentials, broad builds, servers, and
  deploys remain unclaimed.

- Guarded launch package catalog maturity naming. Every launch catalog source id
  now has a DX-facing official package name, and the `LaunchPackageMaturity`
  contract includes the existing lock-backed adapter-boundary classification
  used for Authentication. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-source-honesty.test.ts`; browser
  proof, live providers, broad builds, local servers, and deploys remain
  unclaimed.

- Extended the DX Build readiness-gate source projection. Reports, consumer
  snapshots, generated `dx.build.readiness`, and generated Zed handoff now
  preserve content/MDX counts, CSS optimization/source-map counts, and image
  metadata/placeholder counts from `dx.www.sourceBuildReceipt`, while keeping
  product 100/100 gated on installed-binary smoke and governed runtime proof.
  Guarded with red/green `node --test
  .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`; no server, broad build,
  deploy, installed-binary refresh, or runtime proof was run.

- Closed the launch template-readiness receipt gap. Public launch/package
  surfaces that reference `.dx/forge/template-readiness/*.json` now resolve to
  checked-in source and browser-preview receipts, including launch route
  readiness, readiness bundle, runtime checklist, runtime approval request,
  runtime evidence stub, verification lane, companion docs, Zed handoff, and
  3D scene readiness. The new guard verifies every referenced receipt exists,
  parses, and keeps `runtime_proof`, live provider execution, network calls,
  fake proof, and secrets unclaimed. Browser/runtime/provider proof remains
  pending behind explicit approval.

- Added a dx-style mixed media range canary. The Rust-native PostCSS compatibility layer now lowers mixed strict/inclusive media range syntax such as `(48rem < width <= 72rem)` and `(30rem <= height < 50rem)` into readable min/max media queries while preserving the no-PostCSS runtime contract. The code-backed matrix and JSON fixture now include `mixed-media-range-syntax`, and the source guard requires that row while public wording stays scoped to "PostCSS replacement for DX starters." Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 14/14 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Broad builds, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity remain skipped by policy.

- Cleaned data-loader launch extension discovery. Legacy pages data-loader
  scanning now looks at `.html` launch page source instead of stale `.pg`
  files, and the public developer contract no longer includes `.pg` / `.cp`
  in allowed source globs. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`; broad
  Cargo builds, local servers, browser automation, deploys, full `dx build`
  proof, and App Router loader parity remain unclaimed.

- Repaired 3D Scene System receipt freshness. The lock-backed
  `3d/launch-scene` package lane now has current SHA-256 mirrors for the
  edited launch runtime asset in package-status, the typed read model, and the
  package receipt. Guarded with the red/green 3D subset of
  `launch-forge-package-status-read-model`, `three-scene-receipt-hash-refresh`,
  the 3D helper `--check --json`, and the focused launch truth cluster.
  Browser/WebGL runtime proof, renderer installs, broad builds, servers, and
  deploys remain skipped.

- Preserved route-handler evidence in DX Build readiness projections. The
  readiness gate now exposes `route_handlers` from `dx.www.sourceBuildReceipt`
  in the source-build receipt summary, generated `dx.build.readiness` graph, and
  generated `dx.build.zedHandoff`; its quality guard also covers the newer
  installed-smoke route-handler helper modules. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`; installed-binary
  refresh, local servers, broad builds, deploys, and governed runtime proof
  remain skipped.

- Tightened the installed `dx build` smoke around graph evidence. The smoke
  report now summarizes the `dx.build.graph` receipt and fails if
  `app/api/health/route.ts` is missing its `app-route-handler` node, `GET`
  method, source-owned execution contract, no-node-modules declaration, or
  no-lifecycle-script proof. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; installed-default
  refresh, live handler execution, broad builds, servers, deploys, and full
  external framework clone parity remain skipped.

- Hardened the installed `dx build` smoke against stale build receipt schemas.
  `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json` must now report schema `dx.build.graph`, and
  the source-build manifest plus both source-build receipt aliases must report
  their exact stable schemas. The Zed handoff receipt is now gated the same
  way. Graph-shaped, source-build-shaped, or editor-handoff-shaped
  experimental receipts now fail the smoke instead of being accepted as launch
  build evidence. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`; installed-default
  refresh, live handler execution, broad builds, servers, deploys, and full
  external framework clone parity remain skipped.

- Cleaned the public launch extension constants. `dx-www/src/lib.rs` now
  exports `.html` as `PAGE_EXTENSION` and `.tsx` as `COMPONENT_EXTENSION`,
  matching the parser/dev/build launch source contract instead of advertising
  stale internal `.pg` / `.cp` names to downstream code. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs`; broad
  Cargo builds, local servers, browser automation, deploys, and full `dx build`
  runtime proof remain skipped by policy.

- Added a dx-style custom property `var()` fallback canary. The Rust-native PostCSS compatibility layer now substitutes full declaration values for custom-property fallbacks, preserving valid declarations such as `border: 1px solid #1d4ed8` instead of producing token-only fallback output, and emitting authored fallback values from `var(--token, fallback)` including nested `calc(...)` values. The code-backed matrix and JSON fixture now include `custom-property-var-fallbacks`, and the source guard requires that row while public wording stays scoped to "PostCSS replacement for DX starters." Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 13/13 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Broad builds, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity remain skipped by policy.

- Added an explicit standalone Cargo boundary for Flow Forge. The root DX-WWW
  workspace now excludes `integrations/flow-forge`, so manifest-targeted Forge
  cargo commands resolve as their own package instead of failing before tests or
  pulling Forge's heavy media/VCS dependency graph into normal workspace
  checks. Added `benchmarks/flow-forge-workspace-boundary.test.cjs` to guard
  the boundary beside the stable schema/format guard. Verification: red
  targeted Forge cargo test first failed with Cargo's workspace-boundary error,
  green Flow Forge Node guards, and green `cargo metadata --manifest-path
  integrations/flow-forge/Cargo.toml --no-deps --format-version 1`. The
  focused Forge integration test now reaches dependency compilation but fails on
  a corrupted local `cc-1.2.56` Cargo registry extract under `G:\Dev\Caches`;
  no global cache repair, broad build, local server, or deploy was run.

- Cleaned launch readiness naming for WebAssembly and package docs. The
  checked-in launch runtime and browser-preview runtime now set
  `dxWasmLocalReadinessEnabled`; WebAssembly
  Bridge source generators now use `wasm-bindgen-readiness-workflow`; and Data
  Fetching & Cache docs now call the query lifecycle controls readiness
  controls. Guarded with red/green `launch-template-audit-cleanup`, focused
  WebAssembly Bridge source guards, and stale-marker scan. Browser runtime
  proof, provider credentials, broad builds, and deploys remain skipped.

- Removed hardcoded launch/dev color literals from two user-facing surfaces.
  The DX hot-reload error overlay now uses semantic system color tokens instead
  of injected hex/RGB values, and the launch-template favicon now uses
  `Canvas`, `CanvasText`, and `currentColor` instead of black/white SVG fills.
  The favicon Forge media cache/status/restore receipt/package metadata/read
  model were refreshed to the new SHA-256. Guarded with red/green hot-reload
  protocol and launch-template source-honesty tests, plus the Forge media
  restore and package-lock checks. Browser visual proof, servers, deploys,
  broad builds, and external bundler HMR claims remain skipped.

- Cleaned remaining launch readiness naming in source generators and public
  package docs. WebAssembly Bridge generator/check-panel source now points at
  `wasm-bindgen-readiness-workflow` as the readiness surface id, and
  Data Fetching & Cache / Automation Connectors package docs use
  readiness/workflow wording instead of demo copy. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts
  --test-name-pattern "WebAssembly Bridge source generators|public package
  docs"`.

- Added source-build route-handler graph evidence for `dx build`. The source engine now discovers `app/**/route.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}`, emits `route_handlers` in `dx.www.sourceBuildManifest`, counts them in `dx.www.sourceBuildReceipt`, adds `app-route-handler` graph nodes, and mirrors counts/details into readiness and Zed handoff receipts while keeping `node_modules_required=false` and complete Next route-handler parity unclaimed. The installed smoke harness now requires the tiny `app/api/health/route.ts` handler in the source-build manifest, not only in sidecar server contracts. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`, green focused `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_records_app_api_route_handlers_in_manifest_and_graph --test source_build_engine -- --nocapture`, and targeted rustfmt check. Installed-binary refresh, broad builds, servers, deploys, live route-handler execution, and complete Next runtime parity remain skipped.

- Updated launch README truth and Automation Connectors receipt marker naming. `README.md` now reports the current source-checked Forge/template truth instead of the old 40/100 audit baseline: bounded score 89/100, unbounded source score 93/100, live-proof ceiling 89/100 until governed proof is attached, 20 lock-backed package lanes, 30 catalog source ids, and zero live browser/provider packages claimed. Automation Connectors now exposes `data-dx-automation-local-receipt` in source-owned launch status, generated runtime pages, and browser-preview fixtures without retaining the prior demo-named marker. Guarded with red/green `launch-docs-honesty`, focused Forge reality/score/audit cleanup/materializer/automations tests, `git diff --check`, and touched-file conflict scan. Browser/runtime proof, live provider credentials, broad builds, and deploys remain skipped by policy.

- Cleaned the parser launch extension classifier. `ComponentType::from_path`
  now treats `.html` as page source and `.tsx` as component source, matching
  the parser module docs and launch authoring contract instead of requiring the
  stale `.pg` page extension. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; broad Cargo builds, local servers, browser automation,
  package installs, and deploys remain skipped by policy.

- Cleaned the production asset legacy extension classifier. The production
  build module no longer exposes `BinaryTemplate` or special-cases `.pg` /
  `.cp` as build assets; unknown legacy extensions now fall through to the
  normal `Other` static asset path. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-www-parser-launch-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt; broad Cargo builds, local servers, browser automation,
  package installs, and deploys remain skipped by policy.

- Cleaned the dev hot-reload legacy extension classifier. The dev server path
  now treats `.tsx` / `.html` as component changes and classifies old `.pg` /
  `.cp` files as static assets, so the preview launch path does not keep a
  dev-facing supported path for forbidden template extensions. Guarded with a
  focused Node source check and touched-file rustfmt; targeted Cargo unit proof
  timed out after 124s on the busy workspace without diagnostic output.

- Hardened public schema and DX Build readiness-gate source checks. The copied n8n source manifest now uses stable schema `dx.integrations.n8n_nodes_base_reference` plus numeric `format: 1` and no BOM, and the schema/status noise guard scans it for public `.v1` regressions. The DX Build readiness gate now summarizes source-build `node_modules` paths through a small shared helper, blocks source readiness when any source-build receipt path includes `node_modules`, and writes exact path counts/paths into compact readiness projections. Guarded with red/green schema-noise coverage and `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`; local servers, installed-binary refresh, deploys, package installs, and governed runtime proof were not run.

- Added a dx-style nested at-rule PostCSS nesting canary. The Rust-native compatibility layer now lowers nested `@media` and `@supports` rules inside selectors by wrapping the expanded parent selector inside the at-rule, preserving nested child selectors and avoiding invalid `.card @media` / `.card @supports` output. The code-backed matrix and JSON fixture now include `nested-at-rule-transform`, and the source guard requires that row. Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 12/12 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt check. Broad builds, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, cascade-layer flattening, and universal Autoprefixer parity remain skipped by policy.

- Cleaned one remaining public launch-surface readiness class. The checked-in
  `/launch` runtime pages now use `auth-readiness-field`, while package
  evidence selectors such as Zod, Scene, and WASM remain unchanged until their
  cross-lane guards can migrate together. Guarded with red/green `node --test
  benchmarks\launch-template-audit-cleanup.test.ts`.

- Fixed the dx-style public compile boundary. `style::core::engine` is now public so dx-check/Zed receipt contracts and Tailwind/PostCSS evidence helpers are reachable public API instead of private-module dead code, and the `StyleEngine` / theme CSS re-export shims are explicitly treated as intentional exports. Added `benchmarks/dx-style-compile-boundary.test.mjs` to guard the boundary under `#![deny(warnings)]`. Verification: red/green Node source guard and green `cargo check --lib -p dx-style -j 1`; quiet full-package check remains unclaimed because it was terminated without output while other cargo tests were active.

- Added dx-style compound custom-media, `place-*`, and `image-set()` PostCSS canaries. The Rust-native compatibility layer now expands custom media tokens inside compound media queries such as `screen and (--wide)`, emits align/justify fallbacks before `place-items`, `place-content`, and `place-self`, and adds `-webkit-image-set()` fallback declarations for starter responsive image CSS while preserving authored declarations. The code-backed matrix and JSON fixture now include `compound-custom-media`, `place-property-fallbacks`, and `image-set-prefix-fallback`; public wording remains scoped to "PostCSS replacement for DX starters" rather than arbitrary plugin or universal Autoprefixer parity. Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 11/11 tests passing, JSON parse check, and touched-file rustfmt. Broad builds, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, universal image-set syntax normalization, and universal Autoprefixer parity remain skipped by policy.

- Added dx-style `@nest`, strict media, and logical-directional PostCSS canaries. The Rust-native compatibility layer now lowers safe `@nest .theme &` rules, normalizes strict media comparisons such as `(width > 48rem)` and `(height < 40rem)` to calc-based min/max queries, and emits LTR physical fallbacks for directional logical declarations including `margin-inline-start`, `padding-inline-end`, `border-inline-start`, `inset-inline-start`, and `text-align: start`. The code-backed matrix and JSON fixture now include `nest-at-rule-transform`, `strict-media-range-syntax`, and `logical-directional-fallbacks`, and the source guard requires those rows. Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary in an isolated target, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 10/10 tests passing, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-postcss-compatibility.test.ts`, and green targeted `dx-style-launch-contract` source guards for the public scan contract plus Check/Zed style evidence. Broad builds, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary third-party plugin APIs, RTL/writing-mode universal logical fallback claims, and universal Autoprefixer parity remain skipped by policy.

- Added deeper dx-style PostCSS replacement canaries. `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/postcss_compat.rs` now emits Rust-native fallback CSS for safe `:not()` selector lists, simple srgb hex `color-mix()` values, transparent gradient stops, and safe legacy grid display/track canaries without requiring PostCSS or Autoprefixer. The code-backed matrix plus `related-crates/style/fixtures/postcss-compat-matrix.json` now list `not-selector-list-lowering`, `color-mix-fallbacks`, `gradient-transparent-stop-fix`, and `grid-template-prefix-evidence`; public wording remains scoped to "PostCSS replacement for DX starters" with arbitrary plugin parity and universal Autoprefixer parity still unclaimed. Verification: red/green targeted Rust canary, green focused `cargo test -p dx-style --test postcss_compat -- --test-threads=1` with 9/9 tests passing in an isolated target, green PostCSS compatibility Node source/JSON guard, and JSON parse check. Full builds, broad suites, servers, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, third-party plugin APIs, universal grid placement synthesis, and fair equal-output browser matrix proof remain skipped by policy.

- Added WebAssembly Bridge stale-only selected-surface attribution. The official **WebAssembly Bridge** receipt helper now reports `stale_surface_ids`, `stale_surface_types`, `missing_surface_ids`, and `missing_surface_types` from its selected-surface definitions, and mirrors those fields through package-status plus the typed read model when refreshed. The package-owned runbook fixture adds `webassembly-bridge-check-panel-source-stale-only`, which mutates only `core/src/ecosystem/dx_check_receipt.rs` and proves stale output names `webassembly-bridge-check-panel-source` / `check_panel_source` without blaming lower dx-check, generated starter, runbook, materializer, dashboard, or runtime helper files. Upstream source re-inspected from `G:/WWW/inspirations/wasm-bindgen`: `Cargo.toml`, `src/lib.rs`, `crates/cli/Cargo.toml`, and `crates/cli/tests/reference/targets-target-web.js`; public APIs used remain `#[wasm_bindgen]`, `wasm_bindgen::prelude::*`, `JsValue`, `Closure`, `default init(input)`, `initSync(input)`, `WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming`, and `WebAssembly.instantiate`. Guarded with red/green focused `node --test .\benchmarks\wasm-bindgen-receipt-hash-refresh.test.ts --test-name-pattern "check-panel-only stale source drift"` and helper `--check --json` / `--write`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Rust crate compilation, wasm-bindgen CLI execution, generated-Wasm browser proof, and security/performance review were skipped by policy. Next action: expose these stale surface arrays in the Rust dx-check/check-panel package-lane row.

- Added the dx-style official-starter PostCSS replacement canary contract. `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/postcss_compat.rs` and `related-crates/style/fixtures/postcss-compat-matrix.json` now define a code-backed compatibility matrix and Rust-native transforms for local CSS import flattening, nesting, custom media, media min/max syntax including bounded ranges, safe custom selectors, simple `:is()`/`:where()` lowering, custom-property and `env()` fallbacks, logical property fallbacks, expanded vendor-prefix canaries, selector/color/gradient/page-break/flex-grid compatibility diagnostics, and source-origin/source-map receipts without requiring PostCSS or Autoprefixer at runtime. `dx style build` / `dx style check` publish the selected browser target, supported/partial counts, the scoped `dx_starter_replacement_score` canary field, `full_postcss_plugin_parity: false`, unsupported warnings, and no-local-PostCSS proof; lower dx-check and the check-panel surface those fields for Forge, Zed, and Studio. That canary field is not a current product-path full-score claim. Public wording is scoped to "PostCSS replacement for DX starters" until arbitrary plugin and Autoprefixer parity are proven by equal-output tests. Verification: green targeted dx-style compatibility Rust canaries, green PostCSS compatibility source/JSON guard, green focused launch/check-panel source guards, touched-file rustfmt, and dx-check source guard coverage. A broad `dx-www-compiler` Cargo proof for the new dx-check receipt path remains skipped/timed out on this busy machine, so no full compiled dx-check proof is claimed for that path. Skipped by policy: full builds, broad suites, servers, package installs, deploys, live PostCSS/Autoprefixer execution, arbitrary plugin/config parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking. Next action: add approved equal-output browser fixtures against real PostCSS/Autoprefixer baselines before claiming broader ecosystem parity.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.2 font-feature utility support. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for documented `font-features-*` classes without Tailwind or PostCSS: safe arbitrary OpenType feature lists such as `font-features-['smcp','onum']` generate `font-feature-settings`, custom-property aliases such as `font-features-(--dx-font-features)` generate `var(...)`, and unsafe arbitrary values fail closed. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records both canaries under `tailwind-v4.2-font-features`, and the compatibility matrix calls this measured typography support rather than universal Tailwind typography config/plugin parity. Guarded with a red/green focused source test, touched-file rustfmt, `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan. The targeted Cargo utility test printed `1 passed` but still hit the 90s command timeout after the result, so no clean Cargo exit proof is claimed. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: add a receipt-backed stacked/compound `@variant` CSS canary or continue the official Tailwind docs-table walk with the next missing utility family.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 container-size utility support. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for `@container`, named `@container/main`, `@container-size`, and named `@container-size/main` without Tailwind or PostCSS; safe names emit `container-name`, while unsafe names fail closed. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records `@container-size` and `@container-size/main` as supported canaries, and the compatibility matrix calls this measured container marker support rather than universal Tailwind container-query config/plugin parity. Guarded with a red/green focused source test, direct Node source guard, touched-file rustfmt, `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan. The root `dx run` wrapper timed out after formatting and the targeted Cargo utility test timed out on the busy machine; lingering Cargo processes were stopped, so no fresh wrapper or Rust compile proof is claimed for this pass. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: add Tailwind v4.2 `font-features-*` utilities or a receipt-backed stacked/compound `@variant` CSS canary.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 typography detail utilities. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for overflow-wrap helpers (`wrap-normal`, `wrap-break-word`, `wrap-anywhere`), text-indent helpers (`indent-*`, negative `-indent-*`, arbitrary values, and CSS custom-property aliases), vertical-align helpers (`align-middle` plus safe arbitrary/custom-property aliases), text-decoration-thickness helpers (`decoration-auto`, `decoration-from-font`, numeric values, arbitrary values, and typed custom-property aliases), and text-underline-offset helpers (`underline-offset-*`, negative offsets, arbitrary values, and CSS custom-property aliases) without Tailwind or PostCSS. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records `wrap-anywhere`, `indent-8`, `align-middle`, `decoration-4`, and `underline-offset-4` as supported canaries, and the compatibility matrix keeps this scoped to measured subset support instead of full Tailwind parity. Guarded with a red/green focused source test, root `dx run` wrapper guard, touched-file rustfmt, targeted Rust utility test, `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; the broader parity receipt Rust test timed out and was stopped, so no fresh parity compile proof is claimed for that path. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: keep walking the Tailwind v4.3 docs table and promote the next unsupported family into generated-output receipts.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 interactivity additions. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for `scheme-*` color-scheme helpers, scrollbar width/gutter/color helpers (`scrollbar-thin`, `scrollbar-gutter-both`, `scrollbar-thumb-red-500`, `scrollbar-track-slate-200`), `zoom-*`, and `tab-*` utilities without Tailwind or PostCSS. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records representative canaries, and the compatibility matrix now calls this measured subset support instead of universal Tailwind parity. Guarded with a red/green focused source test, root `dx run` wrapper guard, touched-file rustfmt, green targeted Rust utility test, `git diff --check` with CRLF warnings only, and conflict-marker scan; the focused parity receipt Rust test timed out and was stopped, so receipt compile proof is not claimed for that path. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: keep walking the Tailwind v4.3 docs table and promote the next unsupported family into generated-output receipts.

- Added dx-style Tailwind v4.3 logical sizing support. The Rust utility engine now generates normal CSS for `inline-*`, `min-inline-*`, `max-inline-*`, `block-*`, `min-block-*`, and `max-block-*` utilities, including fractions, container scales, arbitrary values, viewport units, and custom-property aliases such as `inline-(--dx-inline-size)`. The sizing value resolver now uses the target axis for `screen`, so width-like utilities emit `100vw` while height/block-like utilities emit `100vh`. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt now includes `inline-1/2`, `inline-(--dx-inline-size)`, and `block-screen` as supported canaries, and the compatibility matrix records this as measured subset support rather than universal Tailwind parity. Guarded with a red/green focused source test; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: keep walking the Tailwind v4.3 docs table and add the next unsupported utility family with generated-output evidence.

- Added dx-style rule metadata consumption to lower dx-check for RUN 12. The style receipt reader now summarizes `style_rule_metadata` rows as class-to-rule evidence with visual property buckets, source files, token references, declaration counts, and Zed/Studio editability; the lower `dx-style` check emits `dx_style_rule_metadata_*` metrics; and the check-panel exposes a `dx-style-rule-metadata` style evidence row with `dx-style:rule-metadata` visibility. This makes the RUN 11 metadata readable by dx-check, Zed, and Studio without scraping generated CSS or shelling out to Tailwind/PostCSS. Guarded with a red/green focused source test; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, generated-app materialization, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, universal Tailwind config/plugin parity, full Autoprefixer parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. Next action: wire the launch-template read model and Studio marker contract to display the rule-metadata row.

- Added dx-style style rule metadata receipts for RUN 11. `dx style build` and `dx style check` now publish `style_rule_metadata_count` and `style_rule_metadata` rows beside the normal generated CSS receipt, mapping each supported generated class to its selector, generated CSS, parsed declarations, visual property categories, source files, source origin, CSS token references, and `zed_studio_editable: true`. This gives Check/Zed/Studio a source-owned visual editing read model without Tailwind/PostCSS, without scraping generated CSS, and without claiming live browser editing or universal Tailwind parity. Guarded with red/green focused source coverage; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, generated-app materialization, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, universal Tailwind config/plugin parity, and fair speed benchmarking remain skipped by policy. - Added the dx-style equal-output canary for RUN 10. `related-crates/style/fixtures/tailwind-equal-output-canary.json` and `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/equal_output.rs` now define a tiny checked declaration-equality seed for `block`, `flex`, `grid`, `p-4`, `mt-2`, and `opacity-50`, exported as `style::core::tailwind_equal_output_canary_contract()`. `dx style build` and `dx style check` now publish `tailwind_equal_output_canary_contract`; lower dx-check reads it, emits receipt/contract/schema/class/equal-output/live-Tailwind/fair-speed metrics, and blocks overclaims when fair speed benchmarking is asserted without live Tailwind execution; and the check-panel exposes a `dx-style-tailwind-equal-output` row for Forge/Zed/Studio. This is not live Tailwind execution, not a fair speed benchmark, and not full Tailwind parity. - Added dx-style package-scoped ownership evidence for RUN 8. Preview-manifest metadata now publishes `stylePackageOwnershipRows` for Forge-owned style surfaces, starting with `shadcn/ui/button` and `animation/motion`, including package id, style scope, required tokens, generated class ownership, unsupported class warnings by package, token source, generated CSS dependency, receipt path, Zed visibility, and runtime-proof status. The new `examples/template/preview-style-package-ownership-read-model.ts` consumes preview/readiness metadata without scraping HTML or raw style receipts, and the launch shell, Studio edit contract, Rust Studio manifest, and materializer expose package-ownership marker names for Zed/Studio consumers. - Added DX Build proof action IDs. Readiness-gate reports and consumer snapshots now expose `proofs.requiredActionIds` beside proof blockers, mirroring the canonical `requiredActions` IDs so DX CLI, Friday, and Zed can bind proof blockers to governed action buttons without prose inference. - Added DX Build proof blocker action mapping. Readiness-gate `proofs.blockerActions` maps each canonical proof blocker to the governed action ids available in the current gate state, including product-seal-only blockers mapping to `refresh-source-build-receipts`. - Added a DX Build last-mile product-seal action. If installed-default smoke and governed runtime proof are already green while `dx.build.readiness.product_ready` remains false, the readiness gate now emits `refresh-source-build-receipts` with `dx build` and next-action text to refresh DX Build readiness instead of leaving operators with a blocked product gate and no required action. Guarded with red/green readiness-gate coverage; no release rebuild, server, install, deploy, binary promotion, or runtime proof ran.

- Fixed the DX Build candidate-smoke action gap. - Tightened DX Build product-proof blocker wording. - Added dx-style `break-inside-avoid` browser-compat CSS support. The utility now emits `page-break-inside: avoid` beside standard `break-inside: avoid` in normal generated CSS, giving the PostCSS-free launch path a real print/layout fragmentation canary. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records `break-inside-avoid` as supported, the `tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json` fixture and `browser_compat.rs` contract now carry six declaration canaries, and dx-check/check-panel fixture counts moved to `6`. - Added dx-style backface browser-prefix CSS support. `backface-hidden` and `backface-visible` now emit prefixed plus standard `backface-visibility` declarations in normal generated CSS, and the engine-level source guard proves `backface-hidden` through `StyleEngine::empty().css_for_class(...)`. The generated-output Tailwind parity receipt records `backface-hidden` as supported, the `tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json` fixture and `browser_compat.rs` contract now carry five declaration canaries, and dx-check/check-panel fixture counts moved to `5`. Next action: continue RUN 7 with another measured browser-prefix/autoprefixer canary.

- Added dx-style grouped classname expansion. DX-WWW now expands compact grouped syntax such as `hover:(bg-accent text-accent-foreground shadow-sm)`, `md:(grid grid-cols-2 gap-4)`, `dark:hover:(bg-card text-foreground)`, and `group-hover:(opacity-100 translate-y-0)` in both the parser and public CLI scanner before handing tokens to the Rust CSS generator. Token-style parenthesized utilities like `bg-token(surface)` and `bg-size-(--dx-bg-size)` stay intact, static helper calls no longer truncate at grouped `)`, and invalid unclosed groups produce `dx-grouping-error:*` diagnostics instead of silent miscompiles. Guarded with red/green focused grouped source tests, green combined token/grouped source guard, green root `..\dx run --check`, and touched-file rustfmt; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full arbitrary variant grammar, full theme/config/plugin parity, full Autoprefixer parity, fair equal-output Tailwind benchmarking, and generated-app materialization were skipped by policy. Next action: RUN 6 should close another PostCSS/Autoprefixer browser-prefix evidence gap and publish it through dx-check/Forge/Zed-readable receipts.

- Added dx-style token-aware classname generation. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for `bg-token(surface)`, `text-token(foreground)`, `border-token(border)`, and `ring-token(ring)` using theme CSS variables, rejects unknown token names by returning unsupported, and records the token canaries in the generated-output parity receipt. The style parser and public CLI scanner now preserve parenthesized class tokens, and the official launch/default theme includes `--surface` for the documented surface token. Guarded with red/green focused source tests, combined token/parity source guard, `dx run --check`, touched-file rustfmt, and source marker scan; focused Cargo tests were attempted but timed out on the busy machine, so no Rust compile proof is claimed. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Tailwind config/plugin/theme parity, and fair equal-output benchmarking were skipped. Next action: continue RUN 5 with grouped classname expansion plus invalid-group and unknown-token diagnostics.

- Added a lock-backed Automation Connectors App Router dry-run route for Lane 5. `automations/n8n` now materializes `app/api/automations/n8n/dry-run/route.ts` into the default template and Forge cache, exposing local readiness/catalog data plus redacted DX/Zed dry-run receipts while keeping `runtimeExecution: false` and returning `missing-config` for live run requests without provider credentials. The Automation Connectors package-reality score moved from 76 to 80, and the route is tracked in the source manifest, package lock, cache manifest, package-add receipt, safety archive, docs, status JSON, and typed read model. Guarded with red/green Lane 5 package reality tests, Forge package lock checks, package receipt freshness checks, shared Forge/template guards, `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; no build, server, package install, deploy, browser proof, live n8n call, or webhook registration ran.

- Added a DX Build product-proof summary to readiness-gate reports and consumer snapshots. The new `proofs` block gives DX CLI, Friday, and Zed direct fields for source readiness, readiness-product intent, installed smoke pass state, installed smoke binary role, installed-default proof, runtime-proof approval, final product readiness, and canonical proof blockers. The live hub gate now publishes those fields while staying source `100/100` and product `82/100` because the installed-default smoke has not passed and runtime proof is not approved.

- Expanded the DX Build readiness-gate quality receipt to cover installed-smoke source modules. Live hub receipts now prove both the gate and the smoke command stay within the small-module boundary, with max line count 165, while still blocking product readiness on the stale installed release binary.

- Fixed the readiness-gate installed-smoke action command for hub consumers. When the gate is written for `G:\Dx` from DX-WWW source receipts, required actions now use `node www/tools/build/dx-build-installed-smoke.ts ...`, so Friday/DX CLI/Zed can run the displayed command from the hub instead of getting a www-local path that does not exist at `G:\Dx`.

- Split the DX Build installed-smoke harness into small source-owned modules. The entrypoint now delegates to `tools/build/installed-smoke/` modules for args, binary provenance, fixture creation, IO, runner, report assembly, and constants, with a source guard keeping each module under 180 lines. Guarded with `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts` and `node --check` on the split modules; live default smoke still fails only because the installed release binary is stale, not because of the refactor.

- Hardened DX Build product proof. The readiness gate now consumes `dx check launch --json` as `receipts.checkLaunch`, requires approved launch proof before product readiness, and rejects passing smoke from candidate override binaries as installed-release proof. The installed-smoke receipt now records `binaryRole`, `binaryDefault`, and `binaryOverride`; live default installed smoke remains stale with 11 blockers, while the debug candidate passes but is labeled `candidate-override`. Guarded with red/green installed-smoke and readiness-gate Node tests; no release rebuild, server, install, deploy, or binary promotion ran.

- Completed Lane 5 Forge package reality for Payments, AI SDK, and Automation Connectors.
