- Added Lane 5 CSS pipeline retained-import evidence. The source-owned CSS adapter now reports retained imports with reasons across the manifest, receipt summary, graph style node, graph consumer snapshot, and Zed handoff. External/rooted imports are labeled `external-or-rooted`, media/conditional imports are labeled `conditional-or-media`, and unresolved local imports remain visible instead of being flattened or hidden. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-css-pipeline-boundary.test.cjs`, targeted rustfmt, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; focused Cargo for the retained-import unit test timed out after 124 seconds in the shared dirty compile state.

- Added dx-style package-panel drift markers to the check/Studio surfaces. `launch-shell.tsx` and the static `/launch` fallback now emit `data-dx-style-package-panel`, `data-dx-style-package-panel-read-model`, drift-state/status, mismatch-field, and readiness-receipt markers for the browser-compat package panel. The Studio edit contract, Rust Studio manifest, and launch materializer advertise the same marker vocabulary, and the materializer records the combined package-panel drift read-model schema on the dx-style drift fixture. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "package panel drift markers"`, full focused `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts`, `dx run --check` on touched TS/TSX files, and `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check dx-www\src\cli\studio_manifest.rs`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Autoprefixer parity testing, generated-app materialization, and broad Cargo checks were skipped by policy. Next action: hydrate the visible package panel from `dxStylePackagePanelFromPreviewAndReadiness()` when preview/readiness receipts are available.

- Added Lane 5 CSS pipeline local `url()` import flattening. The source-owned CSS import adapter now recognizes unconditional local `@import url("../local.css");` statements, flattens them into generated CSS, records them in `flattened_imports`, and carries them through source-map source counts. External URL imports and media/conditional imports are intentionally retained instead of flattened so DX does not overclaim LightningCSS/Turbopack CSS parity. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-css-pipeline-boundary.test.cjs`, targeted rustfmt, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; focused Cargo is blocked before Lane 5 by current shared compile errors in `module_resolver_config.rs` and `studio_manifest.rs`.

- Added a combined dx-style package-panel drift consumer. `examples/template/preview-style-package-panel-read-model.ts` now exports `dxStylePackagePanelFromPreviewAndReadiness()`, joining preview-manifest style evidence with launch readiness drift-fixture metadata into one Studio/Zed package-panel read model. The panel reports drift exercise state, source-guarded or mismatch status, loader/helper paths, allowed states, mismatch fields, and readiness-receipt usage while still refusing static HTML, raw style receipt, and dx-check receipt reads. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "package panel consumes drift"`, full focused `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts`, and `dx run --check` on touched TS files; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Autoprefixer parity testing, and generated-app materialization were skipped by policy. Next action: render this combined read model in the visible dx-check/Studio package-panel surface.

- Added Lane 5 CSS pipeline source-map source-count evidence. The source-owned CSS adapter now records `source_map_source_count` per style, aggregates `css_source_map_sources` in the build receipt summary, mirrors `sourceMapSourceCount` into the graph consumer snapshot, and exposes `source_map_source_count` in the Zed handoff style summary so consumers can trust DX receipts without parsing `.css.map` bodies. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-css-pipeline-boundary.test.cjs`, targeted rustfmt, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; the focused Cargo source-build test is still blocked by the unrelated `ecmascript_analysis.rs` E0382 compile error from another lane.

- Added Lane 5 CSS pipeline source-map and flattened-import evidence. The DX-owned source CSS adapter now records local imports flattened into generated CSS, writes adjacent `.css.map` evidence files with dx-style ownership metadata, reports source-map and flattened-import counts through source-build receipts, graph snapshots, and Zed handoff, and records Turbopack CSS provenance as reference-only. This does not claim full Lightning CSS parity, exact source-map segment mapping, or Turbopack as public DX architecture. Verification so far: red focused source-build CSS test, targeted rustfmt, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan; final focused Cargo proof is blocked by concurrent source-engine lane compile errors/lock contention outside Lane 5.

- Added dx-style drift-fixture marker emission to the launch check surfaces. `examples/template/launch-shell.tsx`, `examples/template/runtime-pages/index.html`, and `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` now expose the `data-dx-check-style-evidence-drift*` marker set for the browser-compat evidence row, including source-guarded state, loader/helper pointers, and allowed drift states. The launch materializer also carries root and `/launch` `styleEvidenceDriftFixtures` metadata so the typed preview/readiness read model can meet real surface metadata instead of relying on contract discovery alone. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style drift fixture markers are emitted"`, full focused `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts`, and `dx run --check` on touched TS/TSX files; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Autoprefixer parity testing, and generated-app materialization were skipped by policy. Next action: connect the preview/readiness drift read model to a real dx-check/Studio package-panel consumer.

- Added the default WWW template architecture contract. `dx new` now writes `dx.www.default_template.architecture_contract` metadata into the generated template manifest, readiness receipt, standalone `.dx/forge/template-readiness/zed-template-handoff.json`, and readiness bundle, and `dx templates --json` reports the same contract from `dx-www/src/cli/default_template_contract.rs`. The launch readiness-bundle consumer now reads the standalone Zed handoff first and exposes the contract fields in JSON, terminal, and Markdown output, proving DX-WWW is the runtime foundation while React/RSC/Node/NAPI, `node_modules`, and external framework clone parity remain false. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, and deploys were skipped by policy.

- Added dx-style drift-fixture marker discovery to Studio contracts. `examples/template/dx-studio-edit-contract.ts` and `dx-www/src/cli/studio_manifest.rs` now list the `data-dx-check-style-evidence-drift*` marker vocabulary for the dx-check health panel, giving Zed/Studio a source-owned way to discover drift evidence markers before looking at preview/readiness metadata. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style drift fixture markers"` and the full focused drift-fixture consumer guard; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Autoprefixer parity testing, generated-app materialization, and broad Cargo checks were skipped by policy. Next action: emit the same drift markers from LaunchShell/static dx-check panel markup so the marker contract becomes visible in generated HTML.

- Added a dx-style drift-fixture package-panel read model. `examples/template/preview-style-package-panel-read-model.ts` now exports `dxStyleDriftFixtureFromPreviewAndReadiness()` for Studio/Zed consumers that need to compare preview-manifest `styleEvidenceDriftFixtures` with launch readiness `style_evidence_drift_fixture` metadata. The consumer normalizes camelCase and snake_case fields, reports `missing`, `preview-only`, `readiness-only`, `source-guarded`, or `mismatch`, lists mismatch fields, and explicitly avoids static HTML or raw style receipt scraping. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-drift-fixture-consumer.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full Autoprefixer parity testing, generated-app materialization, and broad Cargo checks were skipped by policy. Next action: wire this read model into LaunchShell/Studio marker consumers and keep adding generated-output/browser-prefix canaries before making broader dx-style replacement claims.

- Added the source-owned installed-binary smoke receipt contract for `dx build`. `tools/build/dx-build-installed-smoke.ts` creates a tiny TSX/CSS/assets fixture, verifies `dx build --help` is read-only, runs the installed `dx-www` build command, checks `.dx/build/manifest.json` for `source_build_css_*`, checks `.dx/receipts/build/zed-handoff.json` for `style_optimization`, `build_readiness`, and `installed_binary_smoke_receipt`, checks `.dx/receipts/build/readiness.json` for source/product scores plus the canonical installed-smoke receipt pointer, and can persist `dx.build.installedBinarySmoke` with `--receipt` without installing packages or starting a server. The guard uses a fake current binary plus a source scan for the Rust readiness/handoff contract to prove the current source behavior without a governed binary rebuild. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-installed-smoke.test.ts`, targeted `rustfmt`, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan. Next action: governed binary refresh, then rerun the installed-binary smoke against `G:\Dx\bin\dx-www.exe` until it writes a passing receipt.

- Restored the source-owned DX Build readiness gate. `tools/build/dx-build-readiness-gate.ts` now delegates to small modules under `tools/build/readiness-gate/` for CLI parsing, receipt discovery, schema/pointer checks, required-action rows, quality reporting, gate report creation, and consumer snapshot creation. The gate reads DX-WWW workspace readiness/Zed handoff receipts, keeps installed-binary smoke as hub-root release proof, validates the canonical installed-smoke pointer contract, and writes `dx.build.readinessGate` plus `dx.build.readinessGate.consumerSnapshot` receipts behind explicit write flags without running builds, binaries, installs, servers, or Markdown scraping. Current receipts expose `requiredActions` with command, risk, approval, receipt-write, policy, and explicit DX CLI/DX-WWW/Zed/Friday consumer metadata. Verification: red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-readiness-gate.test.ts`; live hub refresh is currently blocked by missing/stale receipts and the failing installed-binary smoke, not by missing gate source.

- Added dx-style state-alias support to preview-manifest evidence. The launch materializer now writes `tailwindParityStateAliasSupportedClassCount` and `tailwindParitySupportedStateAliasExamples` into `DX_STYLE_BROWSER_COMPAT_PREVIEW_EVIDENCE`, and `preview-style-evidence-read-model.ts` returns those fields from `/launch` route metadata or the root preview-manifest row with missing-state defaults of `0` and `[]`. The read model remains a pure Zed/Studio consumer and does not scrape static HTML or raw style receipts. Guard target: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style preview manifest evidence has a typed read model for Zed and Studio"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, generated-app materialization, and broad Cargo checks remain skipped by policy. Next action: add a Zed/Studio package-panel consumer for these preview-manifest state-alias fields.

- Added dx-style state-alias pre-hydration evidence markers. `launch-shell.tsx` now renders `data-dx-check-style-evidence-state-alias-count` and `data-dx-check-style-evidence-state-alias-examples` from `style_evidence_rows`, and the fallback template keeps missing receipts honest with `0` and an empty examples marker. The checked-in static `/launch` HTML, Studio edit contract, Studio manifest source markers, and launch materializer marker lists now advertise the same contract for Zed/Studio readers before React hydration. Guard target: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "static launch check panel exposes dx-style browser compat fallback evidence markers"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks remain skipped by policy. Next action: add the same state-alias count/examples to preview-manifest `styleEvidenceRows` and its typed read model.

- Added dx-style state-alias support to the launch-template typed style-evidence read model. `dx-check-style-evidence-read-model.ts` now consumes the check-panel `tailwind_parity_state_alias_supported_class_count`, `tailwind_parity_supported_state_alias_examples`, and `dx_style_tailwind_parity_state_alias_supported_classes` metric, then exposes camelCase normalized fields for Studio/Zed consumers while preserving missing-state defaults. Guard target: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style browser compat evidence is consumed through the dx-check panel read model"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks remain skipped by policy. Next action: expose these state-alias fields as launch-shell/static data markers for pre-hydration Studio/Zed reads.

- Added dx-style state-alias support to the higher check-panel style evidence row. The `dx-style-browser-compat` `style_evidence_rows` entry now mirrors `dx_style_tailwind_parity_state_alias_supported_classes`, publishes `tailwind_parity_state_alias_supported_class_count`, and carries `tailwind_parity_supported_state_alias_examples` for `target:p-4`, `read-only:bg-blue-500`, `indeterminate:opacity-100`, `has-even:bg-blue-500`, `not-visited:text-slate-900`, and `in-read-only:p-4`. This makes the evidence visible to Forge/Zed/check-panel consumers without raw style receipt reads, while still refusing full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar or full PostCSS/autoprefixer parity claims. Guard target: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-check receipt panel exposes dx-style browser compat evidence row"` and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler dx_check_panel_exposes_style_browser_compat_evidence_row --lib`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks remain skipped by policy. Next action: update the launch-template typed style-evidence read model to consume these state-alias fields.

- Added a dx-style state alias dx-check parity summary. Lower `dx-check` now reads `tailwind_parity_receipt_contract.entries` from `.dx/receipts/style/check.json`, counts supported state-alias canaries through `dx_style_tailwind_parity_state_alias_supported_classes`, and keeps the examples on `supported_state_alias_examples` for Forge/Zed/check consumers. The summarized examples are `target:p-4`, `read-only:bg-blue-500`, `indeterminate:opacity-100`, `has-even:bg-blue-500`, `not-visited:text-slate-900`, and `in-read-only:p-4`; this does not claim full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar or full PostCSS/autoprefixer parity. Guard target: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-tools.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "core dx-check summarizes dx-style Tailwind parity receipt evidence"` and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler dx_style_section_summarizes_tailwind_parity_receipt --lib`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks remain skipped by policy. Next action: surface the same state-alias dx-check parity summary in the higher check-panel/Zed style evidence row.

- Added dx-style state alias generated-output parity receipt coverage. The Tailwind parity receipt fixture now runs `target:p-4`, `read-only:bg-blue-500`, `indeterminate:opacity-100`, `has-even:bg-blue-500`, `not-visited:text-slate-900`, and `in-read-only:p-4` through the real `StyleEngine` and records their generated normal CSS in the receipt consumed by `dx style build` / `dx style check`. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style Tailwind parity receipt records state alias generated CSS"` and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-style parity_receipt_records_common_state_alias_generated_css --lib`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks were skipped by policy. Next action: expose these supported state-alias receipt entries in lower `dx-check` summaries for Forge/Zed.

- Added a source guard and CLI dispatch fix for `dx build --help`. The source dispatcher now handles help before `cmd_build()` can run, and the dedicated help text names the source-owned build engine, emitted receipts, graph snapshot, Zed handoff, and no-`node_modules` contract. This came from the installed-binary smoke finding that the current `G:\Dx\bin\dx-www.exe` still treats `build --help` as a real build and is stale relative to source: its emitted manifest lacks `source_build_css_*`, and its Zed handoff lacks `style_optimization`. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-cli-help.test.ts`. No heavy rebuild, server, package install, deploy, or binary refresh was run; the governed binary refresh plus tiny fixture smoke remains the product/runtime 100/100 gate.

- Added dx-style direct and conditional state alias coverage. The Rust style engine now registers common Tailwind state aliases for direct variants (`target:*`, `read-only:*`, `indeterminate:*`) and for conditional selector composition through `has-*`, `not-*`, and `in-*` (`has-even:*`, `not-visited:*`, `in-read-only:*`). The shared alias set now covers even, visited, target, autofill, read-only/read-write, indeterminate, default, in-range, and out-of-range without claiming full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style has/not/in state aliases cover common Tailwind selectors"`, `dx run --check .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts`, and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-style` filters for `empty_engine_supports_common_form_and_location_state_variants`, `has_even_variant_maps_to_even_selector`, `not_visited_variant_maps_to_visited_selector`, and `in_read_only_variant_maps_to_read_only_ancestor_selector`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Cargo checks were skipped by policy. Next action: add generated-output parity fixtures for these aliases so receipts prove emitted CSS text.

- Added Zed-facing CSS optimization summary to `dx.build.zedHandoff`. The source-build handoff now includes a compact `style_optimization` object derived from the source-build manifest, so future Zed preview and DX CLI/TUI consumers can read style node count, original/retained/pruned rule totals, and minified style count from `.dx/receipts/build/zed-handoff.json` without opening the full graph receipt or treating Turbopack as public DX infrastructure. Guarded with a red/green focused source-build handoff test, full source-build fixture, and green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, installed-binary refresh, live hydration proof, and full Lightning CSS parity were skipped by policy. Current `dx.build.graph` / source-build graph wiring status: 100/100 source-ready; product/runtime is capped at 82/100 until governed installed-binary/runtime smoke and live parity proof run.

- Added build-receipt CSS optimizer summary totals and boolean JSX attribute lowering for source-owned `dx build`. `SourceBuildSummary` now exposes `css_original_rules`, `css_retained_rules`, `css_pruned_rules`, and `css_minified_styles`; the public build manifest emitted by `cmd_build` mirrors those totals as `source_build_css_*` fields so DX CLI/TUI/Zed can read optimizer status without walking every style node. The TSX runtime transform now lowers boolean JSX attributes such as `<button disabled>` to `disabled: true` in source-owned browser ESM. Guarded with red/green `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_writes_receipt_for_tsx_css_assets --test source_build_engine`, red/green `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli transforms_boolean_jsx_attributes --lib`, focused `module_runtime_transform` and `module_tsx_component_runtime` Cargo checks, full source-build fixture, and green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`. Current `dx build` lane status: 99/100 source-ready; remaining gates are governed installed-binary/runtime smoke, live hydration proof, and full bundler/CSS parity validation.

- Added Studio dx-style style-evidence marker coverage. `examples/template/dx-studio-edit-contract.ts` now lists the dx-check style-evidence marker vocabulary for the `dx-check-health-panel` editable surface, aligning Studio/Zed edit-contract discovery with the launch shell, Studio manifest, and preview-manifest read model. The markers expose dx-style browser-compat status, receipt path, fixture path, Zed visibility, class count, and explicit non-parity flags without reading `.dx/receipts/style/check.json` directly. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx studio edit contract advertises dx-style style evidence markers"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and generated-app materialization were skipped by policy. Next action: refresh the generated preview-manifest fixture in an approved materialization window.

- Added compact CSS optimization totals to the `dx.build.graph.consumerSnapshot` contract. Both `tools/build-graph/reader.ts` and the Rust source-build graph snapshot now expose `graph.styleOptimization` with style node count, original/retained/pruned rule totals, and minified style count, so DX CLI, DX-WWW, and future Zed preview can render CSS optimization state without opening every graph node. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts` and `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_writes_receipt_for_tsx_css_assets --test source_build_engine`. Full builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, installed-binary refresh, live hydration proof, and full Lightning CSS parity were skipped by policy. Current `dx build` lane status: 99/100 source-ready; the remaining point is governed installed-binary/runtime smoke and broader runtime parity proof, not missing source graph wiring.

- Added a dx-style browser-compat preview-manifest read model. `examples/template/preview-style-evidence-read-model.ts` now consumes generated `public/preview-manifest.json` `styleEvidenceRows` for Zed/Studio without reading HTML or `.dx/receipts/style/check.json` directly. It prefers `/launch` route metadata, falls back to the root manifest row, returns an honest missing state when no row exists, and keeps full Autoprefixer plus Tailwind/PostCSS output parity false unless the manifest proves otherwise. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style preview manifest evidence has a typed read model for Zed and Studio"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and generated-app materialization were skipped by policy. Next action: wire this read model into the Zed/Studio package panel after refreshing the generated preview-manifest fixture.

- Added dx-style browser-compat evidence to preview-manifest materialization. `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` now emits a structured `styleEvidenceRows` entry for `dx-style-browser-compat` both at the root `public/preview-manifest.json` contract and on the `/launch` route metadata. The entry carries `.dx/receipts/style/check.json`, `related-crates/style/fixtures/tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json`, `dx-style:browser-compat`, canary class count `0`, and explicit false full-Autoprefixer/full-Tailwind-PostCSS parity flags, so Zed/Studio can read the same missing-state truth from manifest metadata without scraping HTML. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "launch preview manifest materializer carries dx-style browser compat evidence metadata"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and materializer execution against a generated app were skipped by policy. Next action: run the lightweight materializer in an approved fixture-refresh window and confirm generated `public/preview-manifest.json` includes `styleEvidenceRows`.

- Added static dx-style browser-compat fallback evidence to the launch check panel. `launch-shell.tsx` now falls back to a typed missing-state `dx-style-browser-compat` row when `style_evidence_rows` has not been loaded, and `runtime-pages/launch.html` mirrors the same `data-dx-check-style-evidence-*` markers for pre-hydration Zed/Studio consumers. The row points at `.dx/receipts/style/check.json` and `related-crates/style/fixtures/tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json`, keeps class count at `0`, and explicitly does not claim full Autoprefixer or Tailwind/PostCSS output parity. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "static launch check panel exposes dx-style browser compat fallback evidence markers"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and generated runtime rematerialization were skipped by policy. Next action: run the launch materializer in an approved window so generated preview-manifest metadata carries the same marker contract from source.

- Added source-owned CSS pruning and minification to `dx build`. The build engine now collects reachable class names from app/components/pages/src sources through `css_usage.rs`, flattens local quoted imports through `css_imports.rs`, prunes unreachable simple class rules through `css_optimizer.rs`, preserves class rules when static usage is dynamic/unknown, minifies the emitted CSS, and records `original_rule_count`, `retained_rule_count`, `pruned_rule_count`, and `minified` in both the source-build manifest and `dx.build.graph` style nodes. Guarded with red/green `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_writes_receipt_for_tsx_css_assets --test source_build_engine`, focused `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli css_optimizer --lib`, full source-build fixture `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine --test source_build_engine`, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`, and targeted rustfmt on touched files. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live hydration proof, installed-binary refresh, and full Lightning CSS parity were skipped by policy. Current `dx build` lane status: 98/100 source-ready; next action is governed installed-binary/runtime smoke plus broader TSX/runtime/CSS parity work.

- Added a dx-style browser-compat check-panel consumer. The launch template now includes `examples/template/dx-check-style-evidence-read-model.ts`, a small Zed/Studio read model that consumes `check_panel.view_model.style_evidence_rows` and normalizes the `dx-style-browser-compat` row without opening `.dx/receipts/style/check.json` directly. `launch-shell.tsx` renders `data-dx-check-style-evidence-*` markers for status, receipt path, fixture path, canary class count, Zed visibility, and full-parity truth flags; `dx-www/src/cli/studio_manifest.rs` and `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` advertise the same marker vocabulary. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style browser compat evidence is consumed through the dx-check panel read model"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and generated static runtime rematerialization were skipped by policy. Next action: rematerialize the governed launch runtime in an approved window so `runtime-pages/launch.html` and `public/preview-manifest.json` carry these markers from source.

- Added the dx-style browser-compat receipt panel row. The DX-WWW check panel reader now parses top-level dx-check sections, mirrors the lower dx-style browser-compat metrics into `style_evidence_rows`, and exposes a `dx-style-browser-compat` row with `.dx/receipts/style/check.json`, `related-crates/style/fixtures/tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json`, receipt/contract/schema/class-count metrics, honest `full_autoprefixer_parity` and `full_tailwind_postcss_output_parity` flags, and `zed_visibility: "dx-style:browser-compat"`. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-check receipt panel exposes dx-style browser compat evidence row"` and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler dx_check_panel_exposes_style_browser_compat_evidence_row --lib` after one compile warmup timeout; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, full autoprefixer parity testing, and broad Forge/Zed runtime UI checks were skipped by policy. Next action: add a package/Zed consumer test that reads `style_evidence_rows` from the check panel receipt instead of raw style receipts.

- Added dx-style browser-compat dx-check metrics. Lower `dx-check` now reads `browser_compat_receipt_contract` from `.dx/receipts/style/check.json` through the shared dx-style receipt reader and emits metrics for receipt presence, contract presence, schema support, browser-prefix canary class count, full Autoprefixer parity, and full Tailwind/PostCSS output parity. The check also warns when the browser-compat contract is missing, the schema is unsupported, or a receipt overclaims full browser/PostCSS parity before a measured equal-output proof gate exists. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-check consumes dx-style browser compat receipt metrics"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, and full autoprefixer parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: surface the browser-compat dx-check metrics in Forge/Zed receipt panels.

- Added dx-style browser-compat receipt visibility. `dx style build` and `dx style check` now include `browser_compat_receipt_contract` beside the generated-output Tailwind parity receipt, carrying the canary schema, fixture path, Tailwind/PostCSS baseline, declaration-fragment comparison scope, covered classes, run policy, and explicit `full_autoprefixer_parity: false` / `full_tailwind_postcss_output_parity: false` flags. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-www style receipts expose browser compat evidence to Check and Zed"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, and full autoprefixer parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: make lower `dx-check` consume this receipt contract and emit browser-compat metrics.

- Added a dx-style browser-compat source contract. `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/browser_compat.rs` now exposes the Tailwind/PostCSS browser-prefix canary as a source-owned Rust contract with schema, fixture path, Tailwind/PostCSS baseline, declaration-fragment comparison scope, launch run policy, and the covered classes `appearance-none`, `select-none`, and `backdrop-blur-md`; `style::core` re-exports it for future Check/Forge/Zed/Friday consumers. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style exposes browser compat fixture as a first-class source contract"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, live Tailwind/PostCSS execution, and full autoprefixer parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: surface this contract in `dx style build` / `dx style check` JSON receipts.

- Added the G-drive DX TS/TSX runner front door. `dx run` now OXC-validates `.ts` and `.tsx` files through the DX-WWW compiler path before running scripts or tests through a G-drive Bun runtime, refusing C-drive Node fallback. The unified CLI forwards `dx run ...` to DX-WWW, `G:\Dx\bin\dx.exe` and `G:\Dx\bin\dx-www.exe` were refreshed from release builds, and DX-owned legacy CommonJS guard/helper files were converted to `.ts` with docs and manifests rewritten to use `dx run --test` / `dx run --check`. Verification included focused release builds, `dx run --check .\examples\template\app\launch\page.tsx --json`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\tsx-next-link-compat.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-safety-archive-source-guard.test.ts`, `dx www version`, legacy-extension and direct-Node-reference scans, `git diff --check`, and conflict-marker scan. Remaining truth: this is a DX-owned TS/TSX command/runtime hygiene win, not full React/App Router parity yet.

- Added a dx-style Tailwind/PostCSS browser-compat reference fixture. `related-crates/style/fixtures/tailwind-postcss-browser-compat.json` now records the declaration-fragment canary for `appearance-none`, `select-none`, and `backdrop-blur-md`, requiring the dx-style declaration fragments to match the Tailwind/PostCSS browser-compat reference list while staying honest that this is not selector-order equality, speed parity, or full autoprefixer coverage. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style browser compat fixture records declaration-level Tailwind PostCSS parity"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, live Tailwind CLI execution, and full autoprefixer parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: in an approved install/build window, regenerate the fixture from real Tailwind/PostCSS output for the same class list.

- Added dx-style PostCSS-free browser-prefix utility coverage. The Rust style engine now emits prefixed normal CSS for `appearance-none`, `select-*`, `backdrop-filter`, `backdrop-filter-none`, and dynamic backdrop filter utilities such as `backdrop-blur-md`; the Tailwind parity receipt records `appearance-none`, `select-none`, and `backdrop-blur-md` as supported with generated CSS evidence. The README and compatibility matrix call this a targeted browser-prefix replacement slice, not full autoprefixer parity. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts -- --test-name-pattern "dx-style browser prefix utilities generate PostCSS-free compatibility CSS"`; full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, equal-output Tailwind benchmarking, and full autoprefixer parity testing were skipped by policy. Follow-up completed by the browser-compat reference fixture above; next action is governed real Tailwind/PostCSS fixture regeneration.

- Added the first transformed runtime-output and CSS local-import-flattening slices for source-owned `dx build`. Linked standalone TS/JS helper modules now pass through `module_runtime_transform.rs`, simple TSX leaf components pass through `module_tsx_runtime.rs`, and simple imported TSX/default route component graphs pass through the split `module_tsx_component_parser.rs` / `module_tsx_component_runtime.rs` adapter. Emitted chunks include executable browser ESM plus transformed `dxSourceText`, `dxRuntimeModule`, dependency `dxRuntimeExports` bindings, and exported `dxRuntimeExports`, and the source-build manifest/graph nodes record `transform_kind` plus `runtime_exports`. The CSS adapter now flattens local quoted `@import` files through `css_imports.rs` before parsing, so token CSS is included in generated CSS without leaving local import statements behind. Guarded with red/green `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --test source_build_engine`, focused `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli module_runtime_transform --lib`, focused `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli module_tsx_component_runtime --lib`, green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`, targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check` on touched Rust/test files, and conflict-marker scan. Full builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live hydration proof, and installed-binary refresh were skipped by policy. Checkpoint `dx build` lane status was 96/100 source-ready before the later CSS optimizer metadata slice.

- Added a Forge safety/archive runbook read model. `examples/template/forge-safety-archive-runbook.ts` now consumes the generated `public/preview-manifest.json` `sourceGuardRunbookFixtures` shape for `forge-safety-archive-rollback-coverage` and exposes the exact source-only rollback proof command. The preview-manifest fixture object now includes command/policy/no-server/no-install/no-build metadata, the React panel and static `/launch` fixture publish stable `data-dx-safety-archive-runbook-*` markers, and the DX Studio edit contract plus Rust Studio manifest advertise those markers for pre-hydration Studio/Zed inspection. Guarded with `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forge-safety-archive-source-guard.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-forge-package-lock.test.ts`, and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-runtime-materializer.test.ts`. Full builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live rollback/restore, live R2/S3 writes, and generated-app runtime proof were skipped by policy. Next action: show the command in the Zed GPUI receipt panel beside rollback coverage, then run governed Web Preview visual proof when approved.

- Added dx-style receipt source metadata for the public PostCSS-replacement path. `dx style build` and `dx style check` now emit `class_source_extension_counts` for `.tsx`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, `.js`, `.html`, and `.mdx` class-bearing files plus `style_entry_file_count` for `.css` style entry files, so Check/Forge/Zed can read source coverage without guessing from raw paths. Next action: rebuild the governed CLI in an approved window and extend the HTML/MDX temp-project CLI smoke to assert these fields from `dx style build --json`.

- Added a Forge safety/archive source-guard runbook. `docs/packages/forge-safety-archive.source-guard-runbook.json` now describes the source-only rollback coverage proof for the `/launch` safety/archive row, and `dx-www/src/cli/studio_manifest.rs` exposes `forge-safety-archive-rollback-coverage` through the Studio/Zed source guard index, runbook fixture paths, contracts, commands, and route source-guard ids. `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` mirrors that fixture into generated preview-manifest metadata so Studio can discover the proof before React hydration or a fresh package-status receipt. - Tightened the dx-style HTML/MDX scan boundary for the public PostCSS-replacement path. `dx-www/src/cli/public_framework_tools.rs` now uses an explicit `is_dx_style_static_class_source` helper for `.tsx`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, `.js`, `.html`, and `.mdx` class-token extraction, while `styles/theme.dx.css` and `styles/globals.css` stay style entry inputs rather than class-token sources. - Connected `dx build` route shells to a linked source-module graph. The source engine now emits browser-executable metadata `.mjs` chunks for the route entry, nested local TSX components, and local TS/JS helpers, and the route shell imports the entry chunk as `dxRouteEntryModule` without claiming full React hydration. The build graph now includes `source-module-chunk` nodes and `imports-source-module` edges, writes `.dx/receipts/graph/consumer-snapshot.json` with schema `dx.build.graph.consumerSnapshot`, and the Zed handoff points at both the full graph and compact consumer snapshot. `cmd_build` now reports `source_build_module_chunks` and graph snapshot emission. Checkpoint `dx build` lane status was 88/100 source-ready before the later runtime-transform and CSS import-flattening slice.

- Added guarded dx-style `@starting-style` arbitrary variant support and fail-closed unknown variant handling. `[@starting-style]:opacity-0` now emits normal CSS under `@starting-style`, unknown variant prefixes such as `[@layer_components]:p-4` no longer silently generate base utility CSS, the generated-output parity receipt marks `[@starting-style]:opacity-0` supported, and the public support/check canary moves to `[@layer_components]:p-4` for the remaining arbitrary at-rule grammar gap. The public PostCSS replacement contract now also names `styles/globals.css` beside `styles/theme.dx.css` and scans static classes from `.html` and `.mdx` files. Passed: targeted arbitrary at-rule guard 3/3, full `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts` 60/60, dx-style filtered `public-framework-tools` guard 3/3, and touched-file Rust formatting. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, equal-output Tailwind benchmarking, and broad Tailwind parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: decide whether to safely support `@layer` arbitrary variants or add the focused HTML/MDX scanner fixture before moving to equal-output Tailwind speed fixtures.

- Added safe dx-style `@layer` arbitrary variant support. `[@layer_components]:p-4` now emits normal generated CSS under `@layer components`, cascade layer names are strict identifier/dot segments, and unknown arbitrary at-rules such as `[@unknown_rule]:p-4` still fail closed instead of generating base utility CSS. The generated-output parity receipt marks `[@layer_components]:p-4` supported and moves the public unsupported canary to `[@unknown_rule]:p-4`. Passed: targeted arbitrary at-rule/parity guard 3/3, full `dx run --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts` 60/60, dx-style filtered `public-framework-tools` guard 3/3, TS syntax checks, and touched-file Rust formatting. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo builds/checks, equal-output Tailwind benchmarking, and broad Tailwind parity testing were skipped by policy. Next action: add the focused HTML/MDX scanner fixture or move to equal-output Tailwind speed fixtures before making stronger replacement claims.

- Cleared the current Forge CLI compile blocker. `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_execution/source_render.rs` now hoists its static-render/Next Link helper block above first use, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www` passes again with only pre-existing duplicate-package/dead-code warnings. The governed binary refresh is still deferred.

- Added durable Forge update/remove dry-run receipts. `core/src/ecosystem/forge_security.rs` now has persistent dry-run writers for root/curated updates, local-registry selected-export updates, and remove previews. These write `UpdateDryRun` / `RemoveDryRun` JSON receipts under `.dx/forge/receipts` without mutating `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, writing package files, or deleting visible app files. `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` now routes non-write update/remove commands through those persistent dry-run writers, while the pure plan functions remain available for in-memory previews. The golden-path contract and static `/launch` row now report 10/10 source steps real while keeping the launch state partial until the governed `dx-www` binary is refreshed and a checked-in dry-run receipt fixture is captured from the rebuilt command. Guarded with `dx run --test benchmarks\forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts benchmarks\forge-remove-command.test.ts`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler dry_run_persists --lib`, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler forge_local_registry_update_selected_export_e2e_from_root_dx_versions --lib`, and `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler forge_local_registry_remove_receipt_rolls_back_from_registry_content --lib`. `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check core\src\ecosystem\forge_security.rs dx-www\src\cli\mod.rs` was attempted but left unapplied because it reports broad pre-existing formatting churn outside this slice. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, live R2 writes, `just run`, and governed binary rebuild were skipped. Next action: refresh the governed `dx-www` binary and rerun the launch-template CLI golden path to capture the rebuilt command's checked-in dry-run receipt fixture.

- Added dx-style custom-property background size and position aliases. The Rust utility engine now emits normal CSS for `bg-size-(--dx-bg-size)` and `bg-position-(--dx-bg-position)` through the shared arbitrary/custom-property value path, the generated-output parity receipt marks `bg-size-(--dx-bg-size)` supported, and the public support/check canary moved to `[@starting-style]:opacity-0` for the broader arbitrary at-rule variant gap. Also refreshed the launch-runtime token guard to the existing `--destructive` theme token. Passed: targeted 3-test dx-style canary guard, full `benchmarks/dx-style-launch-contract.test.ts` 60/60, filtered `benchmarks/public-framework-tools.test.ts` 3/3, TS syntax checks, targeted `rustfmt --check`, scoped `git diff --check`, and scoped conflict/trailing-whitespace scans. - Continued the source-owned `dx build` engine into an ecosystem-connected route-shell and linked source-module slice. The build engine now emits compiler-backed route outputs from `dx_compiler::delivery::compile_react_app_route`: fallback `index.html`, canonical `index.dxpk`, `page-graph.json`, `route-unit.json`, and an executable ESM route-shell chunk with `mount()` plus `dxRouteEntryModule` while explicitly marking `fullReactHydration: false`. The source manifest now records `route_outputs` and linked source-module chunks for route/component/helper imports; the graph receipt records `source-module-chunk` nodes and `imports-source-module` edges; and `cmd_build` reports route outputs plus canonical build, graph, graph snapshot, and Zed handoff receipt status. New canonical ecosystem files are `.dx/receipts/build/latest.json`, `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json`, `.dx/receipts/graph/consumer-snapshot.json`, and `.dx/receipts/build/zed-handoff.json` with `dx.build.graph`, `dx.build.graph.consumerSnapshot`, `dx.www.moduleGraph`, `dx.forge.sourceGraph`, and `dx.build.zedHandoff` names. Guarded with red/green `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_emits_ecosystem_receipts_and_route_shell_chunk --test source_build_engine`. Full builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, full Rolldown parity, and live React hydration proof were skipped by policy. Checkpoint `dx build` lane status was 88/100 source-ready before the later runtime-transform and CSS import-flattening slice.

- Added Automation Connectors shared check-panel row. - Added TSX App Router request-prop, Link, and Image lowering. Generic TSX execution now passes matched route params and query search params into `build_tsx_source_render_surface`, emits `data-dx-tsx-page-prop-bindings`, and publishes a `dx.tsx.requestPropBindings` contract in `__DX_TSX_SOURCE_RENDER__`. The bounded source renderer resolves safe `params.slug`, `params["slug"]`, `searchParams.query`, `searchParams["query"]`, simple destructuring aliases like `const { slug } = params`, renamed aliases like `const { slug: postSlug } = params`, and member aliases like `const preview = searchParams.query` into static DOM snapshots, layout/template App Router shell composition, and source-owned component return previews when those values are already known from DX route matching. Static-safe `next/link` imports lower `<Link href="/path">` to source-owned `<a>` output, and static-safe `next/image` imports lower `<Image src alt width height />` to source-owned `<img>` output with DX framework markers instead of unresolved component noise. This improves React/App Router compatibility without `node_modules`, but still does not claim complete Next runtime parity: async Server Components, arbitrary request code, cookies/headers, dynamic `generateMetadata`, rich Link prefetch semantics, Next Image optimization, and generic JavaScript execution remain blocked. Guarded with red/green request-prop, Link, and Image Node guards, focused `dx run --test benchmarks\tsx-next-image-compat.test.ts benchmarks\tsx-next-link-compat.test.ts benchmarks\tsx-app-router-page-props.test.ts benchmarks\nextjs-compatibility-map.test.ts`, TS syntax checks, targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check`, targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www` with unrelated pre-existing warnings, scoped diff check, and conflict-marker scan. Broader `public-framework-contract` was attempted and currently fails outside this TSX slice because `dx-www/src/cli/mod.rs` no longer contains the expected `styles/globals.css` starter contract marker. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and live browser parity proof were skipped. Next action: add a dynamic `/blog/[slug]?preview=1` TSX fixture proving visible route/search aliases, `next/link` anchors, and `next/image` media through the generic App Router shell.

- Added Authentication Studio manifest receipt hash coverage. The lane 1 package `auth/better-auth` keeps official **Authentication** naming while `examples/template/authentication-receipt-hashes.ts` now hash-backs `dx-www/src/cli/studio_manifest.rs` as selected surface `authentication-studio-manifest-source`. - Added the source-owned `dx build` engine first slice. The existing `cmd_build` hook writes `.dx/build/source-build-manifest.json` and `.dx/build/source-build-receipt.json` with `dx.www.sourceBuildManifest` and `dx.www.sourceBuildReceipt` schemas, no `.v1` suffixes, and `node_modules_required: false`. Added the red/green fixture `dx-www/tests/source_build_engine.rs` for TSX `app/page.tsx` + CSS + public SVG asset -> manifest/receipt. Mirrored official upstream sources for Rolldown, Oxc, and Lightning CSS in `G:\WWW\inspirations`; Lightning CSS is provenance/reference only because it is MPL-2.0. Guarded with `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli source_build_engine_writes_receipt_for_tsx_css_assets --test source_build_engine` and targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check`. Next action: implement the first executable Rolldown-informed JS chunk/link adapter while preserving Oxc diagnostics and the source-build receipt.

- Added the DX build graph / Turbopack source-study slice. DX-WWW now has a source-owned `dx.build.graph` receipt writer under `tools/build-graph/` with small model/scanner/resolver/receipt/reader files, a `dx-graph.ts` CLI-shaped entrypoint, `docs/build-graph-model.md`, and the fixture `benchmarks/fixtures/build-graph/minimal-app`. The graph models TSX routes, components, dx-style generated CSS, Forge package surfaces, dx-check receipts, public assets, and deploy outputs with the names `dx.build.graph`, `dx.www.moduleGraph`, and `dx.forge.sourceGraph`, while marking `turbopackPublicDependency: false`. Turbopack source in `vercel/next.js` was inspected only for MIT-licensed provenance and graph/invalidation ideas; no upstream code was copied and no Turbopack/Turborepo dependency was added. Root DX CLI source now exposes `dx graph` through a small runner that invokes this writer with the current project by default. The new reader validates receipts, and `dx graph --consumer-snapshot --json` emits `dx.build.graph.consumerSnapshot` so DX-WWW and future Zed preview can consume counts, node kinds, invalidation, names, and provenance without inventing a parallel parser. Guarded with red/green `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-graph-receipt.test.ts`, focused CLI graph tests, and source-level root CLI smoke through `cargo run --manifest-path G:\Dx\cli\Cargo.toml --bin dx -- graph ... --consumer-snapshot --json`, proving a generated CSS change invalidates the source rebuild chain plus dependent Forge/check/deploy receipt nodes without running a build. Full builds, servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, Cargo release build, binary refresh, and runtime proof were skipped. Next action: refresh the governed installed binary in the release window, then point native Zed UI at `.dx/receipts/graph/latest.json` or the compact snapshot.

- Added TSX context provider value seeding for generic App Router routes. Context metadata now records safe literal `createContext(...)` defaults and safe literal `<Context.Provider value=...>` sources as `context_runtime.initial_values`, and the generated `__DX_CONTEXT_RUNTIME__` initializes `getContext`/`resolveContextValue` from those seeded values instead of starting every context at `null`. App Router responses expose `data-dx-context-initial-values`, and the public framework guard checks `initial_values`, `provider_value_source`, `safe_literal_context_value`, `program.initial_values`, and `context_initial_values`. This remains honest source-owned context probing, not full React context parity: dynamic provider expressions, provider tree reconciliation, `useContext` execution, concurrent rendering, arbitrary imports, and browser runtime proof are still open. Guarded with `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, targeted `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check`, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www`; the Cargo check passed with pre-existing Forge duplicate/dead-code warnings.

- Added a source-owned TSX context runtime. Generic App Router responses now expose `data-dx-context-runtime` and inject `__DX_CONTEXT_RUNTIME__` from `dx.tsx.contextRuntime`. The runtime detects context names from `createContext`, `.Provider`, and `useContext(...)`, exposes `getContext`, `resolveContextValue`, `setContext`, and `attachContextRuntime`, reflects values to `data-dx-context-read` markers, and dispatches `dx:context-runtime-ready` plus `dx:context-value` without requiring `node_modules`. This is honest provider-value-map support for the current compiler-owned TSX subset, not full React context parity: provider tree propagation, `useContext` execution, concurrent semantics, and generic component import execution remain unimplemented. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, touched-file `rustfmt --check`, and targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --bin dx-www`; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, and live provider propagation proof were skipped.

Type-Safe API `unsupported_surfaces` entries such as `trpc-websocket-subscriptions` are folded into `selected_surfaces` with `status: "unsupported-surface"`, `reason`, and `app_owned_boundary`, and the next action now prioritizes selecting a supported upstream-backed surface when that state appears. Re-inspected upstream tRPC `11.17.0` server/client/fetch adapter/HTTP batch/React Query sources for `initTRPC.context().create()`, `createCallerFactory`, `fetchRequestHandler`, `createTRPCClient`, `httpBatchLink`, and `createTRPCReact`. Guarded with a red targeted Cargo fixture that failed on the missing unsupported surface row before implementation; the follow-up targeted Cargo run timed out while shared Rust jobs were active, so final verification for this run is the lightweight Type-Safe API package-lane guard plus scoped hygiene checks. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, live tRPC subscriptions, live route/client/server/auth/persistence proof, and broad Cargo compilation were skipped. Next action: rerun `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler dx_check_latest_panel_exposes_type_safe_api_unsupported_surface_context --lib` when shared Rust jobs clear, then publish the targetable fixture in Type-Safe API docs/runbook.

The fixture keeps selected SHA-256 file hashes current, flips only `receipt_hash_refresh.status` and `receipt_hash_refresh.stale_file_count`, verifies the row and receipt status become stale, keeps `database_orm_hash_mismatch = 0`, and verifies `database_orm_receipt_hash_refresh_current = 0`, `database_orm_receipt_hash_refresh_stale = 1`, and `database_orm_receipt_hash_refresh_missing = 0` with a next action pointing to `database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --write`. Re-inspected upstream `drizzle-orm` `0.45.3` package metadata, SQLite `withReplicas`, `selectDistinct`, `$count`, and the better-sqlite3 `drizzle` driver. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-dx-check-package-lane-panel.test.ts`, targeted syntax checks, and scoped hygiene checks. Full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo compilation, live SQLite/replica proof, and browser visual proof were skipped. Next action: expose this stale-helper-only Database ORM proof as a targetable Cargo command after unrelated shared reader compile blockers settle.

The `i18n/next-intl` package surface also indexes the static dx-check package-lane template markers, including `data-dx-check-package-lane-template`, `data-dx-check-package-lane-row`, `data-dx-check-package-lane-dx-style-status`, `data-dx-style-surface`, and `data-dx-token-scope`, so Zed/DX Studio can discover the receipt-less Internationalization template from the manifest without scraping the runtime page first. Re-inspected upstream next-intl `4.12.0` provider, hooks, routing, navigation, request config, and middleware public APIs. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\next-intl-launch-package-lane-template.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo compilation, live locale routing, browser visual proof, SEO alternate-link review, and runtime dependency installation were skipped. Next action: add a generated-starter materialization guard proving the Internationalization source guard and package-lane template survive template output.

- Added dx-style mask-type utility generation. The Rust mask utility now emits normal CSS for Tailwind SVG mask-type helpers `mask-type-alpha` and `mask-type-luminance`, matching the official Tailwind v4.3 mask-type declarations without adding PostCSS. Next action: implement `font-stretch-*` utilities with generated-output proof.

- Improved TSX App Router rendering by preserving ordered JSX child trees. `DxReactJsxElement` now records `parent_index` and ordered `child_nodes`, the Oxc/custom JSX lowering paths populate that tree, and the TSX source renderer uses it for nested intrinsic DOM plus nested source-owned component preview insertion. The static preview now prefers the composed snapshot when available, so nested TSX does not degrade into fake sibling-only HTML. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, touched-file `rustfmt --edition 2024`, and targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with only the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning. This is stronger TSX compatibility, not full React/App Router parity.

- Added bounded TSX static template prop interpolation. Source-owned component previews now resolve static template literals with prop bindings, including common patterns such as `` `card ${className}`.trim() `` and `` `Hello ${props.name}` ``, when each interpolation is a literal or a safe caller prop binding. This improves real `.tsx` className/text rendering while keeping arbitrary JavaScript, function calls, spreads, hooks, and runtime data reads out of the static preview. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024`, and targeted `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with only the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning.

- Added bounded TSX static conditional prop branches. Component return previews now resolve safe ternaries such as `active ? "enabled" : "disabled"` and `` variant === "primary" ? `button ${variant}` : "button" `` when the condition and selected branch are literals, safe template literals, or caller prop bindings. This covers common className/text branch logic while still rejecting function calls and arbitrary JavaScript. Guarded with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --tests`, and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli resolves_static_conditional_prop_branch --lib` with only the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning.

- Added Forge R2/S3 remote HEAD CLI receipt command. `dx forge remote-head` / `remote-head-health` / `r2-head` now requires a real Forge registry manifest fixture, resolves a single package or selected surface, emits `dx.forge.remote_head_cli_report`, evaluates the existing remote object HEAD health receipt, and can persist `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes/<package>-<version>-r2-head-health.json` with `--write-receipt` or `--output`. - Added conic and composite dx-style mask generation. The Rust mask utility module now emits prefixed normal CSS for Tailwind conic masks (`mask-conic-from-*`, `mask-conic-to-*`, and `mask-conic-*` angles), mask-composite helpers (`mask-add`, `mask-subtract`, `mask-intersect`, `mask-exclude`), and official repeat/position aliases including `mask-repeat-space`, `mask-repeat-round`, `mask-top-left`, and `mask-bottom-right`. The Tailwind parity receipt now marks `mask-conic-from-50%` as supported, and later mask passes promoted `mask-l-from-50%` plus arbitrary linear masks; the current honest mask gap is `mask-radial-[100%_100%]`. Next action: implement arbitrary radial mask sizing before any full Tailwind mask claim.

The lane now uses `dx add forms --write` as the front-door command, keeps `react-hook-form` `7.75.0` from `G:/WWW/inspirations/react-hook-form` as provenance metadata, records inspected upstream files (`src/useForm.ts`, `src/useFormContext.tsx`, `src/controller.tsx`, `src/useController.ts`, `src/useFieldArray.ts`, and `src/types/form.ts`), lists the selected provider/registered-field/controlled-field/field-array/resolver/launch-lead surfaces, exposes `data-dx-component="launch-lead-form"` plus Zed edit markers, and guards the work with `dx run --test .\benchmarks\forms-react-hook-form-package-doc.test.ts`.
