- Surfaced unsupported dx-style scanned utilities in core dx-check. The `dx-style` check section now reads `unsupported_scanned_class_count` and `unsupported_scanned_class_findings` from `.dx/receipts/style/check.json`, records receipt/count metrics, and raises high-severity `dx-style-unsupported-scanned-class` findings when real app utility tokens scanned but generated no CSS.
- Exposed Forge R2/S3 HEAD health evaluation in remote read plans. Fixture-backed R2 dry-runs now serialize `object_head_health_evaluation` beside the metadata plan and approval-gated HEAD receipt, so dx-check, DX-WWW, Zed, and future Friday can see not-measured, missing-required, missing-optional, byte-mismatch, safe-install, and blocking-count fields without inventing their own interpretation or touching live R2/S3.
- Reported unsupported dx-style scanned utility classes. `dx style build` and `dx style check` receipts now include `unsupported_scanned_class_count` and `unsupported_scanned_class_findings` for Tailwind-like source tokens that dx-style cannot generate yet, and `dx style check` fails on those real unsupported app utilities without treating ordinary semantic component classes as Tailwind parity failures.
- Restored the missing Forge remote HEAD health module. `core/src/ecosystem/forge_remote_health.rs` now evaluates `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` receipts into `dx.forge.remote_object_head_health` with missing-required, missing-optional, byte-mismatch, blocking-count, warning, and next-action fields so targeted Rust checks compile and consumers can block unsafe remote installs honestly.
- Added a fake-provider Forge R2/S3 HEAD execution harness. `execute_r2_remote_object_head_checks_with_provider` can now populate `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` from an injected test provider with measured existence, bytes, ETag, and last-modified fields while preserving explicit approval, `write_allowed=false`, and an honest no-live-R2 warning when `network_allowed=false`.
- Added approval-gated Forge R2/S3 HEAD execution receipts. Fixture-backed remote read plans now carry `dx.forge.remote_object_head_execution_receipt` with per-object `head-object` entries copied from the verified metadata plan, explicit `approval_required=true`, `approved=false`, `executed=false`, no measured remote fields, and `network_allowed=false` / `write_allowed=false`, so consumers can render the future live-probe receipt shape without implying any R2 request happened.
- Activated the Oxc TSX parser backend for the public CLI path. `core/src/delivery/tsx_ast.rs` now emits `dx.tsx.parserBackend`, validates syntax through `oxc_parser::Parser` under the `oxc` feature, derives import declarations and `export const metadata` route metadata from the Oxc `Program` AST, carries backend status into `DxTsxModuleAst`, `DxReactJsxDocument`, and App Router source-render JSON, and `dx-www --features cli` now enables `dx-www-compiler/oxc`. The old `swc_parser` module is now a thin compatibility shim over the maintained parser pipeline instead of a broken outdated Oxc visitor path.
- Summarized dx-style Tailwind parity receipts in dx-check. The core `dx-style` section now reads `.dx/receipts/style/check.json`, extracts `tailwind_parity_receipt_contract`, exposes receipt/schema/supported/unsupported/intentional-difference metrics, and raises honest low-severity findings for known unsupported parity fixtures and intentional differences instead of hiding them in raw style receipts.
- Added read-only Forge R2/S3 object metadata planning. Fixture-backed remote plans now carry `dx.forge.remote_object_metadata_plan` with exact `head-object` checks for `latest.json`, package manifests, and content-addressed blobs, including expected content hashes and byte lengths from the verified manifest while marking every check `planned-not-checked` and keeping network/write execution disabled.
- Exposed dx-style Tailwind parity receipts through DX-WWW style tooling. `dx style build` and `dx style check` receipts now include `tailwind_parity_receipt_contract` from `style::core::build_tailwind_parity_receipt()`, with schema, Tailwind baseline, supported/unsupported/intentionally-different counts, known unsupported examples, intentional differences, generated-output entries, and dx-check/Forge/Zed/Friday consumer hints without claiming full Tailwind parity.
- Added fixture-backed Forge R2/S3 install previews. `plan_r2_remote_read_only_install_from_manifest_fixture` parses a real registry package manifest fixture, verifies package id/version and integrity, resolves selected exports, maps front-facing target paths, hashes existing local files, and emits `dx.forge.remote_manifest_install_preview` conflict counts; `dx forge add package#surface --registry r2 --dry-run --remote-manifest <manifest.json> --format json` exposes that plan without network reads, writes, blob fetches, or live cloud claims.
- Added a generated-output Tailwind parity receipt to dx-style. `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/parity.rs` now feeds curated Tailwind-familiar classes through `StyleEngine::css_for_class`, records generated CSS for supported utilities, keeps unsupported gaps such as `mask-radial-[100%_100%]` visible, and marks intentional differences such as the minimal `container` utility without claiming full Tailwind parity.
- Extended destructured TSX prop alias lowering to simple renamed aliases and static literal defaults. `dx.tsx.componentDestructuredPropAliases` now records `renamed-alias-object-pattern`, `default-value-object-pattern`, alias-to-prop names, default value metadata, and resolves `{ title: heading }` / `{ title = "Untitled" }` style reads in safe intrinsic previews without claiming full JavaScript parameter evaluation.
- Documented the TSX parser backend truth: Oxc parser crates are present as optional workspace dependencies, but the active TSX extraction path still uses DX-WWW's custom scanner; the next architecture step is an Oxc-backed parser adapter and measured source-surface comparison before public-path switching.
- Implemented guarded dx-style `content-*` utilities for the supported pseudo-element path. `content-none`, `content-['hello_world']`, `content-[attr(data-label)]`, `content-(--dx-label)`, `before:content-['New']`, and `after:content-(--dx-suffix)` now emit normal CSS while full Tailwind content/plugin parity remains partial.
- Lowered simple destructured prop aliases into source-owned TSX component return previews. `dx.tsx.componentReturnPreview` now carries `dx.tsx.componentDestructuredPropAliases`, recognizes direct object-pattern component params such as `function Card({ title })` and `const Card = ({ title }) => ...`, and uses those aliases to resolve literal caller props in safe intrinsic preview HTML without claiming rest/spread/default/nested prop evaluation or full component execution.
- Added a read-only R2/S3 provider plan contract for Forge remote lifecycle dry-runs. `dx forge add/update/remove --registry r2 --dry-run --format json` source now carries `dx.forge.remote_read_plan` with selected exports, package/version object keys for `latest.json`, `manifest.json`, and `files/<content-hash>`, plus `network_allowed: false` and `write_allowed: false`; this is provider planning only, not live remote fetch/install.
- Added guarded dx-style pseudo-element variants for the supported utility path. `before:bg-blue-500`, `after:opacity-100`, `placeholder:text-slate-900`, `file:p-4`, `marker:text-slate-900`, `selection:bg-blue-500`, `first-letter:text-slate-900`, and `first-line:bg-blue-500` now resolve through default state tokens while full Tailwind pseudo-element/content utility parity remains partial.
- Lowered simple caller prop identifiers into source-owned TSX component return previews. `dx.tsx.componentReturnPreview` now carries `dx.tsx.componentPropIdentifierBindings`, resolves literal caller props into `{propName}` and `{props.propName}` reads inside safe intrinsic preview HTML, and counts skipped dynamic/spread/event props without claiming full prop evaluation.
- Materialized the TSX state reflection snapshot into generic App Router responses. The safe static DOM snapshot now appears as a hidden `data-dx-tsx-static-dom-preview="safe-common-subset"` surface before runtime scripts, giving `data-dx-state-*` markers a real response-DOM target; the generated state runtime exposes `state_dom_reflection`, exposes `reflectStateSlotToDom`, and reflects initial slots once on startup without claiming React reconciliation.
- Added a skip-safe existing-binary smoke for Forge R2 lifecycle dry-runs. `benchmarks/forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` now runs `dx forge add/update/remove auth/better-auth#client --registry r2 --dry-run --format json` when an existing `target/debug/dx-www` binary supports the lifecycle schema, asserts `dx.forge.remote_lifecycle_plan`, selected exports, requested versions, object/receipt plans, `write_allowed: false`, no materialized package files, no `node_modules`, and no leaked R2 env/config values.
- Added TSX state DOM reflection planning for the generic App Router runtime. `dx.tsx.stateDomReflection` now records direct `useState` reads for text, input value, checked state, boolean attributes, and aria attributes; static DOM snapshots carry compiler-owned `data-dx-state-*` markers; and `__DX_STATE_GRAPH_RUNTIME__` now exposes `reflectStateSlotToDom` / `dx:state-dom-reflection` without claiming full React reconciliation.
- Bridged TSX DOM action previews into the generated state runtime. `dx.tsx.domActionBinder` now includes `dx.tsx.domActionStateBridge`, forwards safe matched `click`, `input`/`change`, and native `submit` previews to `window.__DX_STATE_GRAPH_RUNTIME__.dispatch`, emits `dx:state-runtime-dispatch`, and applies lowerable `set-from-input`, `toggle`, `add`, `subtract`, and `set-literal` operations without claiming full React hook/event parity.
- Added dry-run-only R2 remote lifecycle plans for Forge. `dx forge add/update/remove --registry r2` now returns `dx.forge.remote_lifecycle_plan` planning data with selected exports, requested version, redacted R2 setup labels, object-key plan, receipt plan, and `write_allowed: false`; JSON output stays machine-readable without selected-export banner text, and no network read/write/delete/sync is performed.
- Added guarded dx-style arbitrary at-rule variants for the supported utility path. `[@media_(any-hover:hover)]:opacity-100`, `[@supports_(display:grid)]:grid`, and `[@container_main_(min-width:_32rem)]:p-4` now resolve through safe `@media`, `@supports`, and `@container` wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added the first no-`node_modules` TSX DOM action binder. Generic App Router responses now expose `data-dx-dom-action-binder`, `data-dx-dom-action-descriptors`, and an injected `__DX_DOM_ACTION_BINDER__` script generated from `dx.tsx.domActionBinder`, binding safe `click`, `input`/`change`, and native `submit` preview events without running React handler bodies or claiming React event parity.
- Added guarded dx-style arbitrary descendant and sibling selector variants for the supported utility path. `[&_p]:mt-4`, `[&+*]:mt-4`, `[&~*]:opacity-100`, and escaped literal underscores such as `[&_.file\_name]:mt-4` now resolve through an explicit arbitrary selector decoder while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added guarded dx-style structural pseudo-class variants for the supported utility path. `only:bg-blue-500`, `first-of-type:p-4`, `last-of-type:text-slate-900`, and `only-of-type:opacity-100` now resolve through default state tokens and shared state selector guards while full Tailwind structural variant grammar remains partial.
- Added TSX DOM action descriptors to component runtime binding plans. `dx.tsx.domActionDescriptors` now records safe intrinsic `button`, `input`/`textarea`/`select`, and `form` action targets from source-owned components, including React-style event attributes and matching state event slots, while keeping browser listener attachment and full React event execution unclaimed.
- Extended guarded dx-style nth structural variants for the supported utility path. `nth-last-2:bg-blue-500`, `nth-of-type-[3n+1]:p-4`, and `nth-last-of-type-2:opacity-100` now resolve through safe structural pseudo-class wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary structural variant grammar remains partial.
- Added actionable R2 setup labels to Forge publish dry-run payloads. Core `DxForgeR2Status` now serializes `setup_status` and redacted `missing_config`, so `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package <id> --dry-run --format json` and `dx forge remotes --json` share the same configured/partial/missing setup contract without printing account, access-key, or secret-key values.
- Implemented guarded dx-style nth structural variants for the supported utility path. `nth-3:bg-blue-500` and `nth-[2n+1]:p-4` now resolve through safe `:nth-child(...)` selector wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary structural variant grammar remains partial.
- Added a TSX component runtime binding plan to source-owned component return previews. `dx.tsx.componentRuntimeBindingPlan` now records matching state slots, event slots, and intrinsic form-lowering metadata for local component source files while keeping `full_dom_binding: false` and React submit handler execution unclaimed.
- Added dynamic redacted Forge remote next actions and tightened R2 dry-run value redaction. `dx forge status/remotes --json` now derives `next_actions` from local/R2 `setup_status` and `missing_config`, low-level R2 status JSON now exposes bucket/endpoint/public-base-url readiness as booleans instead of serializing values, and dry-run object plans use `<configured-bucket>` / `<unconfigured-bucket>` labels so consumers get safe guidance without credential or config-value leakage.
- Implemented guarded dx-style `in-*` ancestor variants for the supported utility path. `in-hover:bg-blue-500` and `in-[.is-open]:p-4` now resolve through safe `:where(...)` ancestor wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added a TSX children insertion preview for source-owned component composition. Component entries now include `dx.tsx.componentReturnPreview`, which previews safe intrinsic JSX from the imported component file and substitutes direct `{children}` / `{props.children}` placeholders with caller direct text and literal child expressions when possible; nested child JSX order, prop identifier evaluation, component function execution, event attachment, and hooks remain unclaimed.
- Added actionable R2 setup status to Forge public remote JSON. `dx forge status/remotes --json` now carries a stable `setup_status` plus redacted `missing_config` labels for local and R2 remotes, so DX-WWW, dx-check, and future Zed panels can distinguish missing, partial, and configured remote setup without printing credential values or requiring a binary rebuild in this lightweight pass.
- Implemented guarded dx-style child selector variants for the supported utility path. `*:p-4` now targets direct children and `**:text-slate-900` now targets descendants through generated normal CSS wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Made `dx forge status --json` write the canonical latest Forge status receipt at `.dx/receipts/forge/status-latest.json`. The receipt carries `schema_version = dx.forge.status`, `receipt_path`, `root_package`, and `local_registry_package` so DX CLI/TUI and future Zed GPUI consumers can read one stable latest path without executing Forge or probing fallback folders.
- Added TSX component invocation input metadata to the generic source render surface. Source-owned component composition entries now carry `dx.tsx.componentInvocationInputs` with literal/static props, event and spread prop boundaries, direct text children, direct literal child expressions, skipped child expressions, and static-renderer eligibility; this still does not execute component functions, bind `children`, attach events, run hooks, or claim React runtime parity.
- Implemented guarded dx-style `:not()` selector variants for the supported utility path. `not-hover:bg-blue-500`, `not-[.is-open]:p-4`, `group-not-hover/card:text-slate-900`, and `peer-not-checked/published:opacity-100` now resolve through safe state/arbitrary `:not(...)` wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added redacted R2 partial-credential readiness to Forge status contracts. `DxForgeR2Status` now reports `access_key_id_set` and `secret_access_key_set` booleans without serializing credential values, `dx forge status/remotes --json` public remote state carries the same fields for DX-WWW/dx-check/Zed consumers, and the lightweight Forge benchmark now source-guards the partial-credential case while existing binary R2 publishing remains dry-run only.
- Added source-owned component composition metadata to the generic TSX source render surface. Local component JSX usages now link to bounded source-owned TSX/JSX imports with importer/source/import-kind details plus the imported component file's safe static snapshot, and generic App Router DOM exposes `data-dx-tsx-component-compositions`; this still does not execute component functions, hooks, prop logic, effects, context, or runtime data reads.
- Implemented guarded dx-style `:has()` selector variants for the supported utility path. `has-checked:bg-blue-500`, `has-[img]:p-4`, `group-has-[a]/card:text-slate-900`, and `peer-has-[input:checked]/published:opacity-100` now resolve through safe state/arbitrary `:has(...)` wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added a skip-safe binary smoke for root-`dx` R2/S3 dry-run publishing. When an existing `target/debug/dx-www` binary is present, `benchmarks/forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` now creates a temp one-file `dx` package, runs `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package auth/better-auth --dry-run --format json` with fake Cloudflare/S3-compatible env values, verifies the redacted configured status plus planned `index.json`, manifest, content-addressed file, and receipt object keys, and asserts the fake account id, access key, secret key, and secret env marker names never appear in the JSON output. No live cloud upload, rebuild, server, browser, deploy, or package install was run.
- Implemented guarded dx-style named arbitrary group/peer selector variants for the supported utility path. `group-[.is-open]/card:bg-blue-500` and `peer-[data-state=checked]/published:opacity-100` now resolve through named group/peer wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added literal TSX expression evaluation to the bounded static DOM snapshot. Generic App Router source surfaces now preserve simple string, static template, number, boolean, and nullish expression props/children in `dx.tsx.staticDomSnapshot`, expose `static_dom_snapshot_literal_expressions`, and stamp `data-dx-tsx-literal-expressions` without executing component functions, hooks, object spreads, function calls, or runtime JavaScript.
- Added a skip-safe binary smoke for the root-`dx` local-registry lifecycle. When an existing `target/debug/dx-www` binary is present, `benchmarks/forge-update-local-registry-command.test.ts` now creates a temp one-file `dx` package, publishes v1 to a local registry, installs only `auth/better-auth#client`, publishes v2, previews and writes a green selected-export update through the CLI JSON path, and verifies no unselected `server` file or `node_modules` is materialized. Verification stayed lightweight with Node syntax/test checks; no rebuild, Cargo, server, browser, npm/bun/cargo package install, deploy, or cloud publish was run.
- Implemented guarded dx-style responsive media and feature-query variants for the supported utility path. `max-md:*`, `min-[475px]:*`, `max-[960px]:*`, `motion-safe:*`, `motion-reduce:*`, `portrait:*`, `landscape:*`, `print:*`, `supports-[display:grid]:*`, and `not-supports-[display:grid]:*` now emit nested normal CSS through the Rust engine while full Tailwind responsive/config/plugin parity remains partial.
- Added a bounded TSX static DOM snapshot to the generic App Router source render surface. `dx.tsx.sourceRenderSurface` now carries `dx.tsx.staticDomSnapshot` with safe intrinsic JSX HTML fragments from route, segment, and source-owned import files, plus runtime DOM markers for snapshot status and element count; it remains honest about not executing component functions, hooks, arbitrary expressions, or event handlers.
- Added enriched root-package readiness to `dx forge status --json`. The public status report now exposes `root_package` and `local_registry_package` fields with package id, version, export count, default exports, selective-import readiness, local manifest path, and verified/missing/invalid package state, so DX CLI/TUI and Zed consumers can show whether the one-file `dx` package is actually published locally instead of only seeing that a registry folder exists.
- Added a shared dx-check `detected_not_applied` scoring-config panel fixture. The root CLI fixture pack now includes a real receipt where future custom weights are detected but not applied to the launch-default 500-point score, and DX-WWW plus website smoke checks consume that fixture directly instead of synthesizing the state in test code.
- Added a root-`dx` v1-to-v2 local-registry update fixture for Forge. The Rust fixture publishes `auth/better-auth` v1 from the one-file `dx` manifest, installs only the `client` export from the local registry, republishes v2, plans and writes a green selected-export update, verifies the update receipt keeps `selected_exports=["client"]`, and confirms the unselected `server` file plus `node_modules` stay absent. Verification stayed lightweight with the targeted Node source guard; no Cargo build/test was run.
- Implemented guarded dx-style text flow and line-clamp utilities for the supported utility path. `line-clamp-3`, `line-clamp-none`, `line-clamp-[7]`, `hyphens-auto`, `text-balance`, `text-ellipsis`, and `text-clip` now emit normal generated CSS while full Tailwind typography/plugin parity remains partial.
- Added bounded source-owned TSX import scanning to the generic App Router source render surface. `dx.tsx.sourceRenderSurface` now follows local relative and `@/` TSX/JSX imports from route/segment files, exposes scanned/skipped import counts, and publishes renderable elements/component refs/prop bindings/form surfaces for imported local components without claiming full React import execution; the focused public-framework source guard is green, while Cargo/server/browser verification remains outside this lightweight pass.
- Added `dx forge update` local-registry ownership. `dx forge update <package> --registry local [--version <version>] [--dry-run|--write]` now resolves verified filesystem-registry package versions, honors selected exports such as `package#client,server`, compares them against `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, writes only green no-overwrite updates, and records selected-export update receipts. Root `dx` selective update without `--registry local`, remote update, and live R2 update remain explicit boundaries.
- Added archive-backed `dx forge remove` lifecycle proof. Tracked Forge packages can now produce remove dry-runs and writes from `.dx/forge/source-manifest.json`, hash-check every tracked file, block locally edited files with `forge-remove-local-edit`, archive clean files under `.dx/forge/archive/...` before `remove_file`, remove the package from the manifest, and write `RemoveDryRun` / `RemoveWrite` receipts. This is local/front-facing package removal only; remote uninstall and live R2 cleanup remain unclaimed.
- Added a real launch-template Forge media-status and restore receipt. `forge-package-lock.ts --write|--check` now maintains `.dx/forge/media-status.json`, `.dx/forge/receipts/media/launch-template-favicon-restore.json`, and a hash-verified `.dx/forge/media-cache/.../favicon.svg` copy, exposes `mediaStatus` through the typed read model and `forge-package-status.ts`, and adds dx-check source metrics for media-status, restore receipts, cache files, and missing media cache files without claiming broad Git LFS replacement.
- Added a real launch-template Forge multi-remote status and sync-plan receipt. `forge-package-lock.ts --write|--check` now emits `.dx/forge/remote-status.json` plus `.dx/forge/receipts/remotes/launch-template-sync-plan.json`, exposes `remoteStatus` through the typed read model and `forge-package-status.ts`, and adds dx-check source metrics for remote-status/sync-plan presence, safe remotes, and boundary remotes without claiming live network sync.
- Added a real launch-template Forge VCS status receipt. `forge-package-lock.ts --write|--check` now emits `.dx/forge/vcs-status.json` and `.dx/forge/receipts/vcs/launch-template-snapshot.json` for a deterministic 66-file package/source/media inventory, and the generated read model exposes the snapshot path/counts to DX-WWW, dx-check metric names, and future Zed receipt consumers without claiming full Git replacement.
- Added root `dx` Forge package local-registry publishing and install proof. `dx forge publish --registry local --package <id>` can now publish the root `dx` package manifest into `.dx/forge/registry/local` with manifest/export/default-export metadata, verified content-addressed file blobs, and a publish receipt; `dx forge add package#surface --registry local` can load that local registry package, select export surfaces, and route the files through the existing conflict-aware front-facing install planner.
- Added root `dx` R2/S3 dry-run object planning. `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package <id> --dry-run` can use the root package manifest when it matches the requested package, discovers Cloudflare and S3-compatible env fallbacks without serializing secret values, and returns redacted object URLs for `index.json`, `packages/js/<name>/<version>/manifest.json`, content-addressed file blobs, and the receipt prefix without enabling live root-package R2 upload.
- Added root `dx` Forge package export-map planning for generic selected imports. A package manifest with `[package]`, `[forge]`, `[[forge.files]]`, `[[forge.exports]]`, and `[forge.install]` can now feed `dx forge add auth/better-auth#client --dry-run` through the existing front-facing install planner, selecting only the mapped files and recording `selected_exports` plus a `root-dx-selected-exports` policy decision without creating `node_modules`.
- Added the public Forge CLI alias layer in DX-WWW: `dx forge publish --registry local`, `dx forge publish --registry r2 --package <id> --dry-run`, `dx forge status --json`, `dx forge remotes --json`, `dx forge receipts --json`, and selective surface syntax for current surface-package families such as `dx forge add shadcn/ui#button,card,input` and `dx forge add ui --only button,input`. R2/S3 dry-runs expose object plans and redacted readiness only; live R2 writes still require `--write --yes`; root-package live R2 upload, update/remove, and remote install remain separate boundary work.
- Added a receipt-backed Forge package status read model for the DX-WWW launch template. `forge-package-lock.ts --check` now validates generated `forge-package-status-read-model.ts` against `.dx/forge/package-status.json`, and `forge-package-status.ts` consumes typed rows for `shadcn/ui/button`, `state/zustand`, and `tanstack/query` package receipts, cache manifests, rollback anchors, safety archive receipts, remote/media counts, dx-check metrics, and Zed receipt surface names without claiming live registry, remote rollback, or remote sync.
- Added the DX-WWW framework completeness contract for the launch starter. The matrix now records routing parity, server/client model, dev experience, production dashboard template, and package ecosystem coverage with honest evidence/status fields, is materialized into generated starters as `components/launch/framework-completeness.ts`, is surfaced by the launch template registry/DOM markers, and is counted by `dx-check` project-contract metrics.
- Wired the DX-WWW launch/Studio dx-check panel to the `dx.www.check_panel_view_model` render contract. The React launch shell, runtime-safe `/launch.html`, Studio manifest, edit contract, and runtime materializer now expose receipt status, score availability, bucket/finding/action counts, last-run state, and explicit missing-receipt empty state markers without running expensive checks or inventing a score.
- Added the dx-style launch contract guard and compatibility matrix. The matrix covers the requested Tailwind-family areas, explicitly keeps full Tailwind classname parity unclaimed, marks important modifiers unsupported, and documents partial data/aria/group/peer, transition, animation, and container-query coverage.
- Rewrote the dx-style crate README around the public DX-WWW launch path: normal generated CSS, Rust scanner/generator, theme-token strictness, no hidden dependency surface, no Tailwind/PostCSS requirement, binary style output deprecated/internal, and file evidence for actual memmap2 usage with no dx-style rkyv dependency.
- Implemented dx-style important modifiers for the supported utility path. Plain utilities such as `!p-4`, variant-prefixed utilities such as `hover:!bg-blue-500`, and encoded child utilities such as `!space-x-4` now keep the authored selector and mark generated declarations with `!important`; the compatibility matrix now records this as a supported subset instead of unsupported.
- Implemented guarded dx-style data/aria attribute variants for the supported utility path. `data-open:bg-blue-500`, `aria-expanded:text-slate-900`, and `data-[state=open]:opacity-100` now map to attribute selectors while the compatibility matrix still keeps named group/peer and full arbitrary variant grammar partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style named group/peer variants for simple names. `group-hover/card:bg-blue-500`, `group-focus/nav:text-slate-900`, and `peer-checked/published:opacity-100` now map to named group/peer selectors while the compatibility matrix still keeps full arbitrary group/peer grammar partial.
- Implemented guarded Tailwind-style dx-style container query variants for min-width container queries. `@sm:bg-blue-500`, `@[475px]:text-slate-900`, and `@lg:hover:opacity-100` now emit normal `@container (min-width: ...)` CSS while complete named-container and max-range grammar stays partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style transition utilities for the supported utility path. `transition-colors`, `duration-300`, `delay-200`, `ease-in-out`, arbitrary duration values, and arbitrary easing values now emit normal CSS while advanced Tailwind transition behavior/property aliases remain partial.
- Implemented guarded Tailwind-style dx-style animation utilities for the supported animation path. `animate-spin`, `animate-ping`, `animate-pulse`, `animate-bounce`, `animate-none`, and arbitrary `animate-[...]` values now emit normal generated CSS/keyframes while custom Tailwind animation config/theme extension parity remains partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style arbitrary group/peer selector variants for safe selector fragments. `group-[.is-open]:bg-blue-500` and `peer-[data-state=checked]:opacity-100` now map to group/peer wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style named and max-width container query variants. `@lg/main:bg-blue-500`, `@max-md:text-slate-900`, and `@max-[960px]:opacity-100` now emit normal `@container` CSS while full Tailwind container-query config and edge grammar remain partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style transition property aliases and transition behavior utilities. `transition-[height,opacity]`, `transition-(--dx-transition-property)`, `transition-discrete`, and `transition-normal` now emit normal CSS while transition plugin/theme extension parity remains partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style animation CSS variable aliases. `animate-(--dx-animation-enter)` now emits `animation: var(--dx-animation-enter)` while broader Tailwind animation config/theme extension parity remains partial.
- Implemented guarded dx-style safe generic arbitrary selector variants. `[&>svg]:bg-blue-500` and `[&:nth-child(3)]:opacity-100` now map to selector wrappers while full Tailwind arbitrary variant grammar remains partial.
- Added the dx-style registry snapshot cache plan as isolated Rust code. `src/cache/registry_snapshot_plan.rs` defines the future `.dx/style/registry.snapshot` format/version contract for class registry keys, theme tokens, screen/container tokens, and checksums, while keeping rkyv unwired, binary style output internal, and normal generated CSS as the public launch path.
- Added an explicit PostCSS-replacement contract to `dx style` build/check receipts. The public CLI now names the scan roots, extensions, class/className extraction patterns, token entry file, generated CSS output, no PostCSS adapter dependency, no Tailwind dependency, and no template-local `node_modules` requirement.
- Tokenized the launch-template runtime CSS bridge for dx-style launch hygiene. `examples/template/runtime-assets/launch-runtime.css` now derives its runtime palette from DX theme tokens and `color-mix(...)` instead of embedding hardcoded hex/rgb color literals.
- Added a normal CSS renderer to the DX-WWW style compiler. `dx-www/src/build/style.rs` now exposes `STYLE_PUBLIC_OUTPUT_MODE = "normal-generated-css"` and `StyleCompiler::compile_normal_css`, while DXC1 is explicitly `deprecated-internal` compatibility rather than the public launch path.
- Added a TSX class scanner entry point to the DX-WWW style parser. `dx-www/src/parser/style.rs` now exposes `STYLE_CLASS_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` and `extract_class_attribute_tokens` for static `className`/`class` extraction, giving the no-PostCSS dx-style path a Rust-owned scanner hook without claiming dynamic expression parity.
- Extended dx-style class scanning to static TSX expression strings. The parser and `dx style` CLI scan contract now recognize `className={\`...\`}`, `className={"..."}`, `className={'...'}`, and matching `class={...}` forms while keeping arbitrary JavaScript class expression parsing out of scope until a dedicated parser pass.
- Extended dx-style class scanning to common static helper-call strings. The parser and `dx style` CLI scan contract now extract quoted tokens from `cn(...)`, `cx(...)`, `clsx(...)`, `classNames(...)`, and `cva(...)` calls for TSX class composition while keeping arbitrary JavaScript parsing and runtime branch evaluation out of scope.
- Filtered dynamic template class placeholders out of dx-style scanner output. Tokens containing `${...}` / `$` are skipped before generated selector discovery so `dx-${variant}` cannot become fake generated CSS, while concrete neighboring class strings still scan.
- Wired the public `dx style build` generator to the dx-style utility engine. `dx style build` and `dx style check` now split scanned static class tokens, generated `dx-*` template classes, and generated utility classes; supported static utilities emit normal CSS rules through the Rust engine, while unsupported tokens remain unclaimed instead of becoming fake placeholders. The generated CSS finish step uses the existing Lightning CSS parse/print path when it preserves the source hash, keeping the PostCSS replacement path Rust-owned.
- Hardened `dx style check` against legacy CSS tool configs. Root-level `postcss.config.*` and `tailwind.config.*` files now produce `legacy_css_tooling_findings` and fail the check, keeping the official DX-WWW starter on the dx-style replacement path instead of silently falling back to PostCSS/Tailwind.
- Hardened `dx style check` against legacy Tailwind/PostCSS package dependencies. `package.json` entries for `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`, and `@tailwindcss/postcss` now produce `legacy_css_dependency_findings` and fail the check so the public starter keeps the no hidden dependency surface launch promise.
- Hardened `dx style check` against stale legacy CSS lockfile entries. Common lockfiles (`package-lock.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `bun.lock`, `bun.lockb`) are now scanned for Tailwind/PostCSS packages and produce `legacy_css_lockfile_findings` when dependency residue remains after `package.json` cleanup.

All notable changes to the dx-www framework will be documented in this file. The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

## [Unreleased] - 2026-05-21

### Live QA

- Re-centered the generated launch template on the public TSX route path: `app/page.tsx` is now the default `/` dashboard, `app/launch/page.tsx` stays as the `/launch` alias, and the dev runtime has a scoped TSX App Router launch responder that reads `LaunchShell` source markers instead of relying on legacy page materialization for the default proof. A fresh generated app on `http://127.0.0.1:3000/` returned 200 with `data-dx-route="/"`, no literal `{children}`, one form, one canvas, stable `data-dx-icon` markers, and `/favicon.svg` returning 200.
- Ran a live generated-template QA pass with `dx-www new target\live-qa\dx-launch-live-20260521-203450` and `dx-www dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000`. The generated app stayed no-`node_modules`, `/api/health` reported the DX runtime, and `/_dx/hot-reload/version` returned a hot-reload token, but `/launch` rendered the generic fallback shell with literal `{children}` instead of the composed Forge launch dashboard.
- Recorded the launch runtime blockers found live: zero visible auth, payment, form/Zod, state/query, animation, 3D canvas, docs/markdown, automations, database/backend, or Studio edit-marker surfaces on `/launch`; manifest-advertised `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, and `/backend` routes returned 404 in the generated app; the AI route's exported `const POST` was not detected by the current route handler path; and the dynamic tRPC route was not reachable at `/api/trpc/health`.
- Verified the Stripe checkout boundary is at least route-backed: `POST /api/checkout` returned the safe contact fallback without processing payment data when `STRIPE_PRICE_ID` is absent.
- Added a live runtime materialization bridge for the generated launch template. The bridge writes `.html` pages, a passthrough pages layout, CSS/JS runtime assets, a Studio preview manifest, and function-export API route handlers so the current `target/debug/dx-www.exe` can serve `/launch`, `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, `/backend`, `/api/auth/session`, `/api/checkout`, `/api/ai/chat`, and `/api/trpc/health` without rebuilding the Rust binary or creating app-local `node_modules`.
- Re-ran the live generated-template path with `dx-www new target\live-qa\dx-launch-runtime-20260521 --template launch`, `node tools\launch\materialize-www-template.ts target\live-qa\dx-launch-runtime-20260521`, and `dx-www dev --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000`. The live guard passed: `/launch` no longer leaks `{children}`, contains `data-dx-route="/launch"`, renders a form and canvas, exposes package proof sections, and the manifest-advertised routes return 200.
- Verified visible browser interactions for auth sign-in state, Stripe boundary check, invalid and valid Zod-shaped form validation, state counter, query refresh, Motion toggle, tRPC health, and AI adapter route. Live website readiness is now 76/100, with the remaining gap being full TSX/App Router component rendering rather than the runtime-safe `.html` bridge.
- Recorded the newer `http://127.0.0.1:3001/launch` QA baseline as 72/100: the live route is no longer dummy UI and proves no-template-local-`node_modules`, 37 Studio markers, mobile safety, theme/font/scrollbar, route smoke, forms/Zod, state/query, local tRPC, docs preview, AI route, WASM add proof, automations readiness, and Studio edit markers, but it still needs a real dashboard flow with login, settings, payment, dashboard data, and fewer package-card proof surfaces before the launch template surface is strong.
- Completed the 68/100 urgent launch-readiness fix pass at source and binary level: the source tree fixes the missing `js/openapi/dx-launch.yaml` Forge path mapping, emits the TSX `/launch` route/components before Forge package materialization can fail, serves `/favicon.svg` from `public/favicon.svg`, preserves stable `data-dx-icon` markers through runtime icon rendering, and adds mobile overflow plus canvas pixel-proof guards. `target/debug/dx-www.exe` was rebuilt from the current source, and a generation smoke confirmed `dx-www new --template launch` now writes `app/launch/page.tsx`, `components/launch/launch-shell.tsx`, `openapi/dx-launch.yaml`, and `public/favicon.svg`.
- Took over the stalled package lanes for Motion, Vercel AI, tRPC, InstantDB, Fumadocs, next-intl, 3D scene, Supabase, and automations/n8n at source/materialization scope. The combined guard bundle now passes 81/81 tests, and the core launch source gate passes 7/7 tests; no local server, browser run, deploy, or full build was started.

### Fixed

- Hardened generic React/App Router fallback copy extraction so dynamic slot text like `{children}`, `props.*`, and error placeholders cannot become public fallback headings. This protects the `.tsx` public story while the broader App Router renderer is still being completed.
- Moved non-launch App Router dev responses onto the shared `app_router_execution::render_app_router_runtime` path. Generic TSX pages now publish `data-dx-renderer="tsx-app-router-generic"` plus a `__DX_APP_ROUTER_EXECUTION__` JSON contract with route/search params, layout/template/boundary counts, metadata sources, node_modules status, and explicit `public_authoring: "tsx"` / `legacy_page_formats: "internal-only"` markers.
- Improved App Router route-handler parity: dev routing now resolves dynamic API route files such as `app/api/trpc/[trpc]/route.ts`, the safe route interpreter executes exported const arrow handlers, and factory/alias const exports are detected as honest `501 route-handler-boundary` responses instead of failing as missing methods.
- Fixed generic TSX App Router request context so query-string normalization no longer drops `searchParams` from dynamic route DOM markers, and server contracts now infer exported-const route-handler request signatures such as `(request: Request)` instead of reporting them as generic JSON handlers.
- Split App Router route matching into `dx-www/src/cli/app_api_routes.rs` and `dx-www/src/cli/app_page_routes.rs`, reducing the giant CLI module's ownership of dynamic API/page lookup while preserving the existing `dx dev` request path.
- Added the manager handoff document and source-only developer/AI understanding commands: `dx explain /route`, `dx doctor`, `dx check packages`, and `dx export --analyze`. They write route contracts, package maturity receipts, doctor receipts, and static export analysis under `.dx/` without starting a server or claiming runtime proof.
- Added a framework risk register to `dx doctor`. The doctor receipt now emits `dx.framework.riskRegister`, `score_ceiling`, and explicit risks for TSX runtime parity, the oversized CLI module, Forge package overclaiming, static export proof, web-performance proof, Studio manifests, public-story crowding, and source-copying/legal policy so DX-WWW cannot report complete readiness while known launch risks remain.
- Added a generic TSX App Router semantics ABI. `__DX_APP_ROUTER_EXECUTION__` now carries `tsx_semantics` from `dx-www/src/cli/app_router_semantics.rs`, including composition, imports, source-owned boundaries, hooks, event handlers, client/server boundaries, and warnings for dynamic imports, React context, and implicit client boundaries without claiming full React runtime parity.
- Fixed `dx-www dev` request lookup so query strings are stripped before route and static-asset resolution. `/?qa=1` now resolves to `/`, and cache-busted assets like `/styles/dx-landing.css?v=test` resolve to their source file instead of returning 404.
- Fixed dev-server refresh staleness by applying `no-store` cache headers to dev HTML/static responses, disabling the legacy response cache while hot reload is enabled, and wiring the Axum server to the existing watcher with an incrementing hot-reload version for app, pages, components, server/api, styles, public, and Forge route/source manifest changes.
- Verified the fix live against `examples/conversion-proof` on `http://127.0.0.1:3765/`: a single server process reflected an edit to `pages/index.html` at `/` and `/?qa=1`, reflected an edit to `styles/dx-landing.css` at `/styles/dx-landing.css?v=test`, and changed `/_dx/hot-reload/version` after both edits before the server was stopped.
- Fixed the launch dev static path for favicon parity: `/favicon.svg` now resolves through the same public-file path as `/public/favicon.svg`, including cache-busted query-string requests on the Axum path.
- Fixed the current launch entrypoint after the TSX reset: `/` from `app/page.tsx` is the primary generated dashboard route, `/launch` is an alias, and the public responder path is TSX/App Router scoped rather than legacy page-format scoped.
- Fixed the Vercel prebuilt deploy command contract to include static output readiness, file counts, byte totals, and content hash, which removed the only warning from the targeted `dx-www` rebuild.
- Fixed the next-intl runtime marker shape for `mission-locale-format` so source/materialized guards can map the format-preview copy target deterministically.
- Fixed the launch route contract so `examples/template/launch-route-contract.ts` names `/launch` and `app/launch/page.tsx` as the primary generated route instead of treating the root alias as the main launch surface.
- Fixed the inactive 3D scene and i18n package drift by carrying `lib/scene/bounds-report.ts`, `createDxSceneBoundsReport`, `createDxDashboardIntlNumberPreview`, and localized price preview markers through Forge metadata, generated-template materialization, Studio/Zed discovery, and the runtime-safe bridge.
- Fixed the automations/n8n launch package catalog wording so CLI/Zed discovery explicitly names the credential schema and workflow-node readiness boundary used by the visible dashboard workflow.
- Corrected the launch template public entrypoint back to the package-demo route: `/launch` / `app/launch/page.tsx` is the primary TSX dashboard, while `/` / `app/page.tsx` remains the root alias/home preview route. The route contract and package source guards now agree on this handoff.

### Added

- Added a DX-WWW reader and CLI view for the root `dx-check` command-center receipt. `dx_compiler::ecosystem::read_dx_check_latest_panel` and `dx check --latest-receipt --json` now read `.dx/receipts/check/check-latest.json`, validate the embedded `dx.check.zed_panel` payload, and return a compact `dx.www.check_panel` state for Studio/Web Preview/Zed panels without rerunning expensive checks.
- Added a Studio/Web Preview dx-check panel contract. The preview manifest now publishes a `check_panel` field for `dx check --latest-receipt --json`, the launch shell and runtime-safe `/launch.html` page expose stable `data-dx-check-*` markers, and the edit/materialization contracts map the health panel back to source while keeping Lighthouse, CDP, E2E, lint, format, package install, and full-build execution opt-in.
- Added the `dx.www.check_panel_view_model` render model to the DX-WWW dx-check receipt reader. Ready receipts now expose score meter, bucket rows, blockers, warnings, quick fixes, last-run metadata, and refresh/detail actions; missing or malformed receipts expose explicit empty states without fake scores.
- Added a source-only guard that proves the launch panel renders from `dx.www.check_panel_view_model` fields and that runtime/Studio markers include missing-receipt, score-state, and view-model status signals.
- Added honest Forge package maturity labels to the launch catalog: `slice`, `adapter-boundary`, `boundary`, and `full`. The launch UI now exposes package maturity per catalog row, and source guards fail if launch packages lose that classification or adapter-boundary packages are marketed as full replacements.
- Added Motion local preference persistence for the visible `/launch` dashboard workflow: the source-owned dashboard model now exposes `motionDashboardPreferenceStorageKey`, `readMotionDashboardPreference`, and `writeMotionDashboardPreference`, the React/runtime launch proofs publish `data-dx-motion-preference-storage` plus `data-dx-motion-storage-key`, and the runtime guard verifies order/reduced-motion preference write and restore behavior without a template-local `node_modules` workflow.
- Hardened the `i18n/next-intl` `/launch` dashboard workflow with a source-owned `DashboardIntlFormatPreview` / `createDxDashboardIntlFormatPreview` contract backed by real `useFormatter().dateTime` behavior. The React workflow, runtime-safe `/launch.html`, runtime bridge, Studio manifest, package catalog, receipt, docs, and source guard now carry `data-dx-intl-format-preview`, `data-dx-intl-format-source-api`, and `data-dx-intl-format-time-zone` so the formatted launch window cannot disappear behind metadata.
- Added the `DashboardIntlNumberPreview` / `createDxDashboardIntlNumberPreview` contract to the `i18n/next-intl` `/launch` dashboard workflow, backed by real `useFormatter().number` currency formatting. The React workflow, runtime-safe `/launch.html`, runtime bridge, Studio manifest, package catalog, receipt, docs, and source guard now carry `data-dx-intl-number-preview`, `data-dx-intl-number-source-api`, and `data-dx-intl-number-currency` so the localized plan price is visible in the generated launch surface.
- Added a source-owned `3d/launch-scene` renderer capability report API and visible `/launch` action. `createDxSceneCapabilityReport` inspects the browser WebGL context attributes, supported extensions, texture limits, and renderbuffer limits; the React launch scene and no-`node_modules` runtime bridge now expose `data-dx-scene-action="inspect-renderer-capabilities"`, `data-dx-scene-capability-report`, and `data-dx-scene-capability-status`; Forge metadata, Studio manifests, generated materialization, receipt paths, and the launch-scene guard all fail if the capability workflow disappears.
- Added a source-owned `3d/launch-scene` viewport/DPR report API and visible `/launch` action. `createDxSceneViewportReport` measures CSS size, drawing-buffer size, browser `devicePixelRatio`, effective pixel ratio, and package quality/performance DPR budgets; the React launch scene and no-`node_modules` runtime bridge now expose `data-dx-scene-action="measure-viewport-dpr"`, `data-dx-scene-viewport-report`, and `data-dx-scene-viewport-status`; Forge metadata, Studio manifests, generated materialization, receipt paths, and the launch-scene guard all fail if the viewport workflow disappears.
- Added a source-owned `3d/launch-scene` bounds/framing report API and visible `/launch` action. `createDxSceneBoundsReport` computes a Three `Box3` / Drei `Bounds.fit`-style bounds box, fit target, and camera distance from the source-owned procedural interaction map; the React launch scene and no-`node_modules` runtime bridge now expose `data-dx-scene-action="fit-scene-bounds"`, `data-dx-scene-bounds-report`, and `data-dx-scene-bounds-status`; Forge metadata, Studio manifests, generated materialization, receipt paths, and the launch-scene guard all fail if the bounds workflow disappears.
- Added a source-owned `3d/launch-scene` raycast hit-report API and visible `/launch` action. `createDxSceneRaycastReport` mirrors the inspected Three `Raycaster`, React Three Fiber `state.raycaster`, and Drei `meshBounds` workflow through the package-owned interaction map; the React launch scene and no-`node_modules` runtime bridge now expose `data-dx-scene-action="inspect-raycast-hit"`, `data-dx-scene-raycast-report`, and `data-dx-scene-raycast-status`; Forge metadata, Studio manifests, generated materialization, receipt paths, and the launch-scene guard all fail if the raycast workflow disappears.
- Added explicit upstream source-guard metadata to the `api/trpc` dashboard workflow receipt and tightened the tRPC Forge guard so the receipt must name the real upstream source files and asserted APIs it depends on.
- Aligned the `api/trpc` dashboard workflow provenance with its receipt guard, including fetch response handling, batch streaming, HTTP subscriptions, split links, infinite query options, and subscription options in both starter metadata and Forge materialization.
- Pointed the starter `api/trpc` dashboard helper at the concrete dashboard workflow receipt path so dashboard, Forge materialization, docs, and generated-template discovery share the same handoff artifact.
- Removed stale legacy tRPC proof-card IDs from the `api/trpc` package handoff doc and guarded the doc against those markers returning.
- Added the missing source TSX `check-health` action to `TrpcLaunchHealth`, backed by a typed `HealthCheckResult` helper in `trpc-launch-contract.ts`, and recorded the local contract exports in the tRPC dashboard receipt so the source component matches its receipt, Studio metadata, and runtime-safe `/launch` bridge.
- Replaced stale `api/trpc` CLI public API catalog names with the real Forge slice exports for route handling, clients, server callers, dashboard workflow, and local health/launchEvent contracts, and guarded the catalog against regressing to `createTRPCRouter` / `useTRPC` only.
- Added the core launch template surface registry as a generated starter file (`components/launch/template-surface-registry.ts`) and made `LaunchShell` render it through `data-dx-component="launch-template-worker-registry"` so package workers have one source-owned slot contract for dashboard surfaces, selectors, receipts, env boundaries, hot-reload targets, and no-`node_modules` rules.
- Simplified the public `/launch` App Router page so it loads metadata/providers and renders the composed `LaunchShell` instead of duplicating package workflows outside the shell.
- Aligned the Stripe dashboard-starter readiness contract with the `/launch` billing workflow: `StripePlanCheckout` now uses the same `createDxStripeDashboardCheckoutRequest({ planId, checkoutMode, contact })`, `createDxStripeDashboardMissingConfigReceipt`, plan-specific `STRIPE_PRICE_ID_STARTER` / `TEAM` / `SCALE` env boundary, and request-body shape as the generated Checkout route, and the focused Stripe source guard fails if that package API contract disappears.
- Wired `automations/n8n` into the launch operating dashboard mission summary: `LaunchAutomationBridgeStatus` now publishes `LaunchAutomationDashboardState` into the source-owned `automation-mission-summary.tsx` component, `/launch` renders `data-dx-component="launch-automation-mission-summary"` with selected connector, auth schema, credential types, workflow-node readiness, and receipt state, the no-`node_modules` runtime page/script mirror the same mission-card markers, and the automations source guards require the dashboard summary plus redacted receipt markers.
- Aligned `automations/n8n` package catalog and Studio metadata with the real `createDxN8nRunReceipt` public API name, and tightened the automations guard so stale invented receipt-helper names fail source verification.
- Renamed the `/launch` n8n connector surface from proof-card markers to product workflow markers: `launch-automation-connector-workflow`, `launch-automation-catalog-summary`, and `data-dx-automation-workflow="connector-readiness"` now replace the older connector marker names across source, runtime bridge, catalog, Studio metadata, docs, and guards.
- Tightened the `automations/n8n` launch component API so the non-dashboard surface is `connector-readiness` and local dry-run receipts use the `connector-readiness` workflow id instead of stale proof/preview naming.
- Hardened `automations/n8n` receipt intent wiring: Forge and starter-dashboard receipt helpers now preserve a normalized workflow intent, `/launch` and the no-`node_modules` runtime bridge expose receipt-intent markers, DX Studio metadata indexes them, and the Rust/Zed `dx automations run --json` bridge carries the redacted seed `workflow_intent` into run receipts.
- Wired `automations/n8n` required env into the real package API and launch dashboard: `requiredEnvForDxN8nConnector`, readiness results, run receipts, `/launch` source/runtime markers, Studio metadata, package catalog, and the Rust/Zed run receipt now carry `data-dx-automation-required-env` / `required_env` without reading secrets.
- Tightened the Better Auth `/launch` dashboard workflow so signed-in profile update, account deletion, linked-account refresh/link, active-session refresh, and revoke-other-sessions actions share the same `data-dx-auth-network-state` missing-config gate and safe local receipt behavior as the anonymous sign-in path.
- Added a visible Better Auth email sign-up action to the `/launch` account dashboard using the existing source-owned `signUpDxBetterAuthEmail` helper, with `data-dx-auth-interaction="email-sign-up"` and a safe local missing-config preview instead of fake account creation.
- Added an honest Better Auth local account-session preview to `/launch`, exposing the account management workflow with `data-dx-auth-session-source="local-preview"` while making clear that no cookie, server session, or account is created.
- Aligned the no-`node_modules` `/launch` runtime bridge with the Better Auth account dashboard contract, including `email-sign-up`, `preview-local-session`, `data-dx-auth-session-source`, and local preview session markers plus a browser-only local preview interaction.
- Professionalized the Better Auth runtime bridge by routing sign-up, session preview, route-check, sign-out, and provider-missing states through one `setBetterAuthRuntimeState` marker writer, so the dashboard card and session proof surface cannot drift apart.
- Added the source-owned Better Auth workflow receipt at `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/auth-better-auth.json`, recording the real upstream APIs, `/launch` selectors, safe local-session preview, missing-config boundaries, app-owned production responsibilities, and no-template-local-`node_modules` policy. The launch route contract and template surface registry now carry that exact receipt path, and the Better Auth source test guards the handoff.
- Wired the Better Auth workflow receipt into the generated-starter CLI path: `dx new` now materializes `.dx/forge/receipts/auth-better-auth.json`, includes it in the generated launch receipt source package and readiness file list, and exposes Better Auth aliases, exported files, dashboard usage, DX icon metadata, source mirror, required env, and exact receipt paths in launch catalog metadata.
- Completed the Better Auth CLI catalog provenance pass: the launch metadata now lists the full Better Auth exported file set, including account deletion, Next route handlers, `.env.example`, README, and curated provenance tied to the local Better Auth source mirror.
- Added Better Auth provider readiness metadata to the launch CLI catalog. GitHub/Google provider env is now discoverable separately from the base Better Auth secret/url env, with `credential_state: "missing-config"` matching the visible `/launch` missing-provider workflow.
- Extended the Better Auth launch route contract and template surface registry with GitHub/Google provider env and provider DOM markers, so Zed/DX Studio discovery can trace `/launch` missing-provider readiness back to the source-owned account workflow instead of only the CLI catalog.
- Added public launch CLI surfaces for `dx style build|watch|check`, `dx imports sync|check`, `dx check web-perf`, and `dx deploy vercel`. The new Rust-owned command paths write dx-style CSS/import/deploy/web-performance receipts and keep URL performance collection/deploy execution honest until governed runtime approval.
- Tightened `dx style check` for the public starter contract so missing required theme tokens, stale generated CSS, unused generated classes, hardcoded colors, and Tailwind leakage fail the check instead of remaining advisory-only report fields.
- Added a core `dx check` dx-style section so project checks now report required-token gaps, stale generated CSS, hardcoded colors, Tailwind leakage, and unused generated selectors without needing a separate style-only command first.
- Added web-performance receipt fields for Core Web Vitals, request count, transfer size, and a governed Rust CDP attach plan. URL mode now writes `.dx/receipts/check/web-perf/cdp-plan.json` with device profiles, CDP domains/sequence, metric sources, score model, and blocked-until conditions while keeping measured scores behind explicit runtime governance or Lighthouse JSON import.
- Hardened Lighthouse JSON import receipts for `dx check web-perf`: incomplete reports now expose `score_completeness`, missing categories, and a partial measurement status instead of claiming a full 400-point total from partial category evidence.
- Updated the generated `dx new` starter toward the public App Router `.tsx` contract with dashboard, settings, auth, and billing routes, professional component folders, `styles/theme.dx.css`, `styles/app.generated.css`, generated import-map targets, and no template-local `node_modules`.
- Hardened `dx deploy vercel` into a guarded local preflight pipeline: the command now runs dx-style build/check, import sync/check, `.dx/build` inspection, content-hashed upload planning, and manifest/receipt writing while external Vercel execution remains approval-gated.
- Added a Vercel prebuilt command contract to the deploy manifest path. `dx deploy vercel` now writes `.dx/deploy/vercel-command.json` describing the Build Output API v3 adapter from `.dx/build` into `.vercel/output/static`, the `.vercel/output/config.json` shape, required `VERCEL_TOKEN`, and the exact `vercel deploy --prebuilt` argv without running external deployment in source-only passes.
- Added local Vercel Build Output materialization for deploy preparation. Non-dry-run `dx deploy vercel` can now prepare `.vercel/output/static` from `.dx/build`, write `.vercel/output/config.json`, preserve `.vercel/project.json`, and still stop before external Vercel CLI execution unless explicitly approved.
- Added the Supabase profile workflow to the generated launch dashboard: `supabase/client` now carries aliases/source mirror/provenance/exported-file/env/boundary/receipt/docs metadata, advertises generated `lib/supabase/metadata.ts` discovery, materializes `lib/supabase/profile-workflow.ts` plus `components/launch/supabase-profile-workflow.tsx`, imports real `getDxSupabaseCurrentProfile` and `upsertDxSupabaseProfile` helpers, mounts from the shell's `account-data-dashboard`, uses `<dx-icon name="database:supabase" />`, exposes package-owned editable local profile fields from the typed `dxSupabaseProfileFields` descriptor list, a safe fixture action, missing-config upsert receipts, registers DX Studio editable surfaces and preview package-surface metadata for the account-data section plus profile workflow, records `examples/template/.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-supabase-client-dashboard-workflow.json`, materializes the generated-starter receipt at `.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-supabase-client-dashboard-workflow.json`, exposes that receipt through `data-dx-supabase-receipt-path` on the visible Supabase dashboard workflows, and maps the data dashboard's local `profiles` read-model action through `readDxSupabaseProfilesReadModel` as `data-dx-component="supabase-schema-query-workflow"` plus `data-dx-dashboard-workflow="supabase-schema-query"` instead of hiding behind package cards.
- Added a receipt-level Supabase four-pass reality audit: the dashboard workflow receipt now classifies `supabase/client` as `REAL` for upstream-backed Forge package code, generated `/launch` materialization, visible profile/schema-query interactions, and Studio/Web Preview discovery, while keeping hosted credentials, reads/writes, realtime, RLS rollout, and governed browser QA as explicit partial runtime boundaries.
- Added a Drizzle dashboard workflow for the starter: `db/drizzle-sqlite` now materializes a real `dashboard-workflow.ts` helper around the existing Drizzle analytics, join, CTE, and query helpers, exposes `readDrizzleDashboardQueryPlan` with Drizzle `.toSQL()` SQL/params previews, records professional source mirror/provenance/export/receipt metadata, documents the package plus source guard and deferred runtime boundaries in `docs/packages/db-drizzle-sqlite.md`, is consumed by `examples/dashboard` through a visible `<dx-icon name="pack:database" />` readiness panel, and now powers the generated `/launch` data section as a selectable SQLite read-model workflow with query-plan preview, a safe local runtime receipt, mission-control database card status/detail updates, and a source-owned dashboard workflow receipt copied by the runtime materializer.
- Exposed the Drizzle `/launch` runtime dependency boundary as source-selectable metadata: `LaunchDrizzleDashboardData`, the runtime-safe `/launch.html` page, the Studio edit contract, preview-manifest materializer, package catalog, route contract, receipt, docs, and Forge metadata now carry `data-dx-drizzle-runtime-dependencies="drizzle-orm,better-sqlite3"` instead of leaving the app-owned SQLite runtime requirement as prose only.
- Hardened the launch template surface registry for Drizzle so the shared `data-backend` slot contract now advertises `data-dx-drizzle-runtime-dependencies` and requires a runtime dependency boundary in addition to typed schema/readiness/receipt ownership.
- Extended the Drizzle dashboard query-plan API with a real `overview` plan built from Drizzle aggregate/select/leftJoin builders and `.toSQL()`, so the starter dashboard overview preview is package-backed instead of a UI-only summary.
- Added a Drizzle query-plan selector API: `db/drizzle-sqlite` now exports `getDrizzleDashboardQueryPlan` and `readDrizzleDashboardQueryPlanById`, the starter dashboard marks SQL previews with the by-id package export, and package catalog/Studio/docs metadata expose the selector for Zed Web Preview handoff.
- Professionalized `content/fumadocs-next` for the starter dashboard and generated `/launch`: the Forge slice now exports `lib/fumadocs/dashboard-workflow.ts` plus `components/dashboard/fumadocs-docs-workflow.tsx`, package catalog metadata now carries structured `/launch` `dashboardUsage`, `dxIcon: "pack:fumadocs"`, and a source-owned dashboard workflow receipt, `dx new` now materializes that receipt into `.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-content-fumadocs-dashboard-workflow.json`, `examples/dashboard` consumes it through a visible Documentation System workflow, and `/launch` now mounts a `docs-help-changelog` workflow with page-tree selection, mission-control docs summary updates, route readiness, OpenAPI env readiness, LLM export readiness, changelog notes, DX icon markers, `docs-workflow` runtime styling, and a safe local route receipt instead of a Fumadocs proof card. The live runtime materializer and conversion-proof preview manifest now expose `launch-fumadocs-docs-workflow` as the editable Documentation System surface instead of the stale `fumadocs-docs-navigation-proof` selector, and the page-tree buttons/receipt output now carry `aria-pressed`, `role="status"`, and `aria-live="polite"` semantics for the governed browser pass.
- Hardened lane 14 official naming for the Documentation System: package catalog metadata, starter dashboard copy, `/launch` docs/help/changelog copy, runtime-safe launch copy, `docs/packages/content-fumadocs-next.md`, and the dashboard receipt now use `Documentation System` as the front-facing package name while recording `fumadocs` only as `upstreamPackage`, `sourceMirror`, or `basedOn` provenance. Inspected upstream source remained `G:\WWW\inspirations\fumadocs` with public APIs `defineDocs`, `loader`, `getBreadcrumbItems`, `llms(source)`, `createOpenAPI`, `createFromSource`, `staticGET`, and `useDocsSearch`; focused verification was red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\fumadocs-dashboard-workflow.test.ts`. Skipped by instruction: builds, broad suites, servers, browser automation, installs, deploys, and `just run`. Next action: keep any future CLI receipt/dx-check label work on the official Documentation System name while preserving upstream provenance fields.
- Hardened the Fumadocs template surface registry proof: `examples/template/template-surface-registry.ts` now participates in the `content/fumadocs-next` receipt and package guard, locking the `content-docs` surface to `launch-fumadocs-docs-workflow`, the Fumadocs dashboard receipt path, and the no-static-docs-card worker contract.
- Added a receipt-level Fumadocs four-pass reality audit: the dashboard workflow receipt now classifies `content/fumadocs-next` as `REAL` for source-owned Forge/package/dashboard/Studio discovery, records upstream Fumadocs evidence files and public APIs, names the dashboard consumers and guards, and keeps live renderer/search/OpenAPI execution as an explicit partial runtime boundary.
- Hardened the Fumadocs DX Studio edit contract: `docs-help-changelog-workflow` now records package-specific interaction selectors, state markers, and the dashboard workflow receipt path for the route preview action, page selector, rendered markdown, changelog, missing-config, receipt, and OpenAPI proxy markers, with the Fumadocs guard covering that source map.
- Hardened the generated Fumadocs preview-manifest path: `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` now writes the package-specific page selector, rendered markdown, changelog, missing-config, local receipt, and OpenAPI proxy markers into `public/preview-manifest.json` for `launch-runtime-docs`, and the Fumadocs dashboard receipt records the generated manifest contract.
- Hardened the Rust-owned Fumadocs Zed manifest handoff: `dx-www/src/cli/studio_manifest.rs` and `examples/template/launch-route-contract.ts` now expose the same route-preview, page selector, rendered markdown, changelog, missing-config, local receipt, and OpenAPI proxy markers as the source and generated preview manifests.
- Hardened the Fumadocs package catalog handoff so launch package metadata, generated Forge metadata, package docs, receipt, and guard coverage now carry the same detailed docs-help markers as `/launch`, including page options, rendered markdown, changelog, selected-page, TOC, missing-config, receipt, OpenAPI code-usage, and OpenAPI proxy selectors.
- Hardened the Fumadocs exported-file handoff so the launch package catalog, package docs, dashboard workflow receipt, and source guard now list the generated layout, MDX, API page, docs route, LLM/search/OpenAPI route, content, OpenAPI schema, and package README files that the Forge slice materializes.
- Hardened the Fumadocs front-facing package API handoff so `launchPackagePresentation` now advertises the MDX/source/UI/page/OpenAPI/search/LLM APIs and App Router files behind the visible docs workflow instead of reducing the package to helper-only proof.
- Hardened the Fumadocs receipt-level upstream API handoff so the dashboard workflow receipt records the full MDX config, source plugin, page-tree, TOC, UI layout/page, LLM processed Markdown, OpenAPI proxy/page/code-usage, and search API set imported by the Forge slice.
- Hardened Documentation System receipt integrity so package catalog metadata, generated Forge metadata, package docs, the dashboard workflow receipt, and the focused guard now name the selected docs/help/LLM/OpenAPI/search surfaces and record SHA-256 hashes for the source-owned Forge/catalog/dashboard/docs files. Verification passed with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\fumadocs-dashboard-workflow.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and `just run` were skipped. Next action: wire those `source_hashes` into shared dx-check/Zed stale-receipt status rows.
- Hardened Documentation System dx-style compatibility so the visible `/launch` docs/help workflow and starter dashboard workflow now carry `data-dx-style-surface="documentation-system"`, and the catalog, generated Forge metadata, package docs, receipt, and guard expose `dx.forge.package.dx_style_compatibility` with token source, generated CSS target, surface/source ownership, and no-inline-style/no-hardcoded-color boundaries. Verification passed with red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\fumadocs-dashboard-workflow.test.ts`; full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, and `just run` were skipped. Next action: surface this compatibility receipt in shared dx-check/Zed package-status rows.
- Added a professional `ai/vercel-ai` dashboard workflow proof in `examples/dashboard`: the starter now imports `AiLaunchAssistant`, exposes provider readiness, required env, upstream AI SDK public API markers, prompt input, DX icon tags, and a safe missing-config receipt without creating `node_modules` or faking model success. The `/launch` App Router shell now replaces the old AI route proof card with `launch-ai-assistant-dashboard-workflow`, using `LaunchAiChatStatus` as a real assistant workflow with provider selection, prompt editing, `/api/ai/chat` route-contract preview, missing-config receipt state, and DX icon markers. The materialized static `/launch` runtime now uses the same assistant workflow marker and safe stream-contract action marker instead of the older `vercel-ai-live-demo` proof-card identity, and generated `/api/ai/chat` stubs now return an explicit `503` missing-config receipt with `credentialsConfigured: false` until `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` is app-owned and configured; the runtime UI maps that receipt to a visible missing-config state with `data-dx-ai-request-id` traceability instead of generic error or fake model success. The Forge package now also exports `lib/ai/dashboard-readiness.ts` and `components/ai/ai-launch-assistant.tsx` so `dx add ai/vercel-ai --write` can materialize the same workflow, with generated metadata, DX CLI discovery, Studio manifest, launch route contract, and the concrete `2026-05-22-ai-vercel-ai-launch-assistant.json` receipt artifact pointing to `docs/packages/ai-vercel-ai.md`, the dashboard workflow, both provider env gates, the package workflow receipt, the materialized receipt path, and the launch runtime proof.
- Hardened the Vercel AI assistant preview action with prompt-state and action-state markers, local `invalid-prompt` handling for empty prompts, and source guards that require the prompt/action/request-id markers across the runtime, receipt, route contract, Studio manifest, and package docs.

No Vercel deployment was run for this follow-up.
- Added the public DX-WWW framework strategy for launch: lead with a React-familiar `.tsx` `app/` surface, Rust-powered runtime, Forge packages, no hidden dependency surface, `dx-check`, receipts, and fast static deploys; keep `.html`/`.tsx` as internal or generated low-level files until the compiler/runtime story is mature; and simplify the public CLI story to 5-7 obvious commands while leaving evidence commands in expert/Zed handoff surfaces.
- Corrected the public DX-WWW icon/style story: the launch landing uses `<dx-icon name="pack:name" />` and `dx-icons` metadata instead of external icon branding, the Vercel export bridge preserves DX icon metadata with a static fallback, and README/framework docs now lead with React-familiar `.tsx`, dx-style generated CSS, Forge, Check, static export, and no template-local `node_modules`; binary style output is deprecated for now and premature zero-hydration claims stay out of the launch pitch.

The launch proof is `examples/template/icon-status.tsx`, and Lucide remains only an upstream ecosystem dependency where another package legitimately owns it, such as Fumadocs source plugins.

- Added BLAKE3 source-path metadata to runtime evidence import plans so DX CLI/Zed can detect stale or replaced external proof files before runtime evidence is finalized.
- Added `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-completeness` so import-plan receipts for supplied build-log, route-response, and preview-proof artifacts can be validated against the launch verification lane without running builds, previews, servers, copies, or installs.
- Tightened `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-completeness` to require an import-plan receipt, validate declared `.dx/forge/runtime` target paths, and report source-path/hash/stale-source coverage before final runtime evidence can be accepted.
- Added `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-finalize` so complete imported runtime evidence can produce `.dx/forge/runtime/final-launch-evidence-receipt.json` and update the generated runtime evidence contract only after completeness and current source-hash checks pass.

- Added `dx forge launch-runtime-evidence-review` so finalized launch evidence can be reviewed against the finalization receipt, current source artifact hashes, and finalized runtime evidence JSON without running builds, previews, servers, installs, or copying proof files.
