- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-index` so operators can inspect the full launch evidence sequence, expected files, rerun commands, stale-step hints, and release-packet status without reading runtime artifact contents or running builds, previews, servers, installs, or runtime commands.
- Surfaced the launch evidence operator index path and command in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle, discovery metadata, verification lane, Zed handoff metadata, and the launch evidence packet next-command chain.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-status-timeline` so DX CLI/Zed can summarize the latest completed launch-evidence step, next blocked step, release readiness, and file-timestamp freshness without reading runtime artifact contents.
- Surfaced the launch evidence status timeline in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, verification lane, Zed handoff metadata, and the release packet next-command chain.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-handoff-digest` so operators can write a Zed-openable Markdown handoff from the status timeline, operator index, release packet, and final runtime review without reading runtime artifact contents.
- Surfaced the handoff digest in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, verification lane, and Zed handoff metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-release-checklist` so operators can turn the handoff digest, status timeline, operator index, and release packet into a final no-execution launch signoff checklist.

- Surfaced the release checklist in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, verification lane, and Zed handoff metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-share-manifest` so DX CLI/Zed can export release checklist, handoff digest, status timeline, operator index, and packet paths from one no-execution handoff JSON.
- Surfaced the share manifest in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and release checklist next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-index` so DX CLI/Zed can record share manifest, release checklist, digest, and packet files for long-term no-execution launch archive handoff.
- Surfaced the archive index in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and share manifest next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-receipt` so DX CLI/Zed can write a final no-execution archive receipt for the archive index and operator handoff target.
- Surfaced the archive receipt in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and archive index next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-archive-ledger` so DX CLI/Zed can collect the archive receipt, archive index, share manifest, release checklist, handoff digest, and packet into one durable no-execution release ledger.
- Surfaced the archive ledger in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and archive receipt next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-retention-policy` so DX CLI/Zed can record which release proof files should be kept, refreshed, or regenerated after archive-ledger handoff.
- Surfaced the retention policy in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and archive ledger next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-retention-review` so DX CLI/Zed can validate the retention policy against the archive ledger and release proof handoff chain without reading runtime artifact contents.
- Surfaced the retention review in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and retention policy next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-release-seal` so DX CLI/Zed can record the retention review, retention policy, archive ledger, and release packet as the final no-execution launch handoff seal.
- Surfaced the release seal in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and retention review next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-summary` so DX CLI/Zed can condense the release seal, retention review, release packet, and final runtime review into one terminal-friendly no-execution launch handoff report.
- Surfaced the operator summary in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and release seal next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-completion-ledger` so DX CLI/Zed can record the operator summary, release seal, retention review, and final runtime review as the final no-execution launch evidence completion map.
- Surfaced the completion ledger in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and operator summary next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-closure-memo` so DX CLI/Zed can write a human-readable Markdown release closeout from the completion ledger, operator summary, release seal, and final runtime review handoff.
- Surfaced the closure memo in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and completion ledger next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-final-brief` so DX CLI/Zed can write the final no-execution launch closeout pointer from the closure memo, completion ledger, operator summary, and release seal.
- Surfaced the final brief in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and closure memo next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-operator-runbook` so DX CLI/Zed can write a compact restartable next-worker checklist from the final brief and closeout artifacts.
- Surfaced the operator runbook in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and final brief next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-handoff-capsule` so DX CLI/Zed can write one restart artifact that points at the operator runbook, final brief, closure memo, and completion ledger.
- Surfaced the handoff capsule in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and operator runbook next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-resumption-index` so DX CLI/Zed can write an ordered no-execution restart-lane map from the handoff capsule for source-only, runtime-approved, and release-closeout follow-up work.

- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-continuation-packet` so DX CLI/Zed can write one restart packet over the recovery brief, resumption index, handoff capsule, and operator runbook.

- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-ledger` so DX CLI/Zed can write a durable no-execution restart ledger over the operator resume card, continuation packet, recovery brief, and resumption index.

- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-checklist` so DX CLI/Zed can write source-only, runtime-approved, and release-closeout next actions from the restart ledger.

- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-brief` so DX CLI/Zed can write a compact Zed-openable Markdown handoff from the restart checklist.
- Surfaced the restart brief in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart checklist next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-manifest` so DX CLI/Zed can write one indexable no-execution JSON handoff over the restart brief, restart checklist, restart ledger, and operator resume card.
- Surfaced the restart manifest in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart brief next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-receipt` so DX CLI/Zed can write the latest resumable handoff receipt from the restart manifest.
- Surfaced the restart receipt in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart manifest next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-summary` so DX CLI/Zed can write a terminal-friendly no-execution restart handoff over the restart receipt, manifest, brief, and checklist.
- Surfaced the restart summary in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart receipt next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-snapshot` so DX CLI/Zed can write one latest-openable no-execution restart file over the restart summary, receipt, manifest, and brief.
- Surfaced the restart snapshot in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart summary next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-dispatch` so DX CLI/Zed can write a one-command no-execution next-worker dispatch card over the restart snapshot, summary, receipt, and manifest.
- Surfaced the restart dispatch in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart snapshot next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-closeout` so DX CLI/Zed can write a final no-execution Markdown closeout from the restart dispatch with Friday and essencefromexistence operator actions.
- Surfaced the restart closeout in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart dispatch next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-restart-signoff` so DX CLI/Zed can write a reviewable no-execution acceptance receipt from the restart closeout with Friday and essencefromexistence acceptance metadata.
- Surfaced the restart signoff in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart closeout next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-acceptance-index` so DX CLI/Zed can write a final no-execution Markdown handoff index over restart signoff, closeout, dispatch, and snapshot artifacts.
- Surfaced the acceptance index in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and restart signoff next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-acceptance-digest` so DX CLI/Zed can write a terminal-first no-execution JSON status line from the acceptance index for Friday's final handoff.
- Surfaced the acceptance digest in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and acceptance index next-command metadata.
- Added `dx forge launch-evidence-friday-baton` so DX CLI/Zed can write a final no-execution Markdown orchestrator handoff over the acceptance digest, acceptance index, restart signoff, and launch verification lane.
- Surfaced the Friday baton in generated `/launch` route metadata, readiness bundle metadata, discovery metadata, Zed handoff metadata, and acceptance digest next-command metadata.
- Added generated-template file materialization for the `/launch` route in `dx new`, including the route page, launch route contract, launch shell, package catalog, counter, markdown preview, tRPC health placeholder, and runtime catalog loader.
- Tightened the generated launch component manifest so the auth session, AI chat, Zod validation, realtime, and WebAssembly companion files stay visible to DX CLI/Zed after `dx new`.
- Added targeted `dx new` coverage that verifies every launch-template manifest file is materialized and the generated package catalog carries the full launch package set.
- Added a stable `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-route.json` receipt for the generated `/launch` route, linked from template metadata and backed by the source-owned Forge receipt/doc flow before runtime proof; the route contract, template metadata, and receipt now expose package/materialized-file counts, source check status, and an explicit permission-gated runtime verification field without a default runnable build command.
- Added `dx forge template-readiness` as a Forge-facing alias for the generated `/launch` readiness verifier so operators can inspect template readiness from either the template catalog lane or the Forge launch lane without starting servers or running full builds.
- Added a non-runnable runtime verification request contract for `/launch` so DX CLI and Zed can see the required approval status, automation default, and expected runtime evidence before any governed build/preview pass.
- Added typed materialized-file and readiness-summary helpers to `examples/template/launch-route-contract.ts` so DX CLI/Zed can compare the source route contract with the generated `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-route.json` receipt without running a server.
- Added `dx new` materialization for the `/launch` route shell, including `components/launch/launch-shell.tsx`, route contract metadata, package catalog, local state preview, typed API health placeholder, and content preview without package installs or `node_modules`.
- Added the generated starter UI Forge doc at `.dx/forge/docs/dx-www-starter-ui.md` so `dx new` starters have matching source-manifest and documentation artifacts.
- Added `examples/template/react-markdown-preview.tsx` as a real content-rendering wedge for launch notes with `skipHtml` enabled.
- Added source-level launch-shell verification coverage in `benchmarks/launch-template-shell.test.ts` so the composed template surface stays tied to the package catalog and CLI metadata without starting a local server.
- Added source-level launch package slice verification in `benchmarks/launch-package-slices.test.ts` so the launch package set stays tied to CLI metadata, Forge registry registration, scorecard/trust-policy coverage, and real upstream public API markers without compiling the workspace.
- Tightened the react-markdown launch slice so its safe default `allowedElements` list does not conflict with app-owned `disallowedElements` overrides.
- Raised the DX-WWW crate recursion limit to keep the expanded template discovery JSON compiling as the launch-template metadata set grows.

### Notes

- No local server or full build was started by the orchestration update.
- Better Auth verification stayed lightweight: targeted source reading, source-owned session-status template proof, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-shell.test.ts`, `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler better_auth -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p dx-www dx_add_better_auth_alias_writes_source_owned_auth_package -- --nocapture`, diff/conflict checks, and no local servers or full builds.
- Motion verification stayed lightweight: source/API/export inspection, focused red/green registry and receipt tests, scorecard/trust-policy checks, CLI package/template discovery tests, and no local servers or full builds.
- Vercel AI verification stayed lightweight: source/API inspection, source-owned AI chat template proof, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-shell.test.ts`, targeted CLI/materialization Cargo checks, diff/conflict checks, and no local servers or full builds.
- Vercel AI structured-output verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection against the local Vercel AI SDK mirror, a focused red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-structured-output-slice.test.ts`, and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-package-slices.test.ts` passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- Vercel AI embeddings verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection against the local Vercel AI SDK mirror, a focused red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-embeddings-slice.test.ts`, and launch package/template guards passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- Vercel AI provider-freedom verification stayed lightweight: source/API/docs inspection against the local Vercel AI SDK mirror and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\vercel-ai-provider-freedom-slice.test.ts` passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- Supabase verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, focused red tests, implementation, and `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler supabase -- --nocapture` passing without starting servers or full builds.
- Drizzle verification stayed lightweight: source/package/API inspection, focused red registry tests, focused migrator, view, relational-query, join, set-operation, cte-query, transaction, prepared-query, conflict-write, mutation, and analytics source guards, implementation, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-migrations-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-views-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-relational-query-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-joins-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-set-operations-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-cte-query-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-transactions-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-prepared-queries-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-conflict-writes-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-mutations-slice.test.ts`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-sqlite-analytics-slice.test.ts`, scoped rustfmt, and no installs, local servers, or full builds.
- shadcn input verification stayed lightweight: upstream source/API inspection, focused registry/discovery tests, `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler ui_input_alias_materializes_real_upstream_input_component`, shared CLI package/template discovery checks, and no local servers or full builds.
- shadcn textarea verification stayed lightweight: upstream source/API inspection, focused registry/discovery tests, `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler ui_textarea_alias_materializes_real_upstream_textarea_component`, shared CLI package/template discovery checks, and no local servers or full builds.
- wasm-bindgen verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, a focused red registry test, implementation, and `cargo test --manifest-path .\Cargo.toml -p dx-www-compiler --lib wasm_bindgen_materializes_real_launch_loader_slice` passing without servers or full builds.
- tRPC verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, focused red registry tests, implementation, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler trpc_next_alias_materializes_typed_app_router_slice`, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-shell.test.ts`, diff/conflict checks, and edition-aware `rustfmt --check` on touched Rust files.
- TanStack Query verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, a focused registry test, source-owned cache-status template proof, `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-template-shell.test.ts`, targeted CLI/materialization Cargo guards, and no servers or full builds.
- InstantDB verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, a focused red registry test, implementation, launch-template realtime/presence proof wiring, a minimal raw-string delimiter repair in the in-progress wasm-bindgen slice, and `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler instantdb_react_alias_materializes_realtime_launch_slice` passing without servers or full builds.
- InstantDB room-topics verification stayed lightweight: source/API inspection against the local InstantDB mirror and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\instantdb-room-topics-slice.test.ts` plus launch package/template source guards passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- InstantDB storage verification stayed lightweight: source/API inspection against the local InstantDB core mirror and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\instantdb-storage-slice.test.ts` plus launch package/template source guards passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- InstantDB streams verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection against the local InstantDB mirror and `dx run --test .\benchmarks\instantdb-streams-slice.test.ts` plus launch-template source guard coverage passed without installs, servers, or full builds.
- next-intl verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, focused red/green launch-shell guard, source-owned starter-message coverage, targeted compiler and CLI checks, and no local servers or full builds.
- Zod verification stayed lightweight: source/API/export inspection, focused red/green launch-shell guard coverage, implementation, source-owned validation/JSON Schema proof wiring, and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler` checks for the Zod package, scorecard, trust policy, and local registry manifest path passing without servers or full builds.
- React Hook Form verification stayed lightweight: source/API/export inspection, a focused existing registry contract, source-owned templates, `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check` on touched Rust files, `git diff --check`, conflict-marker search, and targeted cargo checks for the package, scorecard, trust policy, and local registry manifest; no local servers or full workspace builds were started.
- Stripe.js verification stayed lightweight: source/API/export inspection, a focused existing registry contract, source-owned templates, targeted cargo verification for the payment slice, package-discovery metadata, template metadata, and no local servers or full workspace builds were started.
- Zustand verification stayed lightweight: source/API/export inspection, focused registry and discovery tests, implementation, `cargo test -p dx-www-compiler zustand_alias_materializes_real_launch_state_slice`, `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_packages_json_reports_launch_discovery_metadata`, and `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli www_templates_json_reports_launch_template_metadata`; no local servers or full builds were started.
- Fumadocs verification stayed lightweight: source/API/example inspection, real Forge source templates, route-contract metadata, `cargo test -q -p dx-www-compiler fumadocs_alias_materializes_launch_docs_slice`, `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli forge_packages_json_reports_launch_discovery_metadata`, rustfmt/diff/conflict checks, and no local servers or full workspace builds.
- Launch catalog verification stayed lightweight: a focused template-catalog assertion was added around `launch_package_catalog`, implementation followed, and targeted `cargo test -p dx-www forge_packages_json_reports_launch_discovery_metadata` plus `cargo test -p dx-www www_templates_json_reports_launch_template_metadata` checks passed; no local servers or full builds were started.

### Fixed

- Fixed the wasm-bindgen Forge loader cache so failed module initialization removes the pending cache entry before future retries.
- Fixed the Supabase Forge slice imports so browser clients import `readSupabasePublicConfig` and server clients import `defaultSupabaseEnv` plus `requiredEnv`.

## [1.0.2] - 2026-01-13

### Changed

- Replaced bincode with DX Binary Codec-Removed unmaintained `bincode` dependency (RUSTSEC-2025-0141)
- Implemented custom binary codec in `dx-www-binary/src/codec.rs`
- Zero-copy length-prefixed encoding for strings and arrays
- All 79 binary crate tests pass with new codec
- Eliminates cargo audit warning for unmaintained dependency

### Security

- Removed `bincode 2.0.1` which was flagged as unmaintained
- Custom codec provides full control over binary format

## [1.0.1] - 2026-01-13

### Fixed

- Stream Reader Header Validation
- Implemented proper magic bytes verification (DXB1 and DX formats)
- Added version validation (accepts v1 and v2)
- Improved error codes for invalid headers
- Stream Reader Patch Handling
- Implemented delta patch chunk processing
- Added PatchHeader parsing (base_hash, target_hash, algorithm)
- Support for Block XOR (algorithm 1) and VCDIFF (algorithm 2) formats
- Proper error handling for invalid patch headers
- Token Revocation Check
- Implemented token revocation verification in auth_middleware
- Added credential store integration for refresh token validation
- Proper AUTH_1004 error response for revoked tokens
- Improved Error Messages
- Enhanced panic messages in sched crate with context about browser requirements
- Improved thread spawn error messages in reactor crate
- Better documentation for WASM-specific code paths

### Changed

- Replaced generic `expect()` messages with descriptive error context
- Added `# Panics` documentation sections for functions that can panic
- Improved code comments explaining WASM-specific requirements

## [1.0.0] - 2026-01-13

### Added

- Production Readiness
- JSX Splitter: Proper template generation from JSX AST (no more dummy templates)
- JSX Splitter: Conditional rendering detection (&&, ternary expressions)
- JSX Splitter: List rendering detection (.map() calls with key tracking)
- Morph: ClassToggle binding type implementation
- Morph: Style binding type implementation
- Binary: CRC32 checksum validation in HTIP format
- Delta: Size comparison to return full target when patch is larger
- Delta: Patch chunk handling in WASM client
- Storage: IndexedDB and Cache API size calculation
- Storage: Entry counting across storage backends
- Property-Based Tests
- Parser round-trip consistency (Property 1)
- Parser error location accuracy (Property 2)
- Parser banned keyword detection (Property 3)
- Delta patch round-trip (Property 4)
- Delta patch size bound (Property 5)
- Morph dirty bit processing (Property 6)
- Morph binding type coverage (Property 7)
- Binary validation: magic bytes, version, signature, checksum (Properties 8-11)
- Splitter no dummy templates (Property 12)
- Splitter conditional binding generation (Property 13)
- Splitter iteration binding generation (Property 14)
- Server Integration Tests
- Health check endpoint verification
- Binary streaming verification
- SSR bot detection verification
- Delta patch serving verification
- Error handling (404) verification
- Documentation
- Comprehensive rustdoc for core, binary, and server crates
- Architecture diagrams in docs/architecture.md
- Complete example applications (todo-app, dashboard, blog)
- Improved error messages with file paths, line/column numbers

### Changed

- Replaced all `.expect()` calls in production code with proper error handling
- Replaced all `.unwrap()` calls in production code with fallible patterns
- Pre-compiled regex patterns using `once_cell::sync::Lazy` for better performance
- Response builders now use `unwrap_or_else` with fallback responses

### Security

- All unsafe code blocks now have documented safety invariants
- cargo audit passes with no known vulnerabilities
- 19 crates with `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- Eliminated panic-prone code paths in production handlers

## [0.1.0] - 2026-01-08

### Added

- Core Framework-Audited all unsafe code with documented safety invariants
- Added #![forbid(unsafe_code)] to safe crates
- Updated docs/SECURITY.md with current threat model

### Changed

- Improved parser error messages with suggestions for common mistakes
- Enhanced compilation error messages with context and fix suggestions
- Runtime errors now include component names and stack traces

### Deprecated

### Removed

### Fixed

### Security

- All unsafe code blocks now have documented safety invariants
- cargo audit passes with no known vulnerabilities

### CI/Lint

- All clippy warnings resolved with `-D warnings` flag
- Added lint allowances for intentional patterns:-`collapsible_if` in form, rtl, cache, core, error crates
- `should_implement_trait` in core crate
- `regex_creation_in_loops` in core crate
- `len_without_is_empty` in sched crate
- `vec_init_then_push` in server crate
- `doc_nested_refdefs` in server crate
- Comprehensive lint allowances in reactor crate for low-level I/O operations
- Fixed `manual_div_ceil` warnings in binary/delta.rs using `div_ceil()` method
- All workspace tests pass (excluding WASM client crates due to panic_impl conflicts)

## [0.1.0] - 2026-01-08

### Added

- Core Framework
- TSX/JSX parser using OXC (Oxidation Compiler) with full AST support
- Tree shaking for dead code elimination with usage graph analysis
- HTIP (Hyper Text Interchange Protocol) binary format for template streaming
- Delta patching for efficient incremental updates using BLAKE3 hashing
- Runtime
- Cross-platform I/O reactor with io_uring (Linux 5.1+), epoll (Linux), kqueue (macOS/BSD), and IOCP (Windows) backends
- Sub-20KB WASM client runtime with pure FFI (no wasm-bindgen)
- JavaScript host function implementations for DOM manipulation and event handling
- Dirty bit tracking for incremental DOM updates
- Server
- SSR (Server-Side Rendering) with binary streaming
- Delta patching for efficient client updates
- WebSocket support for real-time communication
- Authentication middleware with Ed25519 tokens
- Developer Experience
- Compile-time accessibility auditing (a11y) with AST analysis
- Binary validation engine for forms with zero runtime overhead
- RTL detection and CSS flipping for internationalization
- Print stylesheet generator
- Infrastructure
- Cargo workspace with 29 member crates
- CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions (fmt, clippy, test, build)
- Cross-platform builds (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Code coverage reporting with cargo-llvm-cov
- Benchmark suite with Criterion
- Testing
- Property-based testing with proptest
- Parser round-trip consistency tests
- Delta patch round-trip and corruption detection tests
- Reactor I/O callback invocation tests
- A11y rule detection tests

### Changed

- N/A (initial release)

### Deprecated

- N/A (initial release)

### Removed

- N/A (initial release)

### Fixed

- N/A (initial release)

### Security

- Eliminated unsafe `.unwrap()` calls in non-test code paths
- Added `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` lint configuration for safe crates
- Documented all uses of unsafe code with safety invariants
- Integrated cargo-audit in CI for vulnerability detection

## Upgrade Guide

### Upgrading from 0.1.0 to 1.0.0

- No breaking API changes
- All `.expect()` and `.unwrap()` calls in production code replaced with proper error handling
- Regex patterns now pre-compiled for better performance
- Version number updated to signal production readiness

### Upgrading to 0.1.0

This is the initial release. No upgrade steps required.

[2026-05-22] - Database ORM Check-Panel Mirror Attribution

CHANGED:
- Added `mirror_problem_count` to the Database ORM DX Studio/check-panel receipt-hash helper row in `core/src/ecosystem/dx_check_receipt.rs`, preserving official Database ORM naming while keeping `drizzle-orm` `0.45.3` and `G:/WWW/inspirations/drizzle-orm` as provenance.
- Updated the Database ORM source guard to require Rust row support for helper path arrays plus `mirror_problem_count`.
- Updated Database ORM docs and framework notes to state that `current_files`, `stale_files`, `missing_files`, `stale_mirror_files`, `missing_mirror_files`, and `mirror_problem_count` are Studio-visible source-only helper evidence.

NOTES:
- Upstream Drizzle files inspected: `drizzle-orm/package.json`, `drizzle-orm/src/sqlite-core/db.ts`, and `drizzle-orm/src/better-sqlite3/driver.ts`; public APIs used remain `withReplicas`, `selectDistinct`, `$count`, and `drizzle`.

- Focused Database ORM verification also covered `dx run --test .\benchmarks\drizzle-dx-check-package-lane-panel.test.ts .\benchmarks\drizzle-package-status-read-model.test.ts .\benchmarks\drizzle-receipt-hash-refresh.test.ts`, `node examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --write`, `node tools/launch/run-template-receipt-helper.js examples/template/database-orm-receipt-hashes.ts --check --json`, benchmark syntax checks, refreshed JSON parse checks, scoped `git diff --check`, and conflict/trailing-whitespace scans.
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check .\core\src\ecosystem\dx_check_receipt.rs` remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in the shared receipt reader around the State Management test fixture.
- Heavy checks skipped by policy: full builds, broad suites, local servers, browser automation, package installs, deploys, `just run`, Cargo compilation, and live SQLite runtime proof.
- Exact next action: add a tiny generated JSON fixture for a Database ORM mirror-only drift row so Studio tests can assert nonzero `mirror_problem_count` without mutating selected Drizzle source hashes.
